raw_text: BROTT, ARTHUR MOORE.—Clerk to attorney-general, British Guiana, 1871; clerk, colonial registrar's office, July, 1874; 3rd clerk, immigration department, Oct., 1876; stipendiary magistrate's clerk, Mar., 1879; compiler of Summary Convictions Ordinances of the Colony, 1877 to 1883; chief constable, police magistrate and sheriff of Demerara; has several times acted as special and stipendiary J.P.; ag. ass't pol. mag., Georgetown, 1890, to Jan., 1891, and May, 1893, to Feb., 1894; ag. stip. mag., Essequibo Islands, April, 1891, to Aug., 1892.
BROTT, SIR JOSEPH PALMER, K.C.M.G.—Secretary for mines, New South Wales, 1883, to Oct., 1885, when appointed Secretary for Lands; resigned Dec., 1885; speaker of the Legislative Assembly, 1892.

BROTT, T. KINGSMILL.—Clerk of petty sessions and crown lands agent, Gunnedah, Liverpool Plains, N.S.W., 1857; police magistrate, 1874; Maitland, 1882; stipendiary magistrate, Central Police Court, Sydney, 1884.

BERDEEN, 7TH EARL OF, creat. 1682; Visct. of Martine, Baron Haddo, Methilie, Tarves, Kellie, 1682; Bart., 1642 (Scot.); Visct. of Gordon of Aberdeen (U.K.), under which title sit in the House of Lords; P.C. (1886); M.O. (1892); John Campbell Hamilton-Bruce, 3rd son of the 5th Earl. Born 1847; died, 1897. Hon. Isabel Maria, youngest daughter of Sir Barton Tweedmouth; succeeded his brother, 1870; lord lieut. of Aberdeenshire, 1880; high commissar to General Assembly, Church of Scotland, 1891-6; lord lieut. of Ireland, Feb. to Aug., 1886; gov. gen. of Canada, 1893.

ACKROYD, EDWARD JAMES.—Clerk, registry supreme court, Mauritius, Sept., 1853; clerk to first puisne judge, April, 1859; chief clerk in registry supreme court, Jan., 1863; chief clerkmaster's office, Mar., 1864; in July, 1871, obtained from Council of Legal Education an exhibition in common law, and in Nov., 1871, a certificate of honour of the first class; called to the bar, Middle Temple, June, 1872; district and stipendiary magistrate, Riviere au Renpart, Aug., 1873; for many years member of the Council of Education, Mauritius; registrar of the supreme court of Hong Kong, Dec., 1881; appointed on a commission to amend and revise the laws and ordinances of Hong Kong, Aug., 1882; acting puisne judge, Mar. to Dec., 1883; and acting attorney general, 1886-8, and 1890; acting puisne judge, April, 1891; acting chief justice, June, 1891; puisne judge, 1892; retired, 1895.

ACKROYD, JOHN HENRY.—Third clerk, registry supreme court, April, 1854; clerk to second puisne judge, April, 1855; admitted solicitor of supreme court, Mauritius, April, 1859; police and stipendiary magistrate for the lesser dependencies of Mauritius, 1876; special commissioner to investigate the treatment and state of the liberated Africans at Seychelles, May, 1881; district judge, Seychelles, 1882; district magistrate, Grand Port. Sept., 1884; acting assistant colonial secretary, 1885; district magistrate, Plaines Wilhelms, 1887; police and additional dist. mag., Aug., 1895.
ADAMS, GEORGE P.—Recorder of titles, Tasmania, July, 1876; registrar, supreme court, 1885.
ADAMS, M.—Surveyor-general, province of New Brunswick, Canada.
ADAMS, P. F.—Surveyor-general for the colony of New South Wales, 17 March, 1868.
ADAMS, ROBERT PATTEN—Solicitor-general, Tasmania, 20 Dec., 1867; puisne judge 1889; formerly chairman of quarter sessions and commr. court of requests.

ADAMS, W. H.—B.A. Pemb. Coll., Oxon; honours in law schools; dist. commrnr. Winnebah, G. Coast, Apr., 1891, ag. sheriff, Sept., 1891; dist. commrnr., Acera, Jan., 1892; ag. priv. sec. to Governor Sir W. B. Griffith, 1893.

ADDERLEY, SIR AUGUSTUS JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G. (1884).—Formerly a member of the legislative council, Bahamas; special commissioner for the Bahamas and Jamaica at the International Fisheries Exhibition, 1883; royal commissioner, Colonial and Indian Exhibition, 1885, and executive commissioner for West Indies and British Honduras sections.

ADDISON, GLINTWORTH WALSH FRASER.—Born 1831. Assistant gold commissioner, northern dist., New South Wales, 1858; police magistrate, Tenterfield, 1869; police magistrate, Maitland, 1875; stipendiary magistrate, Sydney, 1882.

ADDISON, R. H.—Clerk, and interp. to R.M. Umbazi, Natal, 1876, to R.M. Lion's River, 1882, Lieut. Zulu Carbineers, 1883, asst. commr. and R.M. Ndwandwe, dist. Zululand, 1887; ditto, Nqutu dist., 1889.

ADRIAN, F. O.—Entered colonial office Sept., 1863; assistant clerk, 2nd class, 28th Dec., 1870; 1st class assistant clerk, 26th Jan., 1877; superintendent of the correspondence branch, general department, and clerk for legal instruments, 1st Oct., 1880; appointed officer of arms of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, May, 1882.

ADYE, GENERAL SIR JOHN MILLER, G.C.B. (1882).—Entered the Royal Artillery, Dec., 1836; captain, 1846; colonel, 1867; assistant adjutant-general, R.A., throughout the Crimean War, also during the Indian Mutiny; served in the Sitana campaign of 1863-4; director of artillery, 1870 to 1875; governor of the Royal Military Academy, Woolwich, June, 1875; surveyor-general of ordnance, May, 1880; and a colonel-commandant, R.A., 1881; was chief of the staff in the expedition to Egypt in 1882 (and received thanks of Parliament); is author of "A Review of the Crimean War," "The Defence of Cawnpore;" governor of Gibraltar, 1 Jan., 1883, to Dec., 1886.

AGNEW, THE HON. SIR JAMES WILSON, M.D., K.C.M.G. (1895), Hobart.—Member of the legislative council, 1877-81, and 1884-7; member (without portfolio) of ministries, 1877-89-81; premier and chief secretary, Mar., 1886, to Mar., 1887, when he resigned. Resigned seat in legislative council, July, 1887. Is a magistrate for the territory, a member of the council of education, a vice-president of the Royal Society, &c.

AIKINS, THE HON. JAMES COX.—Ed. at Victoria University, Canada; returned to Canadian Assembly in 1854, where he sat until 1861; elected to the legislative council, 1862, and at the union of the B. N. A. Provinces in 1867, was called to the Senate by Royal Proclamation; sworn of the Privy Council, 9th Dec., 1869, and was secretary of state and registrar-general of Canada from that date until the resignation of Sir John A. Macdonald in Nov., 1873; re-appointed to same office in Sir J. A.'s new administration, 19th Oct., 1878; became minister of inland revenue; lieut.-governor of Manitoba and Keewatin, Dec., 1882-88.
AITKEN, C. COLQUHOUN.—3rd class clerk, col. sec. office, Jamaica, Feb., 1878; 2nd class clerk, general register office, 1878; 1st class clerk, 1880.

AKERMANN, SIR JOHN WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1887).—Many years member of the legislative council, Natal; served on the immigration board, council of education, &c.; nominated to the executive council, 1870; delegate to the South African conference on confederation in 1876; elected member of the legislative council in 1880; member of native commission of 1882; re-elected stake, 1882; and again to the enlarged council of 1884; and again after dissolution of 1886; and again in 1890-1; delegate to the Cape Town custom's conference, 1888; is a J.P.

AKITOEY, DANIEL.—Born at Lagos, 1863; C.M.S. Coll., Institute, Lagos; copyist to q. Advocate, Lagos, Aug., 1886; registrar of deeds and clerk to registrar-general, Feb., 1887; registrar of instruments affecting land, and chief clerk to general registry, July, 1892; dep. regtr. births, deaths and marriages, and aliens, Jan., 1895; regtr. Mar. to May, 1895.

ALBURY, JOSEPH BENSON, M.D.—Member House of Assembly, Bahamas, 1869; J.P. for public vaccinator, 1881; acting surgeon-superintendent of the New Providence Asylum, 1879; public physician, 1883; commrnr. Col. and Ind. Exhibition, 1885; memb. Leg. Coun., 1889; surg. supt., New Providence Asylum and Victoria Hospital, 1896.

ALEXANDER, ARTHUR HARVEY.—Ed. U. Abdin; sub-inspector, Jamaica constabulary, Oct., 1867; 3rd class inspector, April, 1870; 2nd class inspector, Nov., 1869; 1st class inspector, Oct., 1870; acted as inspector of prisons from April to Oct., 1871; one of the commissioners to inquire into the management of prisons; acting agent-general of immigration, April, 1871; confirmed Oct., 1873; official member of legislative council, 1882; immigration agent general, East Guiana, 1884.

ALEXANDER, GEORGE HAMILTON.—Lieut., "4th Batt. East Surrey Regt." 1889; Certificate School of Musketry, His Majesty's "Militia Cup," 1893; Lieut., April, 1894; Apptd. to "Provisional Battalion," Cheltenham, 1894; Sub-Insp. "Br. Guiana Police," Nov., 1894.
ALEXANDER, J. F.—Mechanical engr.: Maine State Coll., U.S.A.; Manager Technical Sch., Montserrat, June, 1892; ditto St. Kitts, July, 1894.

ALEXANDER, THOMAS.—Sub-inspector, Jamaica constabulary, Jan., 1872; 3rd class inspector, Oct., 1873; 2nd class inspector, Nov., 1874; 1st class musketry certificate, 1879; and course of instruction for convict service at Wormwood Scrubs, 1884.

ALISON, ARCHIBALD.—Educated at Eton and Sandhurst; formerly lieut. 1st batt., now com. 5th batt., Rifle Brigade; A.D.C. to Sir F. N. Broome, Governor of West Australia, 1887; colonial secretary of Bermuda and registrar-general, May, 1888.

ALLAN, ALFRED.—Clerk to Colonial Secretary, St. Vincent, July, 1878; supervisor of customs, Dixcove, Gold Coast, Nov., 1888; transferred to Saltpond, Jan., 1881; acted as district commissioner. various districts from Jan., 1881, to May, 1886; then he was appointed assistant treasurer; treasurer and controller of customs, May, 1890, Feb., 1891; supdt. money order branch and stamps bk. dept., Singapore, June, 1892.

ALLAN, THE HON. GEORGE WILLIAM, D.C.L.—Born 1822; educated at the Upper Canada College; elected to the bar of Upper Canada, 1846; mayor of Toronto, 1855; elected to the Legislative Council of Canada, 1858; summoned to the Senate of the Dominion of Canada, 1867; lieut.-colonel of the Canadian Militia; Chancellor of the University of Trinity College, Toronto; speaker, Senate, 1879.
ALLAN, GORDON.—Assistant surveyor-general, British Honduras, 1885; surveyor-general, 1886.

ALLARDYCE, W. L.—Cik. and interp.; Provincial Department Fiji, 1879, transferred to Sumatra, 1882; stip. mag., July, 1882; asst. commr.; and stip. mag. and inspr. of native police, Kadavu, Jan., 1890; native lds. commnsmr., memb. leg. coun., 1894; native lds. commnsmr., 1895; ag. asst. col. sec., April, July, and Aug., 1896.

ALLDRIDGE, THOMAS J., F.R.G.S., F.Z.S.—Ag. U.S. consular agent at S. Leone, 1871-2; travelling commssr. for colony; hen. commr. of police, and J.P., 1889; on special mission to interior, 1890, and concluded treaties with various chiefs placing their countries under protection; hon. corresp. sec. roy. col. inst., dist. commissioner and coroner, Br. Sherbro, member, 1893.

ALLEN, SIR JOHN CAMPBELL (KT. BACH., LL.D.)—Barrister of New Brunswick, 1840; was a commissioner, 1845-7, for settling claims to lands in the Madawaska Settlement under the Treaty of Washington of 1842; a member of the House of Assembly of New Brunswick, 1856-65; solicitor general, 1865-7; member of the House of Assembly, 1863-5; attorney general, 1865; delegate from the Government of New Brunswick to the British Government on the subject of confederation in that year; a judge of the supreme court of New Brunswick, Sept., 1865; chief justice, Oct., 1875; president of the court of trial of divorce and matrimonial causes, 1866-1865-65, official reporter of supreme court of province.

ALLWOOD, JAMES.—Second clerk, governor's secretary's office, Jamaica, July, 1862; acting second clerk in the executive committee office, Feb., 1863; May, 1866; clerk immigration department, July to Oct., 1866; first class clerk, finance office, July, 1866, to Dec., 1869; supervisor of district offices, Oct., 1871, to Mar., 1876; now assistant colonial secretary.

ALLWOOD, JAMES.—Solicitor of supreme court, Jamaica, and deputy clerk of the peace, parish of St. Mary, June, 1866; clerk of the peace, parish of St. Elizabeth, Oct., 1868; clerk, Savannah district court, Nov., 1868; ditto, Montego Bay, Jan., 1869; also clerk of the circuit court, July, 1870; clerk, southern district court, Jan., 1880; assistant clerk, colonial secretary's office, Nov., 1885; R.M., 1888.
ALVES, J. M. S.—Third clerk in the colonial secretary's department, Hong Kong, Jan., 1865; clerk, Feb., 1870; 1st clerk, Feb., 1881.

AMIRAYAN, G. G.—Called to the Bar, Inner Temple, 1894; clerk and translator, commissioner's office, Larnaca, Cyprus, Oct., 1878; translator, high court of justice and court of Temyiz, 1881; registrar, supreme court, 1883; was secretary to commission appointed in 1888 to inquire into organization of Cyprus Police.

AMPTHILL, 2nd Baron, creat. 1881, OLIVER ARTHUR VILLIERS RUSSELL, son of the 1st Baron. Born 1869, succeeded his father, 1884. Ed. at Eton and New Coll. Oxon. Rowed in the Oxford eight, 1889-91; presdt. of the Oxford Union, 1891; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, Sec. of State for the Colonies, June, 1895. Is lieut. Royal 1st Devon Yeomanry Cavalry.

ANDERSON, DAVID JULIUS.—Ed. Roy. Coli. Mauritius, and Edin. Univ.; assistant master, Royal College, 9th July, 1867; master, 1872; assistant professor, 1873; acting head master, Royal College School, Curepipe, 1878; head master, Royal College School, Port Louis, 1881; superintendent of schools, Mauritius, Jan., 1888.

ANDERSON, FRANCIS HENRY, M.D., M.C., Edin.—Asst. res. surg. col. hosp. Demerara, July, 1867; res. surg. penal settlement, July, 1870; ditto, col. hosp. July, 1873; med. offr. Buxton dist. Sep., 1873; ditto, Pleasance dist. Nov., 1878; ditto, Georgetown dist. Sep., 1887; acted as med. offr. immigr. dept. July 1877 to Feb., 1878, and July, 1882 to Dec., 1883; and as surg.-general, Dec., 1888 to May, 1889.

ANDERSON, Capt. F. J.—Cadet, R.M. Acad., 1876; served in India as asst. engr., military works dept., 1881-8; Burma expln., 1885 (medal and clasp); dep. col. engr. and surveyor-gen., Penang, April, 1892; acted also as pres. municipal commrs., Penang, 1893-4; ag. col. engr. and surveyor-gen., S.S., Mar., 1896.
ANDERSON, FRANK.—Assist. surveyor, Gold Coast, April, 1885; ag. surveyor-gen., June, 1886; asst. surveyor, Lagos, Mar., 1887, surveyor-gen., Aug., 1888.

ANDERSON, JOHN.—M.A., Aberdeen, 1877; 1st class honours in mathematics; gold medal as most distinguished graduate of year; second class clerk, Colonial Office, 30th June, 1879; Bacon scholar, Gray's Inn, 1887; Inns of Court studentship, 1888; joint commr. with the late Sir J. F. Dickson, Nov. and Dec., 1891, to inquire into certain matters connected with the registry of the supreme court of Gibraltar; private secy. to Sir R. Meade, 17th Aug., 1892. Attached to the staff of the British agent for the Behring Sea Arbitration in London and Paris, 1892-93; 1st class clerk, 11th Mar., 1896.
ANDERSON, JOHN GERARD, M.A.—Under secretary for public instruction, Queensland, 1 Nov., 1878.

ANDERSON, SIR WILLIAM JOHN, KT. BACH. (1896). Educated at Pembroke Coll., Oxford; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1869; judge of the Supreme Court, Turks Islands, Feb., 1874; acting district judge, Jamaica, June, 1882, to May, 1883; and again, April, 1884, confirmed 1886; R.M. Kingston and St. Andrews, 1888; ch. justice Br. Honduras, 1890.
ANDREWS, C. W.—C.C. and R.M., Middelburg Division, Cape of Good Hope, Sept., 1870; C.C. and R.M. Albert Division, 1875; C.C. and R.M., Beaufort West, Oct., 1884.

ANDREWS, MARTINDALE STEWART.—Educated at Merchant Taylors; clerk, Eastern Telegraph Co. at Alexandria, Aug., 1882; 1st class clerk, African Direct Telegraph Co., Bathurst, June, 1886; supt. Accra, Feb., 1887; director of telegraphs, G. Coast Colony, June, 1891.
ANDREWS, WALTER BOYD TATE, J.P.—Deputy registrar-general, 1856, South Australia; registrar-general 1863.

ANGELO, Lieutenant-Colonel E. Fox.—Ensign 14th December, 1834; lieutenant, 13th April, 1855; captain, 9th February, 1864; major, 1st October, 1877; lieutenant-colonel, 2nd March, 1878; served with the 28th regiment in the Crimea, from August, 1855, including the siege and fall of Sebastopol (medal with clasp and Turkish medal); adjutant, Decoeur irregular force, 1860; aide-de-camp to major-general Frank Adams, C.B., 1867; station staff officer, Roorkee, 1871; deputy-assistant quartermaster-general, Saugor district, 1871; brigade-major, Agra, 1874; deputy-assistant adjutant-general, Morar, 1875; assistant adjutant-general, Oude division, 1876; commandant local forces, Tasmania, 1880; inspecting field officer, Western Australia, 1882; government resident, northern division, 1886; R.M., Bunbury, 1889; R.M., Rottnest, 1890.

ANGERS, The Hon. Auguste Réal.—Solr.gen., Quebec, 1874-6; atty. gen., 1876-8; leader of upper house for three years; then puisne judge, superior court, Quebec; lieutenant-governor, Quebec, 1887; senator and minister of agriculture, Canada, Dec., 1892 to 1895.
ANGOVE, Thomas.—Probation clerk, audit-office, Western Australia, July, 1880; second clerk, Treasury, 1886.
ANGUS, James.—Asst. Storekeeper-General, Mauritius, 21st Aug., 1868, has acted on several occasions as Storekeeper General.

ANSON, Major General Sir Archibald Edward Harbord, R.A., C.M.G. (1876), K.C.M.G. (1882)—Entered the royal military academy, Woolwich, 1841; presented with sword for exemplary conduct; 2nd lieut., R.A., June, 1844; 1st lieutenant 1st April, 1846; 2nd captain, 9th July, 1852; 1st captain, 1st Sept., 1853; brevet major, 21st July, 1864; lieutenant-colonel, 12th May, 1866; brevet-colonel, 12th May, 1874; colonel, 23rd Dec., 1875; retired with the honorary rank of major-general 26th Aug., 1879; served at the siege of Sebastopol in 1855; Crimean medal and clasp, 5th class Medjidieh, and Turkish medal; inspector-general of police, Mauritius, Sept., 1858; was employed in 1862 as the responsible member of the British Mission to the court of Radama II of Madagascar, on the occasion of his coronation; lieutenant-governor of Prince of Wales Island, Feb., 1867; admin. gov. Straits Settlements, 1871, 1877 and 1879; conducted the successful military operations for repelling the invasion of the state of Sungai Ujong, 1875-76 (medal and clasp); retired, 1882; J.P. for Sussex.

ANSON, Frank Charles Montresor.—Asst. commissary of tax, B. Guiana, May, 1881; dist. commissary and insp. of weights and measures, June, 1884; secy. to "road arrears commn.", 1889; memb. of commn. to inquire into and assess damages caused by riots, 1890; J.P., 1892; chairman bd. of examiners under "weighers and gaugers ordinance, 1883;" member central bd. of health and excise bd., and act. ch. commissary, 1892-93, 1893-94, 1894-95.
ANTELM, Sir Celicourt, C.M.G. (1890), K.C.M.G. (1890).—Senior unofficial member of the council of government, Mauritius.

ANTHONISZ, James Oliver.—Cadet, Straits Settlements, Oct., 1883; B.A., St. John's College, Cambridge (Senior Optime); assistant Indian immigration agent, Singapore, May, 1886; 3rd magistrate, Singapore, 1888; 2nd ditto, July, 1892; ag. official assignee and regt. of deeds Surveyor, Oct., 1891; ag. 1st mag., July, 1895.

ANTON, C. FALCONER.—Auditor, Oct. 6th Feb. to May, 1877; harbour master May, 1877; Jan., 1881; confidential clerk and clerk of the peace, 1881; stipendiary magistrate, St. Lucia, 1883; auditor, Mar., 1885; treas. comptroller customs and postms., Grenada, 1890.

ANTON, GEORGE.—Clerk, audit-office, Nov. 1872; 2nd clk. col. secretary's-clk., 1878; ag. chief clerk, April to Sept., 1879; again in 1880; treas. accountant St. Vincent, 1881; ag. auditor, Aug., 1888, to end of register supreme court, Dec., 1891; J.P., July, 1892.

ANTROBUS, REGINALD L.—Educated at Chester and at New College, Oxford; of New College, 1872; first class in examinations, 1874; second class in final school, 1876; B.A., 1876; appointed after competitive examination, to be a clerk colonial office, May 3rd, 1877; assistant secretary to the Earl of Kimberley, 5th Oct. to Dec., 1882; private secretary to the Derby, 16th Dec., 1882, to 24th June, 1886; Colonel the Right Hon. F. A. Stanley M.P. (Earl of Derby) 24th June, 1885, to 6th Aug., 1886; and to Earl Granville 6th Feb. to Aug., 1886; administered post of St. Helena, 5th Nov., 1889, to 8th June, 1890; privy. tary to Mr. Mende, 1st Feb., 1892, and to Buxton M.P., 17th Aug., 1892; senior clerk, 1892; principal clerk, 1896.
APPLEYARD, CHARLES W.—Appointed competitive examination to be a clerk, secretariat civil service, and assigned to colony March 14, 1893; matric. Lond., 1893.

ARCHER, F. BISSET.—Clerk in Londonminster Bk., 1884; prin. clk. col. sec. off., 1894; ag. chief clk., Ap., 1894, to Mar., 1895; Oct., 1895 to Jan., 1896, when confirmed; col. sec., Aug. to Oct., 1896; clk. of councils to Oct., 1896; lieut. G. C. rifle volks; ag. to Oct., 1896.
ARCHER, T., C.M.G. (1884).—Asst. for Queensland, 1882 to 1884; reappointed June, 1888; resigned 1890.
ARCHIBALD, Sir ADAMS G., K.C.M.G. (1885) (C.M.G.), 1872.—Lieutenant-Gov. Manitoba and North-West Territories, Lt.-Gov. of Province of Nova Scotia, 1873-83.
ARDAGH, W. D.—Deputy attorney-general, Manitoba.
ARMBRISTER, PERCY W. D.—Acting provost, prothonotary and clerk of Crown, 1884; temporary clerk, Treasury, 1885; ad. police court, 1887.
ARMOUR, J. D.—President and chief of superior court, Ontario.

ARMSTRONG, WILLIAM THOMAS.—Revenue officer, District D., Barbados, Jan., J.P.; sheriff of St. Joseph's parish; lieut. James troop of yeomanry cavalry, 1859; police magistrate of District D., April, 1874-75; inspector of inland revenue offices, 1879; police magistrate District F., 1884.

ARUNACHALAM, P.—Ed. Academy Colombo, Ceylon, and Christ's Coll., Ceylon, which he was a scholar, B.A. 1875; M.A. called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1876; Ceylon Civil Service, April, 1875; commr. of requests, police magistrate, 1876; distm. June, 1887; commr. of requests, 1892.
HIBURNHAM, JOHN ANCHITEL.—Registrar r. Justice Shippard, Cape Town, Mar., 1885; administrator, Br. Bechuanaland, Oct., 1885, tary, 1888.

HILLEY, HON. ANTHONY EVELYN MELBOURNE, a of the 7th Earl of Shaftesbury; born 1836; ed at Harrow and Trinity College, Cam- r., M.A., 1858. Called to the bar at Lincoln's 1863. Was for some time secretary to the late Palmerston; is a magistrate for Dorset, and treasurer of county courts of Dorset from 1874; is 2nd church estates commissioner; amenary secretary to the Board of Trade, l., 1880; parliamentary under secretary of for the colonies, 12th May, 1882, to 24th 1885. Sat in the House of Commons for e from May, 1873, to March, 1880, and for the Wight from April, 1880, to November, . Is author of a life of Lord Palmerston.

HILLEY, E. C.—Master, government schools, ritis, March, 1863; clerk, colonial secretary's 2. July, 1864; chief clerk, railway department, '65; corresponding clerk, colonial secretary's e. April, 1866; acting registrar 20th Jan. to Oct., 1875, and 10th Oct. to 21st Nov., 1874; clerk audit office, July, 1875; officer in charge audit office, Feb. 1876, to May, 1877. Mar to , 1881, and Sept., 1886; ag. auditor-gen., .1887; chairman public offices inquiry com- June, 1888; acting registrar-gen., April, to Aug., 1884; collr. of customs, Apr. ; ag. audr.-gen., Jan., 1894; confirmed Nov., .

SHMORE, ALEX. MURRAY.—Writer, Ceylon vice, 1876; police magistrate, Panvila, 1878; assistant government agent, Western Pro- es, 1883; ditto, Central Province, 1884; ng second assistant colonial secretary, 1887; unstr. of requests and pol. mag., Kandy, 1891; g colonial sec., Gold Coast, Feb., 1894; rec., Cyprus, March, 1895; ag. chief sec., June to , 1896.
TCHLEY, C.—Entered the colonial office, .1868; served in chief clerk's accountant's and al departments; assistant clerk, second class, supt. of the Library, 1st Oct., 1880.

TKINSON, JOHN MITFORD, M.B., (Lond.), I.; M.R.C.S. (Eng.) and L.S.A., 1878, D.P.H. (Lond.), 1894.—Late med.off. No. 3 dist. St. Mary bots, Kensington; supt. Govt. civil and small- hospital, Hong Kong, 1878; med. officer acy/asylum, 1891; J.P. 1896; ag. col.surg., 1895.

TKINSON, NICHOLAS.—Called to the bar, temple, 11th June, 1867; admitted to practice British Guiana, Feb., 1868; acting solicitor-neral, 1869, 1870, and Mar., 1873, to Oct., 1874, when he was confirmed; puisne judge, Aug., 86; acting chief justice 1888 and 1889.

ATTRILL, GEORGE.—Appointed, after a com- examination, to be a clerk of the lower (or second) division of the Civil Service, and signed to the Colonial Office, 9th June, 1884; assistant colonial sec., Gold Coast, Oct., 1893.
ATTYGALLE, J., M.R.C.M.—Medical assistant rmedical department in Ceylon, 1866; assistant colonial surgeon, 1867; colonial surgeon, 1890.

AUBER, STEVEN J.—Temporary outdoor treer, Customs, Sierra Leone, June, 1878; tide after, Aug., 1879; landing waitr., Sept., 1880; minor acting measuring surveyor of shipping ag., 1882; clerk of customs, Gambia, 1883.
ALBERT, EDGAR, C.M.G. (1892).—Poor law commissioner, Mauritius, 1892; memb. coun. of d., 1892, of Bd. of Health, 1894; nominated member coun. of Govt. 1896.

AUCHINLECK, W. D.—3rd clerk, treasury, Antigua, 1867; 1st clerk, 1873; landing surveyor, 1874; treasurer and collector of customs, Nevis, 1881; treasurer St. Kitts's and Nevis, and member of Executive and Legislative Councils, 1883; M.L.C., Leeward Islands.

AUSTIN, JOHN GARDINER, C.M.G. (1876).—Was acting stipendiary magistrate in British Guiana from Nov., 1849, to May, 1850; assistant government secretary from May, 1850, to June, 1851, acting government secretary, June, 1851, to Dec., 1851; assistant government secretary, Dec., 1851, to Feb., 1853; and immigration agent-general, from Feb., 1853; lieut.-governor of Honduras, 1864; colonial secretary of Hong Kong, 1868; adminis- tered the government during October, 1874, and from March 11th, 1875. Retired on pension, 1878.
AUSTIN, J. H.—Deputy commissioner of crown lands, Nova Scotia.

AUSTIN, REV. PRESTON BRUCE.—1st divn. Lond. matric. and deacon (Durh.), 1888; priest, 1890; asst.mr. Huddersfield Coll. Yorks.1884-88; curate Georgetown Cathedral, R. Guiana, 1888, and of Demerara River dist. 1892; ag. asst. mr. Queen's Coll., Georgetown, 1889; J.P. for the Colony, 1893.

AWDRY, REG. V.—Cadet, Sarawak service, Mar., 1876; priv. sec. to H.H. the Rajah, G.C.M.G., April, 1877; ag. mag. ct. of requests, July, 1879; postmr.-gen., Jan., 1891; ag. treasurer and clerk Sup. Council, May, 1895; resdt., 2nd class, 1st div. June, 1898.

AYERS, THE HON. SIR HENRY, G.C.M.G. (1894). K.C.M.G. (1872).—Was member of the Cabinet of South Australia without office, in Mr. Dutton's ministry, July, 1863; chief secretary, July, 1863, to October 1865, and from May, 1867, to Sept., 1868, and again from Oct. 13, 1868, to Nov. 2, 1868; also from Jan., 1872, to July, 1873, and again in 1876 and 1887; president of the legislative council from 1881 to 1893.

AYLES, PHILIP BURNANI CHENERY, C.M.G., (1893). M.R.C.S.E., L.M., L.R.C.P., Edin., July, 1865. Acting surg., Wanisworth Gaol, Nov., 1865; staff-surg., Mauritius coolie emigration, Nov., 1865; acting surg. super. and mag. Flat Island, Mauritius, 1866; acting health officer and surg. in charge of temp. hospital, Port Louis, during fever epidemic, 1867 and 1868; resigned, 1868; civil medical officer and super. of labour transport, Kooshtia sub-division, also surgeon to the Eastern Bengal Railway, 1869; civil medical officer and super. of gaol, Sibsagor division, Assam, April 1873; colonial surgeon and inspector of hospitals Hong Kong, also surgeon of lock hospitals, Sept. 1873; J.P., 1874.

BABY, LOUIS FRANCOIS GEORGES.—Born 1834; called to the bar 1857; created a Q.C. 1873; entered Canadian House of Commons, 1872; sworn of the privy council and appointed minister of inland revenue, 26th Oct., 1878; puisne judge of the Court of Queen's Bench, Quebec.
BACKHOUSE, ALFRED P.—Crown prosecutor, N.W. dist., N.S. Wales, Oct. 1878; ditto S. dist., Jan., 1881, ct. Dist. judge, N.W. dist., 1884.

BADELEY, FRANCIS JOSEPH.—Ed. Clergy Orphan Asylum School, Canterbury, and Jesus Coll. Camb., Senior opt. math. Tripos 1889; Hong Kong cadet, Oct. 1890; passed cadet, Jan. 1893; ag. dep. supt. of police, June to Dec., 1893; ag. asst. postmr. gen., May to July, 1894; ag. asst. regr. gen., July, 1894; J.P., 1894; dep. suptdt. of police and asst. suptdt. fire brigade, July, 1895.

BADEN-POWELL, Sir GEORGE SMYTH, K.C.M.G. (1888), C.M.G. (1884), M.P.—Educated at Marlborough and Balliol College, Oxford, where he graduated in honours, obtaining the Chancellor's prize English essay in 1876. He is the author of "New Homes for the Old Country," "Protection and Bad Times," and "State Aid and State Interference;" and numerous articles dealing with political subjects. Member of the West Indies Finance Commission, 1882. M.P. for the Kirkdale Division of Liverpool, 1885, and again 1886. Member of commission for delimitation of electoral districts in Malta, Jan., 1888; commissioner in connection with the Behring Sea question, 1891 and 1892.
BAILEY, D. J. S., B.A. (Camb.)—Served in Methuen's Horse; cadet Sarawak Service, Mar., 1888; asst. resdt., May, 1891; resdt., 2nd class, 2nd divn., Jan., 1894.

BAILEY, J. B. A.—Appointed a writer to the Ceylon government, 1863; commissioner of requests and police magistrate of Kalpitya, 1865; assistant government agent, Trincomalee, January, 1866; district judge, Batticaloa, acting 2nd assistant colonial secretary, March, 1867. District judge, Batticaloa, Dec., 1867; district judge, &c., Trincomalee, and acting assistant government agent, Puttalam, Jan., 1870; second assistant colonial secretary, May, 1870; fiscal for the central province, April, 1872; asst. gov't. agent, Mannar, Sept., 1872; registrar-general, 1875; grain commissioner, 1880; govt. ag't. North Central Prov., 1882; ditto Eastn. Prov., 1883; ditto N.W. Prov., 1889; ditto Central Prov., 1895.

BAILEY, WILLIAM H.—Entered the service June, 1875, as third clerk, Customs Department, Barbados; promoted second clerk, Nov., 1876; chief clerk, Colonial Secretary's Office, and clerk of Legislative Council, May, 1882; acted for five months in 1892 as governor, Glendary Prison; acting auditor-general, June, 1893, to April, 1894. Aug. to Dec., 1895, and July to Nov., 1896.

BAIN, THOMAS CHARLES JOHN, A.I.C.E.—Assistant to inspector of roads, Cape, May, 1848; suptdt. of convicts and assistant inspector of roads, Breede River station, Bain's Kloof, Mar., 1852; inspector of roads, Jan., 1854; district railway engineer, Worcester extension, Feb., 1873; district inspector of roads, Oct., 1874; at different times held the appointment of visiting magistrate in conjunction with that of inspector of roads at Grey's Pass, Knysna, and the George stations; is J.P. for the western province; a road magistrate since 1854; geological and irrigation surveyor, July, 1888.
BAKER, A. H.—Sub inspr. Br. Guiana Police, Oct., 1892.
BAKER, CAPTAIN A. W.—Inspector-commandant and inspector of weights and measures; also captain of fire brigade, Trinidad, 1877; inspector of prisons, British Guiana, 1888.

BAKER, EZRAEL, A.—Member of executive council and Secy. for lands, New South Wales, Feb. to Mar., 1877; secy. for mines, Oct. to Nov., 1877; secy. for lands, Nov. to Dec., 1877; secy. for mines 1878 to 1881.
BAKER, WILLIAM, A. B.—Assistant clerk in president's office, Nevis, 1878; clerk to provost marshal and registrar of courts, 1879; is also clerk to boards of health and quarantine.

BALL, JAMES DYER, M.R.A.S.—Ed. King Coll., Lond.; employed in Register-General's Office, Hong Kong, Feb., 1875 in mag. May, 1875; in edn. dept., Mar., 1876; passed in Chinese act. ast. interp. of Chinese sup. ct., Oct., 1877; chief interp., 1881; dep. marshal V.A.C. in 1881 and 1882; commiss' of oaths, 1881; employed as consul at Canton in connection with Legat., 1883; judge's clk. sup. ct., Mar., 1884; author of several grammatical and school books of Chinese.
BALLANTINE, DAVID.—Sub. collr. customs Br. N. Guinea, July, 1889; mag. for native affs., Nov., 1890; collr. of customs, regr.-gen. treas., Dec., 1893.
BALLARD, H.—Capt. of the port, Durban Natal.
BALLARD, R.—Chief engineer of Central Northern Railways, Queensland, 1878.
BAMBERGER, A. N.—Resident magistrate Bedford division, Cape Colony, 16th June, 1881.

BAMFYLDE, CHARLES A.—Cadet, Sarawak Civil Service, June, 1875; ast. resdt. and A.P. to II.H. the Rajah, G.C.M.G., May, 1876; asst. (2nd class), Jan., 1882; served in British North Borneo as sub-resdt. and actg. resdt. of Eastern from 1882 to 1884; rejoined Sarawak service in 1884; actg. resdt., 1st div., April, 1885 to Nov., 1886; resdt. (1st class), 3rd div., and member supreme council, Jan., 1888; resdt. of 1st div., Sarawak proper, July, 1890.

BANBURY, GEORGE ALEXANDER.—Appointed to Legacy and Succession Duty Office, Lagos, May, 1873 (upper grade), after competitive examination; assistant colonial secretary and treasurer, Sierra Leone, May, 1880; acted as postmaster, 1880 to 1882; acting collector of customs, 1882; acting colonial secretary and treasurer and member of executive and legislative councils, 1884; assistant colonial secretary and treasurer, St. Helena, 1894; acting colonial secretary and member of executive council, Mar., 1895; collector of dues and taxes, Seychelles, Sept., 1895; collr. treasurer and manager of savings bank, 1889.

BANNERMAN, C. J.—Temporary clerk, coroner's office, Gold Coast, Aug., 1880; qua. advocates' clerk, July, 1884; chief interpreter and clerk to Chief Justice, 1885; taxing master and reg'r. of deeds, 1889; commiss' of oaths, 1890; student, Middle Temple, Michaelmas.
BANNERMAN, W.—Extra clerk, audit. Gold Coast, 1882; 3rd clerk, 1888; junior medical dept., 1889; chief clerk, 1889.

BAPTISTE, GEORGE ALBERT CLARENCE.—Ed. City School, Patna, and Patna College, articulated, Calcutta Univ., Dec., 1867; ass't commissioner, Patna division, Bengal; sub-dec. magistrate and collector, Tirhoot, 1872; in 1873 passed the test in Hindustani, surveying, engineering, and law; salt superintendent, Champaran; and special deputy collector for irrigation purposes, Patna, Shahabad, and Patna, 1873; in 1874 passed the higher examination in law and Hindustani; 1875, deputy magistrate and collector, Patna, but continued on special duty; 1877, on special duty for the acquisition of land for the Tirhoot State Railway; sub-dec. magistrate, Mauritius, July, 1877.
BARBADOS, 5th Bishop of.—(Founded by Right Rev. Herbert Bree, D.D., etc.)

BARLOW, G. PRATT.—Cadet, Sarawak Civil Service, May, 1878; resdt., (2nd class), 3rd div., Jan., 1887; result, Muka, Oct., 1890; ag. resdt., Baram, July, 1892; of Biutulu, Aug., 1893; resdt., Lower Rejang, Oct., 1894.

BARNARD, HENRY CUTHBERT, Assoc. M.Inst.C.E.—Devon and Cornwall railway, 1882 to 1885; assist. engineer public works department, Perak, 1887 to May, 1890; acting resident engineer and traffic manager, Perak State railway, May, 1890; confirmed Aug., 1891.

BARNARD, MAJOR JOHN HENRY, C.M.G. (1874)—Of the Royal Munster Fusiliers; appointed to the Order of St. Michael and St. George for service in connection with the Ashantee war, 1873-74, under Sir J. H. Glover.

BARNES, DOUGLAS DRURY.—Ag. sub. inspr. of pol., Br. Guiana, Sept., 1886; 2nd elk., inspr. gen. office, Jan., 1887; acted as inspr. of pol. on several occasions; dist. inspr. of pol., Aug., 1891; Hythe certificate, Sept., 1891; taken prisoner by Venezuelans while in command of Uruan Station, Cuyuni River, Jan., 1895; suptdt. of police, Br. Honduras, June, 1895; is a J.P. for the Colony.

BARNES, JOHN FREDERICK EVELYN, M.I.C.E.; M.I.C.E. Ire.—Asst. county surveyor, Antrim, 1872; on Abercorn Estates, 1873-9; govt. surveyor Natal, 1879; Durban borough engineer 1882; asst. col. engineer, Natal, 1882; engineer, P.W. dept., 1894.

BARNES, WARREN DELABERE, R.A.—Ed. King's Coll. School, and Pemb. Coll., Camb.; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1888; passed final exam. in Chinese, Nov., 1891; 3rd mag., Penang, 1893; ag. protector of Chinese, Perak, Oct., 1893; warden of mines, Perak, Jan., 1896.

BARNETT, HENRY C.—Colonial surgeon, York, West Australia, and medical officer to convicts, York District, 1868; J.P., 1869; colonial surgeon at Fremantle, 1872; is member of medical board, district registrar vaccinator, and superintending surgeon lunatic asylum.
BARNETT, JAMES.—Colonial architect, New South Wales.
BARNEY, GEORGE HIGGIN.—Inspector of distilleries, N. S. Wales, April, 1855; chief inspector of distilleries and sugar refineries, June, 1880.

BARRETT, S.—Suptdt. of natives, Grahamstown, Cape, Oct., 1875; also inspector of locations, Albany, 1876; commanded a Fingo levy in Gaika war, 1878; R.M., Quthing, Basutoland, July, 1882; asst. commr., Leribe, Nov., 1886; Basutoland commr. on boundary delimitation between Basutoland and O.F.S., Sept., 1891.

BARROW, ROBERT.—Landing-waiter and revenue officer, St. Lucia, Feb., 1882; keeper of Kingstown prison, St. Vincent, Mar., 1883; was for 23 years in 2nd W. I. Regt.; served in Baddiboo expedition of 1861; throughout the Ashanti campaign of 1873-4; was awarded the medal for "Distinguished Conduct in the Field," and the medal with annuity for "Long and Meritorious Service."

BARRY, Sir JACOB DIRK, KNT. BACHEL. (created 1878).—Ed. Cheltenham Coll.; recorder of the high court, Griqua Land West, 1875; appointed temporarily to administer the government, 1875; member of both councils, 1875; judge in the eastern districts, Cape, 1878; puisne judge, supreme court, 1880.
BARTON, THE HON. E., Q.C.—Member legislative assembly, N.S. Wales; attorney general in Dibbs' ministry, 1891-3.

BARTROP, GEORGE FREDERICK.—Barrister-at-Law; emigrated to Victoria, 1852; clerk, audit office, Melbourne, 1854; clerk of petty sessions, 1855; clerk of the peace, &c., Sandhurst, 1860; ditto and deputy sheriff, Ballarat, 1868; warden of the gold fields, 1878; clerk of the peace and registrar county court, Melbourne, 1878; author of "The County Court Manual, 1869;" police magistrate, warden, and coroner of Victoria, 1887.

BASTOW, HENRY R.—Educated as an architect in West of England. Practised several years in Tasmania; employed in Victoria water supply department, 1866; railway department, 1871; architect to education department, 1873; chief architect, public works department, 1884.
BATH, JAMES.—Secretary to board of education, South Australia, 1st Aug., 1867; secretary to minister of justice and education, 1st Aug., 1863.

BATHURST, W. DUNDAS.—Ed. Lanceng, Coll.; agent of Congo Free State, 1883-6; appointed to colonial audit branch exchequer and audit department, Jan., 1889; local auditor, Gibraltar under C. and A. G., Jan., 1891.
BATTEEN, R.—In the excise, England, July, 1862; chief clerk, collector general's office, Jamaica, Dec., 1870; supervisor of revenue offices, Nov., 1878; has acted as collector-general several times.

BATY, SEBASTIAN CONRAD ELTON, M.A.—Ed. St. Paul's School, and St. Peter's Coll., Camb., B.A. in honours, classical tripos, 1889, M.A., 1892; sworn land surveyor Mauritius and dependencies, 1890; govt. surveyor and subdtt. of public works, Seychelles, Sept., 1892.

BAUMGARTNER, G. A.—Writer Ceylon Civil Service, July, 1871. Attached to Colombo Kachcheri, Oct., 1871; additional police magistrate, Kalutara, July, 1873. Attached to colonial secretary's office, Oct., 1873; police magistrate, Point Pedro, 14th Feb., 1874; acting assistant to government agent, Kandy, April, 1876; assistant to government agent, Jaffna, Sept., 1876; ditto, Kandy, Nov., 1876; acting district judge, Badulla, Feb., 1878; acting assistant to government agent, Jaffna, Feb., 1880; district judge, Tangalle, Feb., 1883; ditto, Badulla, 1885; ditto, Negombo, Dec., 1894; regt. gen., May, 1895.

BAUMGARTNER, HARRY PERCY.—Writer in Ceylon service, Oct. 29, 1875; extra office assistant at Colombo to government agent, western province, Jan., 1877; attached to police court, Colombo, Nov., 1877; commissioner of requests and police magistrate, Puttalam and Kalpitiya, Feb., 1878; extra office assistant, Colombo Kachcheri, Feb., 1878; acting assistant government agent, Kalutara, March, 1878; acting office assistant at Colombo, July, 1878; acting landing surveyor and tide surveyor, customs, Galle, Nov., 1878; acting extra assistant at Kalutara to the government agent western province Jan., 1879; acting office assistant at Anuradhapura May, 1879; acting office assistant at Colombo, Oct., 1879; office assistant at Kandy to the government agent central province, Feb., 1883; landing surveyor of customs, Colombo, April, 1884; assistant government agent, Matara, 1891; dep. collr. of customs, May, 1894.

BAXENDALE, ARTHUR SALISBURY, M.I.E.E.—Ed. St. Paul's, Stony Stratford, and Leamington Coll., and at Schl. of Elec. Engineering; for six years in service of Eastern and Eastern Express Tel. Co.; supt. of posts and telegraphs, Selborne, Oct., 1888.

BAXENDALE, F. R. S.—Ed. at Charterhouse; cadet Fiji civil service, 1884; passed cadet, 1885; European offr. armed native constabulary, 1886; clerk and interpreter provincial dept., 1888; mag., 1889; resdt. commissr., Cakaudros Province, 1892; ag. supt. of police and dep.-commissr. armed native constab., Apr., 1896.

BAXTER, ERNEST CHARLES.—Accountant clerk; clerk, R.M. court; sub-distr. assessor stamps, taxing master, and J.P., Vryburg, Free Bechuanaland, Oct., 1885; examiner of accounts, Feb., 1889; collector of customs, 1891.
BAXTER, JACOB.—Member for Halidows Ontario Leg. Assembly; speaker thereof.

BAYLY, MAJOR GEORGE C.—(5th Batt. Brigade).—Passed school of instruction, 1879; 1st class extra certificate, Hythe, 1880; instructor of musketry, 1886; asst. insp. G.C., Dec., 1883; dist. commr., Lagos, and Benin, 1884; ditto, Cape Coast, 1885; sub. insp. of Honduras Constabulary, Dec., 1888; mag. on Walk, Nov., 1889, to Nov., 1890; private secret and aide-de-camp to Sir A. Moloney, Sept., 1892, to Feb., 1892.
BAYLY, LIEUTENANT-COLONEL Z.S., C.M.G. (1880).—Commanding right wing, Cap. Mass Rifles; and acting commdt.-gen. of Cape corps forces; commandant general, 1881; retires 1892.

BAYNES, EDWARD.—Was private secretary governor Leeward Islands 1869 to 1873; clerk in colonial secretary's office; clerk to the legislature and executive council, and clerk to the legislature of Antigua; acting president, Morisset, May, 1886, to Oct., 1887; commr.-in-chief, Morisset, Mar., 1889; ag. commr.-in-chief, Dominica, Feb., 1891, to Mar., 1892, and Mar., 1894, to June, 1895.

BAYNES, THOMAS.—Called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1878; awarded scholarship of one hundred guineas in Real and Personal Property Law, 1879; acted as solicitor-general, Leeward Islands, 1880; confirmed May, 1886; crown member of general and local councils, 1887; commr. to revise law of colony; puisne judge, Leewards, 1889.

BEACH, THE RIGHT HON. SIR MONTAGU EDWARD HICKS, 9th Baronet (created 1619). P.M.—Born 1837; educated at Eton and Christ Church, Oxon, first class in law and modern history; final examination, July, 1858; B.A., 1858; M.A., 1861; deputy-lieutenant for Gloucestershire, 1864; M.P. for East Gloucestershire, July, 1864; parliamentary secretary to the poor law board, 1868; under-secretary of state for the home department, Aug., to Dec., 1868; chief secretary to the lord-lieutenant of Ireland, and sworn member of the privy council, Feb., 1874; added to a seat in the cabinet, 1876; secretary of state for the colonies, 4th Feb., 1878, to 28th Aug., 1880; chancellor of the exchequer and lord president of the House of Commons, 24th June, 1885, to 1886; chief secretary to the lord-lieutenant of Ireland, Aug., 1886, to Jan., 1887; president of the board of trade 1889-92; chancellor of the exchequer, 28th June, 1895; M.P. for West Riding since 1885.
BEAL, J. C.—Educated St. James' Grammar School, Sydney; superintendent government printing office, Queensland, 1862; acting government printer, Dec., 1866; confirmed, 1867.

BEARD, CHARLES HALMAN.—Called to the bar, Leeward Islands, Mar., 1875; awarded Examin by council of legal education, London, 1881; called to the bar, Middle Temple, Trin., 1882; acting puisne judge, Leeward Islands, 1886; lieutenant-governor, ditto, 1889.

BEAUMONT, W. H.—Ensign 75th (Stirling) regiment, August, 1870; lieutenant, Oct., 1871; served in the "Lancashire Rifle Brigade" in 1873; retired in 1875; private secretary to Colonel Milles, ministering the government of Natal, May, 1876; to Sir Benjamin Pine (lieutenant-governor), and to the executive council, July, 1873; acting M. Umlazi Division, Aug., 1874; clerk, col. sec., Jan., 1875; acting governor's clerk and to the executive council, Oct., 1875; R.M., Newcastle division, Feb., 1878; colonial commandant of District No. 1, Natal, at the commencement of the Zulu War, from 1st to 31st Jan., during which time he raised levies called the Newcastle Scouts." R.M., Inanda, May, 1887; E. City div., 1896.
RAVEN, ROBERT.—Minister of finance and culture and premier, British Columbia, Canada, 1877; leader of opposition, 1887.
ECKWITH, A. G.—Government engineer of New Brunswick, Fredericton, New Brunswick; member, Can. Soc. C.E.

ELL, ARCHIBALD G.—Assoc. M.I.C.E. Ed. at Cambridge and Uppingham; employed on surveys in Canada, 1887-8; and by Jamaica p.c.o., after sale of line, 1889-90; asst. to Messrs. Shaw and Hayter, 1891; asst. engr. P.W.D., Guiana, Oct., 1891; asst. col. engr., 1893, and 1896.
ELL, CHARLES D. E.—Examining officer of mines, Port Elizabeth.

ELL, EDWARD HORATIO.—Inspector of police, British Guiana, Mar., 1876; acting assistant-commissioner H.M.'s penal settlement, Massaruni, Feb., 1877; chief inspector of police, Str. Settts., Nov., 1877; ag. inspr. general, Jan. to April, 1878; J.P.; suptd. of police, Malacca, April, 1878; ditto, Singapore, Mar., 1893; ag. inspr.-gen. police, S.S., April, 1894 to Apr., 1895.

ELL, THE HON. SIR FRANCIS DILLON, C.M.G. (1881), KNIGHT BACH. (1873), C.B. (1869).—Formerly Speaker of the House of Representatives, New Zealand; agent general for the colony in London, 1881; resigned, 1891; was commissioner for New Zealand at the exhibition, 1886; delegate to colonial conference, 1887; apptd. under, of legation of honour, for services at Paris exhibition, 1889.

ELL, HARRY CHARLES PURVIS.—Educated at Eton College; writer, Ceylon civil service, 1873; police magistrate, Matale, Mar., 1876; land surveyor, Galle, Jan., 1878; office assistant, Colombo, to the government agent; senior province, Feb., 1878; first landing surveyor, customs, Colombo, May, 1883; assistant collector, customs, Galle, April, 1884; assistant assistant and additional district judge, Kegalla, June, 1885; acting second assistant to colonial secretary, May, 1886; ag. landing surveyor, Colombo, May, dist. judge, Ratnapura, November, 1888; to Kegalla, July, 1889; archaeological commissioner, Dec., 1895.

ELL, H. H. J., F.R.G.S.—Educated in Paris; served Col. Service in May, 1882, as 3rd clerk, in chief's office, Barbados; inland revenue, St. Andrew's, Grenada, 1883; supervisor of towns, Gold Coast, Jan., 1890; travelling and sporting supervisor, July, 1890; seur., asst. treasurer, Gold Coast, Aug., 1891; dist. commr., sheriff, Accra, May, 1892; ag. col. treas. and member ex. and leg. councils, Dec. 1893 to Feb. 1894; is author of "Obeah: Witchcraft in the West Indies," "A Witch's Legacy," "Geography of the Gold Coast," &c.; treasurer and rec.-gen., Bahamas, 1894.
ELL, HUGH HOWARD.—Ed., Abdn. Gram. Sch., and Lond. Univ.; solicitor, supreme court, 1882; dist. commr., Gold Coast, May, 1892.
ELL, THE HON. JAMES.—Minister without portfolio, Victoria, Feb., 1886; minister of defence, 1888-90.

ELL, VALENTINE G., M.I.C.E.—Chief resident engineer, Jamaica Government Railway, 1880-86; ag. director of public works, Aug., 1886; confirmed Mar., 1887; ex officio member leg. comm.; member of privy comm., Aug., 1890.

ELL, WILLIAM.—Clerk, real property office, Brisbane, Jan., 1863; chief clerk, supreme court, Nov., 1873, and also deputy registrar, July, 1876; registrar, Vice-Admiralty Court, Nov., 1877; registrar and probate supreme court, registrar and master in lunacy, &c., Oct., 1882; also Queen's coroner and attorney, July, 1887; is a commissioner for affidavits, a solicitor of the supreme court, &c.

BELLAIRS, MAJ.-GEN. SIR WILLIAM, C.B. (1878), K.C.M.G. (1882).—Entered the army, 1846; adjutant, 1851; captain, 1854; major, 1855; lieutenant-col., 1865; colonel, 1873; brig.-general, 1881. Served with the 49th Regiment, and on the staff of the army throughout the Crimean campaign, being present at the siege and fall of Sebastopol, medal with three clasps, Turkish medal, 5th class of Medjidie, knight of legion of honour; served throughout the Kafir campaign, 1877-8, when he commanded the combined forces on the eastern frontier; also throughout the Zulu campaign, 1879. Has also acted as inspector-general of police, Barbados, 1857-8; local inspector of army schools, 1868-73; colonial secretary, Gibraltar, Aug. to Nov., 1872; administrator of Natal April to May, 1880; member of executive council, Transvaal, 1880-1; commander of the forces at Pretoria in the Transvaal, 1880-81; administrator of Transvaal, April to June, 1881.

BELLAMY, CHAS. VINCENT, Assoc. M.I.C.E. Ed., Plymouth Coll.; Queen's Prizeman, S. Kensington, 1885; dist. engr., P. W. D., Ceylon, 1889; 2nd financial asst., Colombo, 1889; dist. engr., Pallai, Jan., 1890; ditto Vavuniya Apl., 1890; ditto Jaffna, Jan., 1891; exec. engr., Victoria Bridge, Colombo, Sept., 1893; col. engr. and surveyor-gen., Dominica, Nov., 1895.

BELFIELD, HENRY CONWAY.—Ed., Rugby and Oriel coll., Oxon.; B.A., 1877; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1880; went western circuit; mag., coll. of ld. rev., and inspr. of schools, Selangor, 1884; ag. chief mag. and commr. of ld., 1884, confirmed 1888; ag. Br. resdt., June and July, 1889; senr. mag., Perak, 1891; ag. secy. to Govt., Perak, July, 1891, to Jan., 1893; commr. of lands, Federated Malay States, July, 1896.

BELMORE, EARL OF, K.C.M.G. (1872), G.C.M.G. (1890). Somerset Richard Lowry Corry, fourth Earl and Viscount Belmore of the county Fermanagh, Belmore, Viscount and Baron Belmore, of Castle Coole, co. Fermanagh, in the peerage of Ireland, of which he is a representative peer. Born 9th April, 1835; graduated M.A. (hon.) at Cambridge 1856; elected a representative peer for Ireland, Jan., 1857; was Under Secretary of State for Home Department in Lord Derby's third administration July, 1866; Privy Councillor (Ireland), 1867; governor of New South Wales, Aug., 1867; resigned 1872; served as one of the Lord Justices of Ireland, 1885; and again 1886.

BELSTEAD, FRANCIS.—Assistant superintendent Convict Department, Norfolk Island, Aug., 1850; clerk of petty sessions, Westbury, Tasmania, May, 1858; clerk of municipal council, Dec., 1863; commissioner of mines and gold fields, and magistrate and coroner, Launceston, Feb., 1883; secretary and chief commissioner of mines and gold fields for Tasmania, Feb., 1886.
BENJAMIN, ST. BENJAMIN, KT. BACH. (1889).—Three years Mayor of Melbourne.
BENNETT, C. K.—Tempy outdoor offr., S. Leone, July, 1887; warehouseman, 1890; chief clk. of customs, Niger Coast Protectorate, 1891.

BENNETT, JOHN LIGHT.—Ed. Bourton Academy, near Bath, Somerset; joined London Police, Scotland Yard, 1879; warrant officer, Southwark Police Court, 1883; sergt. in charge of Holloway Police Stat., 1887; ag. inspr. 1888; senr. inspr. police, Gibraltar, 1889; chief of police, Gibraltar, 1895; is also supervisor of markets, and admnr. of the Aliens Order in Council.
BENNETT, JAMES FITZMAURICE—3rd clerk, bonded warehouse, British Guiana, June, 1873; 1st clerk, Dec., 1873; 5th clerk, customs, Oct., 1875; 4th clerk, 1879; 2nd class customs officer, 1886.

BENNETT, HENRY JAMES LEE.—M.R.C.S., Eng., 1878; L.R.C.P. and L.M., Edin., 1878; medical officer, Grenada, 1882; late house-surgeon, General Infirmary, Dewsbury, Yorks.; junior resident medical officer, Royal Albert Hospital and Eye Infirmary, Devonport, Devon; member British Medical Association; J.P., 1890.

BENNETT, SAMUEL MACKENZIE.—Financial clerk, Immig. Dept., Jamaica, Nov., 1876; chief clerk, Education Dept., 1882; supervisor of customs, Gold Coast, 1885; acted as dist. commr. at Axim, Saltpond, Anamaboe, and Volta River; assist. col. treas., Sierra Leone, 1888; J.P.; acted as inspr.-gen. of police and col. treasurer, 1889; as treas., 1890, 1891, 1892, and 1893; and as C.C., British Sherbro, 1890 and 1891; M.I.C. and mem. ex. coun. and bd. of education; ag. D.C., Sherbro, June, 1893 to Jan., 1894; ag. judge et al. requests and pol. mag., Freetown, also ag. col. treas., Oct., 1894, to June, 1895.
BENNETT, THOMAS.—Speaker, House of Assembly, Newfoundland, 1869; dist. judge, 1873.

BENNETT, WILLIAM HART.—Appointed after competitive examination, a clerk of the lower division, and assigned to the colonial office, 21st Nov., 1878; chief clerk, chief secretary's office, Cyprus, Jan., 1884; acting assistant to chief secretary, Aug., 1886; acted as chief secy. July to Oct., 1893; ag. commisr. Papho, May to July, 1894; asst. secy. to Govt. June, 1895; ag. chief secy. and member exec. coun. July, 1895.
BENT, THE HON. THOMAS.—Commissioner of railways, Victoria, 9th July, 1881; also vice-president of board of land and works; afterwards speaker of legislative assembly.

BERESFORD, M. H. DE LA POER.—Revenue officer, Leeward District, St. Vincent, Oct., 1883; acted as police magistrate and coroner, Windward District, Dec., 1884; confidential clerk and clerk of councils, Grenada, Jan., 1885, and in conjunction therewith private secretary to governor, Sir Walter J. Sendall, K.C.M.G., Nov., 1887; acting inspr. of prisons, Jan. to Mar., 1889; clerk of councils, and chief clerk to governor, Windward Islands, Nov., 1889.

BERKELEY, SIR GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1881), C.M.G. (1874).—Appointed (1845) colonial secretary and controller of customs, Honduras; administered the government of Dominica from April, 1861, to Dec., 1861; lieutenant-governor of St. Vincent, April, 1864; acting administrator, Laos, Dec., 1872; governor, West Africa Settlements, 1873; governor, Leeward Islands, 1874; retired, 1881.

BERKELEY, SIR HENRY SPENCER R. BACH.—Called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1873; ag. attorney-general of the Leeward Islands, Aug., 1877; solicitor-general, Leeward Islands, June, 1878; colonial secretary, Leeward Islands (acting) 1883; attorney-general, Feb., 1884; ag. chief justice, and chief judicial commissioner Western Pacific, May, 1887, to July, 1888; high commissioner for W. P., Dec., 1887, to Feb., 1888; chief justice of Fiji and chief commissioner for W. P., Feb., 1889; administered the government of on several occasions.

BERKELEY, HUBERT.—Ed. at the Great School, Edgbaston, and H.M.S. "Conway" shipman, R.N.R., 15th Dec., 1881; served H.M.S. "President" and "Trincomalee"; delivered to Sir T. Sidgreaves, chief justice, S.S., 1883; appointed to S.S. police, June, 1884; suptld. of Penghulus Perak, Jan., 1889; in charge of Stiawan District, Nov., 1889; magistrate collector, Upper Perak, May, 1891.

BERKELEY, JOHN HART HARDYMAN—sign, 83rd Regt., Sept., 1869; lieut. and adj., June, 1871; A.D.C. to Sir William Cairns, 1877; A.D.C. and private secretary to Sir Mark O'Connell, 1877; A.D.C. to Sir Arthur Keppel, governor of Queensland, 1877-78; member legislative Council, Nevis, and general legislative council, Leeward Island, May, 1882; elected president of latter council May, 1882, and again 1883 and 1886; nominated member legislative council of St. Kitts, Feb., 1883; member executive council, June, 1883.

BERKELEY, MAURICE J.—Barrister-at-Law (Lincoln's Inn); clerk to the chief justice, Barbados, 1878-83; acting police magistrate, Windward District, Tobago, April to Dec., 1883; acting inland revenue officer, District "C," Barbados, on several occasions; confirmed as acting police magistrate, District "C," Barbados, 1883-84 and 1885-6; acting police magistrate, Windward District, St. Vincent, July, 1884; pol. mag. and petty debt ct. judge, dist. "A," 1884.

BERNASKO, P. W.—Clerk (temp.) col. secy., G. Coast, 1882; dep. regnr. and interpr. S. A., 1886; regnr. and interpr. Divn. Ct. Cape, 1889; also regnr. of deeds, West Prov.; 1st regnr., 1893; asst. sec. to commiss. of enquiry into native cts., Aug., 1894; 2nd lieut. G. C. V., 1894.

BERNAYS, LEWIS ADOLPHUS, C.M.G., C.F.L.S., F.R.G.S., and corresponding member of various scientific societies.—Educated at King's Coll. and St. Thomas' Hospital, and was assistant to Parliament in N.S. Wales, 1853-9; clerk to Legislative Assembly, Queensland, 1859; is secretary of the Brisbane Water Supply, and lecturer on various public institutions.

BERRIDGE, T. PROBYN.—Member of assembly, St. Christopher, from 1843 till 1851, when he was appointed clerk to the legislative council; Appointed to Prince Alfred when His Royal Highness visited St. Christopher; appointed to the magistracy, 1862; one of the clerks of the general legislative council of the Leeward Islands, 1872; acting master, St. Christopher, 1872; commissioner of cemeteries, and member of the Board of the Cunningham Hospital, 1886.

ERRINGTON, ARTHUR T. D.—Educated at Ton College and Christ Church, Oxford; B.A., called Inner Temple, 1879; Oxford circuit; and D.L. for Monmouthshire; private secretary to Sir H. Bulwer in Cyprus, Oct., 1889, and Sir Frederick Dickson in the Straits Settlements, May, 1890; acting chief magistrate, Selangor, Nov., 1890; confirmed 1891; senr. mag., July, 1896.

ELKYY, THE HON. SIR GRAHAM, K.C.M.G. (1856).—Member of the Victorian Legislative Assembly, 1873-75; formed his first administration, taking office as chief secretary and treasurer; resigned; premier in 1877; in Dec., 1878, proceeded to India to procure a settlement of the deadlock between the two Houses of Legislature; chief secretary and postmaster general in Mr. Service's ministry, 1883; agent-general for Victoria, 1886-87; delegate to the Colonial Conference, 1887; adj. commdr. of legion of honour for services at Paris Exhibition, 1889; treasurer in Shiel's ministry in 1892; speaker of assembly, 1894.
ERY, JOHN.—Entered survey department, W.M., 1866; survey department, Fiji, 1877; surveyor-general, 1879; commissioner of works and surveys, 1882; member of executive and legislative council, 1883.

ETTRAM, LOUIS J.—Educated Victoria College, Jersey, 1870-7; appointed to exchequer and revenue department, March, 1878; assistant auditor, Cape Colony, Oct., 1884; acting auditor, 1886-87; auditor, 1888. Prepared scheme for accounts introduced into Gold Coast and Nigeria, 1886; auditor of Windward Islands, 1893; Neeward Is., 1896; ditto, Jamaica, 1897.

ETS, H. N. DUVERGER, C.M.G. (1881).—Settler at Flacq, Mauritius, April, 1862; clerk to magistrate of Port Louis, May, 1854; acting magistrate and stipendiary magistrate at Black River, 1856; at Grand Port, March, 1857; district magistrate at Flacq, March, 1858; acting protector of immigrants, May, 1859, and confirmed July, 1861; member of the legislative council, 1867; chairman, law commission, 1869; census commissioner, 1871; acting treasurer and collector of local revenues, October, 1873; member of executive council, October, 1873; acting receiver-general, January, 1874; confirmed as such, 1877; has acted several occasions as colonial secretary, and for a short time administered the government of the colony; retired, 1889.
EKNELL, H. J.—Police magistrate, Kingston, Jamaica, Mar., 1860; acting judge, eastern circuit, Oct., 1882; R.M., St. Catherine, April, 1883.
EKNELL, W. A.—Ch. clk. col. sec. office, Singapore, Sep., 1879; auditor, Penang, July, 1888.

HEDULPH, GEN. SIR ROBERT, R.A., K.C.M.G. (1886), K.C.M.G. (1886), C.B.—Born in 1856; appointed to Royal Artillery, 1856; served in Crimean Campaign, 1854-56; Indian Mutiny campaign, 1857-59; China War, 1860. Staff Services.—Deputy assistant adjutant-general in India, 1860; military secretary in China, 1860-61; deputy secretary, Madras, 1861-65; deputy assistant quartermaster-general, Woolwich, 1868-71; aide-de-camp to Mr. Cardwell when secretary of state for war, 1871-73; assistant adjutant-general, India Office, 1873-78; special service, Cyprus, 1878; commanding the troops, Cyprus, 1879; was assistant boundary commissioner for the Parliamentary Reform Act of 1867. Was appointed, 1879, H.M.'s Commissioner for arranging payment to be made to the Porte under the lex to the Convention of 4th June, 1878; and High Commissioner for Cyprus, 1879; inspector general of recruiting, 1886; director general of military education, 1st Mar., 1888; quartermaster-general, Jan., 1893; governor and commander-in-chief, Gibraltar, Oct., 1893.

BIGGS, JOHN W. FORBES.—Lieut. 109th regt., 1871; enlisted in Natal mounted police, 1879; 2nd clerk to R.M., Weenen County, Natal, 8 May, 1882; clk. to inspr. of prisons, 1893; clk. to R. M. Estcourt, 1894.

BIGGS, REV. LOUIS COUTIER.—Ordained deacon 1864, priest 1866; B.A. (3rd class in Lit. Hum.) of St. Edmund Hall, Oxford, 1863; M.A. 1866; curate of Grendon Northants, 1864; rector of Parracombe, North Devon, 1868; Chickencell, Dorset, 1870; chaplain of Malacca, 1874; of Penang, 1885.

BIRCH, SIR ARTHUR N., K.C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G. (1875).—Clerk in the colonial office, Feb., 1855; promoted to 3rd class, Oct., 1859; assistant private secretary to Sir E. Bulwer Lyttelton, 1858; private secretary to Mr. Chichester Fortescue, from Sept., 1859, until Feb., 1864, when he was permitted, while still remaining on the establishment of the colonial office, to accept the appointment of colonial secretary of British Columbia; was senior member of the executive council, and ex officio clerk of the same; also president of the legislative council; was administrator of the government of the colony from Sept., 1865, to Nov., 1866, promoted to be assistant senior clerk, colonial office, July 5, 1866; resumed his duties in colonial office in September, 1867; acting lieut.-governor of Penang and Province of Wellesley, Feb., 1871; returned to England, July, 1872; 1st class clerk, colonial office, 30th September, 1872; colonial secretary, Ceylon, June, 1873; administered the government during the spring and summer of 1874 and 1875-6; lieut.-governor, Ceylon, 1876; resigned, June, 1878; is agent of the Western Branch of the Bank of England, Burlington Gardens; executive commissioner for Ceylon at the Colonial and Indian Exhibition, 1886.

BIRCH, ERNEST WOODFORD.—Ed. Harrow; cadet, S.S., Jan., 1876; employed in the colonial office, Downing street, to July, 1878, secretary to the police commission, 1879; passed final examination in Malay, Aug., 1880; J.P. and a magistrate, Malacca; took charge Malacca land office, Feb., 1881; acting collector of land revenue, Singapore, 1881, 2nd assistant colonial secretary, 1882; on special mission in H.M.S. "Espero," to report on the Cocos-Keeling Islands, 1883; magistrate and collector of land revenue, Malacca, 1885; ag. br. resident, Selangor, May, 1892, to Jan., 1893; secy. to govt., Perak, July, 1893; ag. resdt., Perak, Sept., 1895, to July, 1896.

BIRCH, JAMES KORTRIGHT.—Cadet, Str. Sett., 1872; passed final examination in Malay, Dec., 1873; assistant magistrate, coroner, and collector land revenue, P.W., Sept., 1874; collector land revenue, Penang and P.W., and settlement officer Trans Keian, Sept., 1877; collector of land revenue, Singapore, June, 1880; acting pol. mag. and comsr., et al. requests, P.W., Dec., 1881; confirmed, May, 1882; sen. dist. officer, P.W., 1888; 1st mag. and comsr., et al. requests, and supt. of prisons, Penang, June, 1890; ag. treasurer, S.S., Feb., 1894; 1st mag. Singapore, April, 1895, but continued to act as treas.

BIRD, THE HON. R. STAFFORD.—Has represented Franklin District in House of Assembly, Tasmania, since 1882; Member of Commissions on Education and Lunatic Asylums, 1883; treasurer of the colony in the Eysh Government, 1887-92.

BIRD, CHRISTOPHER.—Clerk. Engineer's Department, Natal, Jan., 1874; 2nd clerk, colonial secretary's office, Jan., 1880; 1st clerk, Aug., 1881; clerk, Executive Council, April, 1883, to July, 1885; acting asst. col. sec., April, 1884, to 1886, J.P., 1887; asst. col. secy., Jan., 1888; apptd. principal under sec. on establishment of responsible govt., Oct., 1893; member of civil service bd., 1894.
BLACK, G. G.—Ed. High Sch., Glasgow; Univ. Coll. Sch.; London and Glasgow Univ.; clk., audit dept., W. Australia, 1892; audit examiner, 1894; chief clk., P. W. Dept., 1896.

BLACK, THOMAS Gordon DALLAS.—Served in 2nd Dragoons, "Scots Greys," 1858-68. Ensign 4th W. I. Regiment, Oct., 1868; transferred to 3rd W. I. Regiment, April, 1869; sub-inspector, Jamaica constabulary, Feb., 1870; acting inspector, Aug., 1871; 3rd class inspector (and in charge of detective department), Oct., 1872; 1st class inspector, temporarily, Feb., 1885; 2nd class inspector, Dec., 1875; 1st class ditto, Aug., 1886.

BLACKMORE, EDWIN GORDON.—Educated at King Edward VI Grammar School, Bath; served with the Taranaki Rifle Volunteers in the New Zealand war, 1863-64, and was present, in reserve at the action of Poutoko, 2nd Oct., 1863, and at the storming and capture of the rebel Maori strongholds at Ahualu and Kaitake, March, 1864 (medal); parliamentary librarian to the legislature of South Australia, Oct., 1864; clerk assistant and sergeant-at-arms, House of Assembly, Dec., 1869; clerk of the House of Assembly, May, 1886; clerk Leg. Coun., and clerk of Parls., May, 1887; author of "The Decisions of Mr. Speaker Denison and Mr. Speaker Brand, on Points of Order, Rules of Debate, and the General Practice of the House of Commons, 1857-84." "The Decisions of Mr. Speaker Peel, 1884-90," "Manual of the Practice, Procedure, and Usage of the House of Assembly of S.A." "Manual of the Practice, Procedure, and Usage of the Legislative Council of S.A."; and the "Law of the Constitution of S.A."

BLACKWELL, Lionel Norton.—2nd lieut. 4th Bn. King's Shropshire L.I., Nov., 1888; lieutenant, Sept., 1889; transferred to Thames Mil. Divsn., R.E., Mar., 1894; instructor of musketry, Apr., 1895; asst. inspr., Lagos Housa Force, Nov., 1895; ag. dist. commiss., Oct., 1896.

BLAGDEN, CHARLES OTTO.—Ed. at Dulwich Coll. and Corpus, Oxford (scholar), B.A., 1887 (1st class, final classics); cadet, Straits, Nov., 1888; passed final exam. in Malay, Feb., 1890; ag. dist. offr. South Malacca June, 1890; 3rd mag., Penang, Mar., 1892; dist. offr. S. Malacca, 1893; ag. collr., ld. rev. and officer in charge of treas., Malacca, Dec., 1893 to Mar., 1894; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Nov., 1895; ag. 2nd mag. Singapore, Nov., 1895.
BLAINE, Sir Charles Frederick, Kt. Bach. (1889).—Delegate for Cape Colony to South African Customs Union Conference, 1889.
BLAIR, A.G.—Member for York in New Brunswick House of Assembly; premier and attorney-general from 1883 to 1896; minister of rly. and canals in Mr. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.

BLAIR, WM.—Sec. to Bd. of Edn., and inspr. of schools, Bahamas, 1868; inspr. of schools, Ceylon, 1879; ag. dir. of edn. 1882 and 1884; inspr. of schools, Br. Guiana, 190; is author of a school arithmetic in English, Sinhalese, and Tamil.

BLAKE, The Hon. EDWARD, Q.C. M.P.—Born 1833; called to the bar of Upper Canada, 1836; created a Q.C., 1864; entered Canadian parliament 1867, and has also sat in Ontario assembly; was prime minister of Ontario from 20th Dec., 1871, till 25th Oct., 1872, when he resigned; sworn to the privy council, 7th Nov., 1873, but did not hold office; re-entered the cabinet, 19th May, 1875, as minister of justice, a position he exchanged in Sept., 1877, for that of president of the council; retired from the cabinet, 31st Jan., 1878; declined the chancellorship of Ontario, Dec., 1869, also the chief justiceship of the supreme court of the Dominion, May, 1873, and the K.C.M.G. in May, 1877; is chancellor of the University of Toronto, and President of the Law Society of Upper Canada; leader of the Liberal party in Canada, 1880-7; elected to Eng. Parliament, 1892.

BLAKE, ERNEST EDWARD.—Appointed to clerkship in the colonial office, 4th July, 1875; after a competitive examination: 1st junior clerk, 20th Nov., 1869; 2nd class clerk, 30th Sept., 1875; assist. priv. sec. to the Earl of Kimberley, 1st Oct., 1872; priv. sec., 27th Jan., 1874; clerk and head of the general department, 1st May, 1879; crown agent for the colonies, 1st Jan., 1881.

BLAKE, Sir Henry Arthur K.C.M.G., C.M.G. (1887), F.R.G.S.—Cadet, Royal Irish Constabulary, Feb., 1859; sub-inspector, Mar., 1860; appointed resident magistrate, Feb., 1876; in 1882, was one of the five special resident magistrates selected by Government to carry out measures for the pacification of a large portion of Ireland; appointed governor of the Bahamas, Jan., 1884; Newfoundland, 1887; Queensland, Nov., 1888, but did not take up appointment; governor, Jamaica, Dec., 1888.

BLAKENEY, WILLIAM THEOPHILUS.—Stood for office at Sydney, Feb., 1856; transferred to clerical office, Brisbane, Feb., 1860; under sheriff, May, 1862; deputy regis.-gen., Dec., 1865; now regis.-gen., comrs. of stamps, regis. of patents, designs, and trade marks, and of friendly societies.

BLAKEWAY, Capt. JOHN EDWARD, C.H.—St. Peter's Coll., Camb.; lieut. 4th Batt. Essex Regt., Sept., 1886; capt., Feb., 1895; qualified as commiss. in the line, Sept., 1889; Hythe certificate, 1894; Aldershot and Shoeburyness, 1895; tactics and military topography, 1894; asst. int. S. Leone frontier police, Sept., 1895; is a native of the colony.

BLANCHARD, VICTOR P.—Clerk, Govt. of Roseau, Dominica, 1889; clerk to valuers, commissioners, and commissioners of assessed taxes, Feb., 1895; ag. chief outdoor officer, treaty and quarantine officer, July, 1895 to 1896; ag. sec. quarantine, ibid., 1896.
BLANCHET, J., Q.C.—Provincial secretary and registrar, Quebec; puisne judge, Quebec Bench divn., supreme ct., Quebec, 1891.

BLAND, ROBERT NORMAN.—Ed. St. Paul School and Cheltenham Coll., B.A. Trin. Coll., Duh.; Cadet, Straits S., Oct., 1882; passed out, Malay, May, 1884; collector and magistrate, Memanti, June, 1886; district officer southern district, P. W., 1888; collr. of land rev., Penang, 1889; ditto, Singapore, 1890; offr. in charge of Sunjet Ujong, Dec. 1894; ditto, Negri Seman, Jan. to April, 1895; ag. offr. assignee of debts, Singapore, June, 1896.

BLISSETT, HENRY FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1877)—Clerk, Royal Ordnance Factories, at Portsea, Jan., 1867; assistant commissary, African Commissariat, Aug., 1869; private secretary, 1870-1; administrator of the Gold Coast, and clerk of council, 1872; served throughout the Ashanti Expedition, 1873-4, as control officer with Capt. Glover's force.

BOOTHBY, Josiah, C.M.G. (1878).—Clerk, colonial secretary's office, South Australia, 1853; clerk in audit office, 1854; chief clerk, 1856; chief clerk in chief secretary's office, 1859; also government statistician and superintendent of census, 1860; assistant secretary and government statistician, South Australia, 1866; and under-secretary and government statistician, 1868 to 1880; elected corresponding member of the Statistical Society, London, 1869; also trustee, savings bank, South Australia, 1869; a commissioner for international exhibitions, 1872; joint editor of a work "South Australia; its History, Resources, and Productions," published by authority of government, 1876; executive commissioner representing South Australia at the Paris Universal Exhibition of 1878; elected hon. member of Royal Geographical Societies of Berlin and Vienna, 1878.
BOOTHBY, William Robinson, B.A. (Lond.), C.M.G. (1892).—Sheriff and returning officer of the province of South Australia, 1854; also marshal of the court of vice-admiralty, 1862.
BORCHERDS, P. B., R. M., Tarka, Cape Colony, Aug., 1874; ditto, Stockenstrom, Aug., 1876—Sept., 1884; at Swellendam, Jan., 1886.

BORDEN, The Hon. Frederick William.—Born at Cornwallis, King's County, May 14, 1847; graduate of Harvard Medical School; has represented King's in the House of Commons since 1874, with the exception of one term, being defeated at the general elections of 1882; minister of Militia and Defence in Mr. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.

BOSISTO, Joseph, C.M.G. (1886).—Member Legislative Assembly, Victoria; President, Victorian Commission for the Colonial and Indian Exhibition 1886; an Executive Commissioner Centennial Exhibition, Melbourne, 1888.
BOSMAN, Johannes Jacobus.—Examiner of diagrams, surveyor general's office, Cape, Aug., 1892.

BOUCAUT, The Hon. James Penn.—Entered Parliament, South Australia, 1862. Held office in several ministries, as attorney-general, October, 1865, to March, 1866, and thence to May, 1867; as attorney-general and premier, and again as attorney-general, in 1872; as premier and commissioner of crown lands and public works, from June, 1875, to June, 1876; as premier and treasurer, from 26th October, 1877, to 25th September, 1878, when he accepted a seat on the bench of the Supreme Court.

BOURINOT, John George, M.A., LL.D., Lit.D., D.C.L., C.M.G. (1890).—Educated at Trinity College, Toronto. Officer of the Senate of Canada, 1868; clerk assistant of the House of Commons of Canada, 1873; honorary secretary of the royal society of Canada; presidt. of the Society, 1893-4, and then again secy.; clerk of the House in 1880; member of coun. of American Historical Assn., and of the Amer. Academy of Political and Social Science; hon. foreign memb. Amer. Antiquarian Soc.; vice-presdt. in Canada of Egypt. Explorn. Fund; is author of a large work on Parliamentary Procedure in Canada, of a manual on the Constitutional History of Canada, and of other works on the history and constitution of Canada, and of numerous essays relating to the intellectual and material progress of the dominion, which have appeared in the transactions of English and Amer. societies, Economic Association, and in the pages of "Blackwood," "Edinburgh," and the "Quarterly Review;" lecturer on political science in Trinity University, Toronto; hon. LL.D. from Queen's University, Kingston, and hon. D.C.L. from Trinity University, Toronto, King's Coll. N.S., Bishop's Coll., Quebec; Docteur-ès-Lettres, from Laval Univ., Quebec.

BOURNE, HUGH CLARENCE, M.A. (Balliol College, Oxford).—Assist. secretary, Charity Organization Society, 1881-84; called to the bar (Inner Temple), 1884; registrar-general and examiner of titles, Trinidad, 1893; has acted in addition at various times as regr. sup. ct. and marshal as dep. judge of dist.-ct. and as S.J.P. Pt. of Spain; member of rlyv. and rds. commsn., 1894; as judge sup. ct., 1896.
BOURNE, J. O.—Registrar of titles, Queensland, 1890.

BOVELL, HENRY ALLEYN.—Gilchrist scholar (Lond. Univ.), Jan., 1873; LL.B. (Lond.), Jan., 1877, with second class honours in jurisprudence and Roman law; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1876; acted as solicitor-general, as escheator-general and attorney-general of Barbados, 1881 and 1882; solicitor-general and escheator-general of Barbados, and J.P., Mar., 1882; M.L.C., July, 1883; member of commission to revise laws of Barbados, Mar., 1886; chancellor of the diocese, 1884; acting attorney-general, June, 1884, to July, 1885; atty-gen., Oct., 1886; atty-gen. Br. Guiana, 1896.

BOVELL, JOHN R., F.L.S., F.C.S.—Parochial treas. and collr. of rates, parish of St. John, Barbados, April, 1882; supted. Reformatory and Industrial School, April, 1883; member local committee Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1885; suptd. Botan. Stan., 1886; went to Antigua 1890 to start Skerrett's Training School; vice presdt. Windward dist. Agri. Soc. 1890; member of commns. to inquire into sugar cane borers, Jan., 1893; J.P., Mar., 1893; chairman Emig. Comtce., Apr., 1895.

BOWDLEK, EDWARD.—Articled to city surveyor and architect, York, 1880; employed temporarily on civil staff, R.E., on fortifications on the Humber, July, 1855; super-numerary clerk of works, R.E., to superintend works at the Royal Military College and the erection of the Royal Staff College, Sandhurst, April, 1859; assistant surveyor-general and civil engineer, Mauritius, June, 1862; acting surveyor-general, 1868-9; assistant surveyor-general, Hong Kong, July, 1874; acting surveyor-general, April, 1878; J.P., Dec., 1878, acting surveyor-general, 1880-2; special engineer, Praya Reclamation Works, 1889.

BOWELL, THE HON. SIR MACKENZIE, K.C.M.G., 1895.—Emigrated from Suffolk to Canada in 1833; well-known in Canada as a journalist; has sat in Canadian House of Commons since 1867; sworn of the privy council, 19th Oct., 1878, and appointed minister of customs; minister of militia and defence, 1892; minister of trade and commerce, Dec., 1892; on special mission to Australia in connection with the development of inter-colonial trade, 1893; president of Colonial Conference at Ottawa, 1894; Premier of Canada and Vice-President of Council, 1894-96.

BOWEN, ADDERLEY FRED.—Financial asst. police dept., Penang, April, 1890; ag. asst. offl. assignee and regr. of deeds, Penang, Mar., 1895; sheriff and dep. regr. sup. ct., Penang, Feb., 1896; ag. asst. audr. gen., Mar., 1896.

BOWEN, CUTHBERT, M.A., M.D.—Ed. Harrison Coll. and Codrington Coll., Barbados; B.A. (Hon. classic and lit.), Dux., 1882; super. clk. P.O., Barbados, 1883; asst. master Cumbermere school, 1884; M.D., 1887; physician to Children's Hosp., Philadelphia, U.S.A., 1888; junr. resurg. Gen. Hosp., Bridgeton, Barbados, 1889; senr. ditto, Feb., 1890.
BOWEN, ERNEST F. S.—Entered Colonial Service, Feb., 1892; supt. Pub. Wks. Barbados, Feb., 1895.

BOWEN, RIGHT HON. SIR GEORGE FERGUSON (G.C.M.G., 1860), C.M.G. in 1855, K.C.M.G. in 1856, Hon. D.C.L. (Oxon.), Hon. L.L.D. (Camb.), Charterhouse, and Trin. Coll., Ox.; scholar in 1st class in classics, 1844; fellow of Brasenose College, and member of Lincoln's Inn; chief secretary to the government of the Ionian Islands 1854-59; first governor of Queensland, 1859-60; governor of New Zealand, 1868-73; of Victoria, 1873-79; of Mauritius, 1879-83; of Hong Kong, 1883; retired 1887. Is author of "Ithaca" in "Mount Athos, Thessaly, and Epirus," &c., member of the privy council, 1886; commissioner for delimitation of electoral districts in N.W. Jan., 1888.

BOWEN, G. F.—Temporary clerk, receiver-general's office, Trinidad, Feb., 1857; was Diego Martin Ward Union, Sept., 1859; 4th receiver-general's office, Oct., 1873; 3rd July, 1874; 2nd clerk, Feb., 1877; chief clerk accountant, Jan., 1884.

BOWER, SIR GRAHAM JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1860), C.M.G. (1886).—Retired commander, R.N.; retired navy, 1861, and served on Mediterranean, East Coast of Africa, East Indian, Home, and Australian stations, until 1880, when he was appointed private secretary to Sir Hercules Robinson, governor, Cape of Good Hope, and high commissioner for South Africa; imperial secretary and accountant to the high commissioner, April, 1884.

BOWHILL, JAMES ALLEN PURSER.—Inspector of schools, Tobago, Sept., 1894; provisional revenue officer and keeper of the spirit ward, Nov., 1884; ag. inland revenue officer, April, 1885; ag. auditor and registrar, 8th June, 1885.

BOWKER, COL. JAMES HENRY, F.R.S., F.Z.S., F.S.St., Lond. (Gold Medalist).—Served in Kaffir war of 1846 and 1847, and in Kaffir War of 1851; inspector, frontier armed and mounted police, 1855; served in the Transkei expedition, 1858, and remained in command until the withdrawal of the police in 1865; served in expeditions to Basutoland, 1868, and was high commissioner's agent during the final annexation of Basutoland to the Colony; commandant of the frontier armed and mounted police, 1870; commanded expedition to Hope Town and the Diamond Fields, 1871; one of the three commissioners for the administration of government at the Diamond Fields, and for some time chief commissioner; commanded expedition to Tembuland, 1875; governor's agent, British Basutoland, 1877; retd. 1878 with hon. rank of colonel, J.P. for the Cape Colony; one of the commissioners for Natal for the Indian and Colonial Exhibition.
