BOWREY, J. J., F.C.S., F.I.C., &c.—Analytical chemist, medical department, Jamaica, June, 1879; also curator of the Institute of Jamaica.

BOYCE, ERNEST PEDDER.—Superintendent-clerk, G.P.O., Barbados, June, 1878; notarial and colonial secretary's office, Apr., 1879; sent to commissioners to inquire into the working of the G.P.O., 1879; chief clerk, public library, acting librn., Sept., 1882; asst. clk., petty debts court of St. Michael's, Nov., 1882; acting chief clk., May, 1883, to June, 1884; indl. rev. offl., 1884.
BOYES, E. TH.—Collector and inspector of customs, Hobart, Tasmania, 1st Mar., 1883.
BOYES, LORENZO.—Civil commissioner and resident magistrate, Namakualand division, Cape Good Hope, 1st Dec., 1860; C.C. and R.M. Somerset, 1st July, 1878.
BOYES, R. C. R.—Civil commissioner and resident magistrate of Caledon division, Cape Colony.
WAS CLERK TO RESIDENT MAGISTRATE ALBANY, 1845 TO 1850 CLERK TO THE CIVIL COMMISSIONER OF CRADOCK, JUNE, 1850; CLERK TO DITTO, JANUARY, DEC., 1855.

COYLE, CAVEYSH, C.M.G. (1889).—Educated at Charterhouse; was assistant clerk of seats, principal registry, court of probate, 1869-74; district magistrate, Leeward Islands, Dec., 1879; magistrate and coroner, District E., and registrar-general, Dominica, Feb., 1880; member of legislative assembly, June, 1880; inspector of gaols and prisons, Nov., 1880; deputy-marshal, V.A. court, Jan., 1880; colonial secretary of Bermuda, member of legislative and executive councils, and registrar-general, 1882; col. secy., Gibraltar, 1888; is also chairman of hospital board, and member of board of health, crown land board, and Gavin's alum trust, and chairman of sanitary commission; awarded vellum certificate by Royal Humane Society, and received the special thanks of the Board of Trade, and certificate and medal from the Italian Government for services in connection with the wreck of the "Utopia" on Mar., 1891; colonial secy., Br. Guiana, 1894; dist. Govt., 1894, and 1895-6.
RABANT, EDWARD YEWD, C.M.G. (1879).—Member of the legislative assembly for East London, member of Good Hope; field commandant of Cape volunteers, and late of H.M.'s Cape Mounted Rifles.
RACE, T. E. D.—Dispenser N. P. Asylum, Bahamas, Sep., 1882; clerk police office, Aug., 1882; 2nd clk. col. sec. off., Sep., 1887; has acted several occasions as chief clk. and clk. bd. of wks.

RADBURY, F.—Joined the Straits service, 23 Oct., 1864, as chief officer of the "Pluto;" 17 Mar., 1865, deputy master-attendant, Singapore; 29 Aug., 1865, wright surveyor, member of the Pilot Board, registrar of boats; received thanks of Admiralty for services in connection with attack on Corfu, 1871; harbour master, Penang, 1886; J.P. and mag. of Police.

RADBURY, JOHN SWANWICK, R.A.—Ed. Chester Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxon, 1891; Goldsmiths Co. Exhib., 1892; Sevres Exh., 1894; 1st class, Class Mods., 1895; Ass. Lit. Human., 1895; 1st class Modern History, 1896; B.A., 1895; Librarian, Oxford University Society, 1895; 2nd class clk. Colonial Office, Oct., 1896.

ADDON, THE HON. SIR E. N. C., K.C.M.G.—Assistant commissioner, Santhial Perahs, India, 1857; supdt. of excise, Oudh; in addition to this appointment, was inspector-general of registration and superintendent of statistics for several years, and during several months secretary to financial commissioner; retired, Mar., 1877, and settled in Tasmanian; elected M.P. for West Devon, 1879, and frequently four times elected for same district; leader of opposition, 1886; minister of lands and mines and minister of education in Fysh administration, Mar., 1887; also member of the Federal Council of Australasia; agent-gen. for Tasmania, 1893; premier of Tasmania, 1894.

ADY, ALFRED BARTON, M.Inst.C.E., pupil afterwards chief draftsman, chief engrs', dep. eng. Y., rly., Manchester, Jan., 1872; chief to G. W. Stevenson, M.I.C.E., Part II., 1879; survr. Docking Union and Hunton Urban Sanitary Authority, Mar., 1881; survr., Maldon Sanitary Dist., Essex, 1882; asst. engr. rly., dept., Brisbane, Qnsld., 1885; asst. engr. of bridges (rlys.), 1887; engr. for bridges (main roads), P.W.D., Gold., June, 1889; Govt. architect and engr. for bridges combined, Sept., 1891.
BRAIN, ROBERT S.—Govt. printer, Victoria, Aug., 1887.

BRAITHWAITE, H.W.—Petroleum locker and assistant excise locker, San Fernando, Trinidad, 1873; chief clerk, sub-treasury, 1874; suptd. registrar of births, 1878, J.P.; elected municipal councillor, 1888.

BRAMSTON, JOHN, D.C.L., C.B. (1886).—Graduated B.A., at Balliol College, Oxford, 1854; elected fellow of All Souls', 1855; D.C.L., 1863; barrister, Middle Temple, 1857; went to Queensland, 1859, as private secretary to Sir George Bowen; resigned, 1861. Was member of the legislative council of Queensland, 1863 to 1869 and of the executive council, 1863 to 1866. While in England, in 1867, was assistant boundary commissioner for Devon and Cornwall, under the Reform Act of that year. Returned to Queensland, 1868, and became attorney-general, 3rd May, 1870; resigned, 31st Dec., 1873; represented the Burnett district in the legislative assembly of Queensland, April, 1871, to Dec., 1873; appointed attorney-general of Hong Kong, 1873; was ex-officio member of the legislative and executive councils; acted as judge of the supreme court of Hong Kong, Feb. to May, 1874; appointed an assist. under-secretary of state, Colonial Office, 30th June, 1876; employed on a mission to Berlin in connection with the Angra Pequena negotiations, July, 1886; registrar of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, Feb., 1892.
BRANCH, W. J., M.D.—Inspector of hospitals and medical officer, District No. 1, St. Kitts; is health officer, Bassetere, and M.L.C.

BRASSEY, LORD, 1st baron (created 1886), Sir Thomas BRASSEY, K.C.B., D.C.L. (Hon.) and M.A. (Oxon), called to the bar Lincoln's Inn, 1866; M.P. for Devonport, 1865, and for Hastings, 1868 to 1886; lieutenant commanding Royal Naval Art. Volrs., 1873; hon. commander, 1880; col. 2nd Cinque Ports Art. Volrs., 1891; dep.-lieut. and J.P. for Sussex; was civil lord of the Admiralty, 1880 to 1884, and secretary to the Admty., 1884-5; president of the Statistical Society, 1879-80; appointed a commander of the legion of honour, 1889; chairman of the royal commission on opium traffic, 1894-5; lord-in-waiting, 1893-5; governor of Victoria, 1895.

BRELAND, PASCAL.—Has been for many years member of the North West Council and of the Legislative Assembly of Manitoba. Is a half-breed, and exercises a great and useful influence in the N.W. Territories.

BRETT, MAJOR SIR WILFORD, K.C.M.G. (1864).—Entered the army as ensign in the 76th regiment, May, 1840; lieutenant, Jan., 1843, and captain, June, 1855; private secretary to Sir J. G. Le Marchant, governor of Malta, April, 1858; was also assistant military secretary to Sir J. G. Le Marchant, at Madras, 1865 to 1866.
BREW, SAM. II.—Ed. Wesleyan High School, Cape Coast; clerical asst. gov. off. G. Coast, Apr., 1887; junr. clk. Jan. 1891; 3rd clk. Jan., 1893.
BREWIN, ARTHUR WIMBOLT.—Ed. at Winchester; cadet, Hong Kong, 1888; passed cadet, Dec., 1890; ag. asst. regtr.-gen., May, 1891 to Mar., 1894; J.P., 1894; ag. asst. regtr. gen., 1895.

BREWSTER, EDWARD JOHN.—Educated at Brewood and Cheltenham; asst. dist. officer, Matang, Perak, 1878; asst. superint., Lower Perak, 1878; asst. mag., Krian, 1879; ditto, Salama, 1880; asst. dist. officer, Krian, 1883-4; ditto, Larut, 1885; ditto, Kinta, 1888; dist. officer, Krian, 1889; mag., Krian, 1889; state commissr. of lands, and regt. of mines, Jan., 1892; ag. state auditor, Ap., 1893; dist. mag., Lower Perak, Aug., 1893.
BRIGGS, N. F., B.A.—Judge of petty debt court, Barbados.

BRIGHT, CHARLES E., C.M.G. (1883).—A leading merchant in Victoria; has been chairman of the harbour trust and commissioner for the Melbourne Exhibition; was also commissioner for Victoria at some of the exhibitions in Europe.
BRISBANE, 3RD Bishop (Australia), 1859.

BRISTOWE, LINDSAY W.—Clerk, surveyor-general's office, British Honduras, 1880; in treas. and cust. dept., 1883-1884; secretary Crown Lands Board, 1884; qualified land surveyor, clerk registrar's and record office, clerk of the petty debt court, July, 1884; acted as clerk of the legislative council, 1885; as registrar V.A. Court, 1886; as provost marshal, 1886-7; as registrar and keeper of records, 1887-8-9-90; dist. commissr. Toledo dist., 1892; Belize dist., 1893; notary and commissr. of the sup. ct.; editor Br. Honduras handbook, 1888-93; dist. commissr. G. Coast, 1893; assigned to treas., April, 1894; ag. chief asst. treasurer, Aug., 1894, to Jan., 1895; commissr. Elmina, Oct., 1895, to July, 1896; commissr. Accra, July, 1896; attached to secretariat, Accra, Aug., 1896.

BROADICK, EDW. GRO.—Ed. at Sherborne Sch.; clk. lower divn. H.M.'s office of works, Nov., 1884; Cadet, S.S., 1887; passed Tamil final July, 1889; dist. offr., Nebong Tebal, P.W., June, 1890; passed in Malay, Jan., 1892; ag. 2nd mag. Penang, July, 1893; also ag. 2nd asst. prot. of immigrants, May, 1896.

BROCKMAN, EDW. LEWIS.—Cadet, S. S., 1886; passed in Malay, 1888; agt. supt. of education, Penang, 1888; 3rd Mag. Penang, June, 1890; ag. coll. of land rev., Penang, May, 1889; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Feb. to Oct., 1892; dist. offr. Bukit Mertajam, Mar., 1892; ag. coll. id. rev., Penang, July, 1895; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Nov., 1895; confirmed, June, 1896; also ag. collr. id. rev., Singapore, June, 1896.

BROOMHURST, HENRY WM. FRED. COTTINGHAM.—Educated at Cheltenham College; writer, Ceylon service, 1877; police magistrate, Matale, 1880; assistant to government agent N.W. Provinces, 1885; ditto, Kegalla, 1891; asst. gov't. agt., Kalutara, 1891.

BROOK, HERBERT ARTHUR.—Acting clerk police office, Nassau, May to Oct., 1874; librarian Nassau public library, reading room, and museum, Oct., 1875, to Oct., 1879; clerk police office, Oct., 1879, acting chief clerk col. sec. dept. May to Nov., 1881; and Oct. 1882; acting private secretary to Governor, Mar. to April, 1883; chief clerk, col. sec. dept., June, 1883; and clerk, board of public works; registrar of records, Sept., 1887; supt., Bahamas census, 1891; is a J.P., and member of board of education.
BROOKE, HIS HIGHNESS SR CHARLES JOHNSON, G.C.M.G. (1888).—Rajah of Sarawak.

BROOKING, J. S.—Entered the survey department, Western Australia, 1871; acting inspecting surveyor, 1874; confirmed, 1876; acted for surveyor-general and commissioner of Crown lands, 1881, 1884, 1885, and 1887; inspector of plans and surveys, 1885.

BROOKS, EDWARD PAKENHAM.—Cadet, R.N., Dec., 1862; sub-lieut., 1870; resigned, 1871; asst. district clerk, Grand Port, Mauritius, Feb., 1874; in July, 1874, special district and stipendiary magistrate to inquire into the treatment of labourers employed in the lesser depots of the colony; Feb., 1876, corresponding clerk, col. sec. office, Aug., 1879, joint district and assistant officer of the civil Status Riverina dep't.; Apr., 1880, ditto Savanne; Dec., 1881, ass't. pol. mag., &c., Falkland Islands; ass't. gov't., July, 1889, to Feb., 1890; retired 21st June, 1891; treasurer, Grenada, June, 1892; administered government, Grenada, in Sept., 1892.

BROOKS, G. H.—Clerk of customs, Antigua, June, 1868; warehousekeeper, Cape Coast castle, Aug., 1870; served as issuer during Ashanti campaign, Oct. 1873 to 1874; removed with quarter's staff to Accra, April, 1877; transferred back to Cape Coast, Oct., 1881, as clerk and house keeper.
BROOKS, J. M.—General storekeeper N.W. Government railways, Aug., 1879.

BROOME, WILLIAM.—Entered Natal civil service, 1875; clerk, executive council, and secret committee, 1878; chief clerk, att. general's office, 1879; advocate, supreme court, 1882; secretary, council of education, 1885; N. Newcastle division, 1889; acted several times assistant colonial secretary, 1886-88; J.P., for colony (1889); member of council of educ. in 1891; master and registrar, supreme court, 1891.
BROUGHTON, V. DELVES.—Deputy master Her Majesty's Mint, chief officer of the Melrose Branch, 8th Nov., 1877; was for many years clerk in the Treasury, Whitehall.
BROWN, C. CUTHBERT.—Acting chief book-keeper, treasury department, Gold Coast Colony, 1st March, 1876, confirmed 19th June, 1876.
BROWN, CHARLES GAGE, M.D., C.M.G. (1885).—Has been for many years medical adviser to Colonial Office.

BROWN, G. WILSON, M.A.—Entrance Emmanuel College, Cambridge; scholar, B.A. (Math. Tripos), 1855; M.A., 1881; ass't. master, Scotch college, Melbourne, Victoria, April, 1857; head master, national grammar school, Long, Jan., 1858; organising master and inspector of national schools, May, 1859; organising master, board of education, Sept., 1862; ass't. general, education department, Jan., 1873; secretary for public instruction, Mar., 1878; ass't. to the executive council, Victoria, April, 1880; Jan., 1887; retired 1893.
BROWN, H. V. L.—Government geologist South Australia, Dec., 1882.

BROWN, JOHN.—M.Inst. C.E.; assistant engineer, Cape, 1873; maintenance engineer, west system, 1882; resident engineer, western midland systems of railway, 1884; ass't. engineer-in-chief, June to Nov., 1888; chief test engineer (open lines) Dec., 1890.
BROWN, JOHN EDWARD, J.P., F.L.S., F.R.H.S.—Son of the late Dr. Jas. Brown, author of "The Foster;" conservator of forests, Aust., 1878; director-genl. of forests, N. S. W., 1890.

BROWN, JOHN JAMES.—Ed. Liverpool Collegiate Institution and Royal Coll., Med. volunteer colonial secretary's office, May, 1863; 2nd clerk to senior stipendiary magistrate, Aug., 1863; clerk to the chief judge, 1864; curator of vacant estates, Oct., 1874; agent before the Forest Lands Purchase Commission in conjunction with his office of col. Nov., 1881, till Dec., 1882, and alone from 1882 to Feb., 1884; is also marshal of the admiralty court since Feb., 1873; ass't. ree., Dec., 1888; ag. collr. of customs May to

BROWN, THE HON. N. J.—Member, House of Assembly, Tasmania, 1875; minister of lands and surveys, Oct., 1877, to Dec., 1878, and Dec., 1882 to 1883; was joint representative to Federation Convention at Sydney, 1883; and member of the Federal Council of Australia.

BROWN, RICHARD MYLES.—Ed. Liverpool College Institute, and Royal Coll., Mauritius (1st class scholarship); called to the bar, Middle Temple, June, 1869; counsel to government, Seychelles, 1872 to 1877; practised before supreme court, Mauritius, 1877 to 1884; member of commission on laws and rules affecting civil litigation and procedure of district courts in Mauritius, April, 1882; district judge, Seychelles, July, 1884; judge of Seychelles and senior member of executive and legislative councils, 1889; administrator of government, Seychelles, Nov., 1891, to March, 1892; member of the legislature, &c.

BROWN, W. R. E.—Entered civil service, New Zealand, as clerk to the bench, Mar., 1863; state secretary to premier and secretary to cabinet; registrar-general, Nov., 1873; concurrence clerk; clerk to district court, 1866-8; inspector of bankruptcy, 1866-7; curator of intestate estates, 1872-72; and registrar of friendly societies, 1878-84.

BOWNE, ALBERT.—Appointed, after a competitive examination, to be a clerk of the lower (now chief) division of the civil service, and assigned to Colonial Office, 4th Sept., 1877, to 20th Oct., 1880; clerk in chief secretary's office, Cyprus, 21st Oct., 1880; returned to duty in Colonial Office, Jan., 1881; appointed to act for three years as assistant accountant to the high commissioner for South Africa, April, 1891; acts also as auditor, Basutoland, from Oct., 1892; member of board of enquiry into postal and telegraph administration, Griqualand West, April, 1892; ag. Imperial secy., Jan., to Mar., 1895; and (jointly with Capt. Hawkins), Mar. to Aug., 1896; asst. to Imperial secy., and accountant, Aug., 1895.
BOWNE, DODWELL F.—District judge, Cochin, 1893.
BOWNE, F. J.—Prof. of Eng. St. Mary's Coll., of Spain, Trinidad, Nov., 1864; Prof. of Eng. Math., 1867; Dean of Coll., 1870; presid., 1876.

BOWNE, GEORGE.—Associate to the Judges of the Supreme Court of Tasmania, registrar of the supreme court in bankruptcy, curator of intestate estates, and marshal of the vice-admiralty court, 1866; was clerk of the peace, deputy sheriff, registrar of the court of requests and of insolvent Launceston, 1863; recorder of titles, acting registrar, supreme court, and collector of probate fees, 1875; was private secretary to administrators of the government, June, 1874-75; was acting private secretary to Governor Weld, 1876, 1877-79; was appointed private secretary, 1880; private secretary to governor, Straits, 1881, to Mar., 1884; to the administrator of Tasmania, April, 1884; being private secretary to Sir G. C. Strahan, governor Tasmania, July, 1886, and to administrator Sir W. L. Dobson, 1886-7.

BOWNE, T. NEWTON.—Junior clerk in colonial secretary's office, St. Vincent, 1866; clerk to colonial secretary, 1871; confidential clerk to governor, and clerk of councils and post-office till 1881, when resigned latter office; chief clerk, governor's office, St. Lucia, 1882; acted stipendiary magistrate of the first district of St. Lucia in 1883; inspector of police and inland revenue officer, Tobago, 1884; stip. mag. for Tobago; inspr. of police and marshal of prison, 1892; clerk of the peace, City Caroni, Trinidad, 1895; ag. S.J.P. City Caroni, 1895; is a J.P. for Tobago.

BRUCE, SIR CHARLES, K.C.M.G. (1889), C.M.G. (1881).—Educated at Harrow; author of "Die Geschichte von Nala und Danavanté" (Sanscrit text, published by the Imperial Academy of St. Petersburg), 1862; poems, 1866, and other works; assistant librarian, British Museum, 1863, after competitive examination in modern languages and Sanscrit; professor of Sanscrit, King's College, 1865; rector Royal College, Mauritius, 1868; director of public instruction, Ceylon, 1878; colonial secretary, Mauritius, 1882; administered the government in 1883; lieutenant-governor and government-secretary, British Guiana, 1885; Governor, Windward Islands, 1893; Mauritius, 1897.
BRUCE, GEORGE C.—3rd officer, customs, and ass. harbourmaster, St. Helena, Dec., 1878; 2nd offr., harbourm., emigr. agt., 1890; harbour master, St. Lucia, 1891.

BRUMELL, JOHN.—Admitted to Middle Temple, Oct., 1872; called to the bar 7th June, 1875, practised his profession in British Guiana until 21st April, 1882, when he was appointed a stipendiary justice of the peace, stipendiary justice for New Amsterdam, and sheriff for the county of Berbice, 1883; acted on several occasions as public prosecutor, and on two occasions as judge of the inferior criminal court.

BRYANT, ALFRED THOMAS.—Ed. Tonbridge schol. and Wad. Coll., Oxon.; Cadet, S.S., 1883; agt. collr., land rev., Penang, July, 1886; dist. officer, Malacca, 1887; act. ditto, P.W., 1889; dist. offr. Dindings, June, 1890; agt. collr. of ld. rev. and offr. in charge of treas. Malacca, Mar., 1894; agt. 1st mag., Penang, Aug., 1895.

BUCHANAN, E. J.—Called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1873; advocate of supreme court of Cape Colony, of high court of province of Griqualand West, and of vice-admiralty court, Cape Colony; elected member for Worcester in house of assembly, 1877; acting attorney-general of Griqualand West, Nov., 1879; appointed a puisne judge of the supreme court of the Cape Colony April, 1880; assigned to the court of the eastern district, May, 1880, and to supreme court, June, 1887.

BUCKLE, J. GERALD T.—Ed. Malvern Coll. and Magd. Coll., Camb., B.A. (1888); 2nd class classical tripus cadet; Hong Kong, 1870; passed cadet, Jan., 1893; ag. dep. supt. of police, July, 1892 to June, 1893; ag. asst. col. secy., June, 1893 to Feb. 1894; ag. clk. of councils ap. 1894; J.P., 1894; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of councils, Mar., 1895.

BUCK, QUINTUS A.—Cadet, Sarawak civil service, Mar., 1878; priv. sec. to H.H. the Rajah, G.C.M.G., July, 1875; supt. of police and prisons, Sept., 1877; acting magistrate, ct. of requests, May, 1885; rest. (2nd class, 3rd div.), Aug., 1886.
BUDGE, ALEX. CAMPBELL, J.P.—Entered the civil service of N. S. Wales, Nov., 1858; clerk, executive council, Oct., 1863; secretary to the Australian conference for the last 15 years.

BULLER, GEN. THE RT. HON. SIR HENRY REDVERS, V.C., G.C.B. (1894), K.C.B. (1885), K.C.M.G. (1882), C.M.G. (1879); served as dept. adj. and quartermaster-general in South Africa, Feb., 1881 to Dec., 1881; and in the same capacity in expeditionary force to Egypt, 1882; assistant adjutant-general at headquarters, 22nd July, 1883; served with the expeditionary force to the Soudan under General Graham. n 1884; and promoted to be major-general for distinguished service in the field, 1884; served as chief of the staff in Viscount Wolseley's expedition for the relief of Khartoum, 1884-5; on special service as magistrate in Ireland, 1886; under-secretary for Ireland, Dec., 1886; quartermaster-gen., Oct., 1887; adj.-gen., 1890.

RULLER, Sir Walter Lawry, K.C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G. (1875), F.R.S.—Author of "The Birds of New Zealand;" called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1874; served in the Maori war, 1865 (medal); has served in various offices in the Native affairs department, and several times received the thanks of Government; Commissioner for New Zealand at the Colonial and Indian Exhibition, 1886; is Hon. D.Sc.; officer of Legion of Honour for services at Paris Exhibition, 1889.
BULMER, J. A.—Postmaster, Cyprus, 27 July, 1878; Postmaster-general, Trinidad, 1883.

BULT, C. M.—Assistant postmaster, Kimberley, Dec., 1871; postmaster, Dutoitspan, Sept., 1872; registrar of natives, Dutoitspan, June, 1876; registrar of natives, Kimberley and Dutoitspan, July, 1885; J.P. for Kimberley.

BULWER, Sir Henry Ernest Gascoyne, G.C.M.G. (1883), K.C.M.G. (1874).—Was one of official residents under the lord high commissioner in the Ionian Islands, from October, 1860, until the withdrawal of the British protectorate in June, 1864; appointed treasurer and receiver-general for the island of Trinidad, in Feb., 1866; administered the government of Dominica, Leeward Islands, from Mar., 1867, to Jan., 1869; appointed governor of Labuan, and H.M. consul-general for Borneo, in Aug., 1871; lieutenant-governor of Natal, Aug., 1875, to 1880; Governor of Natal and Special Commissioner for Zulu affairs, Dec., 1881; High Commissioner, Cyprus, 1885; retired, 1892.

RUNDEY, The Hon. William Henry, Q.C.—Attorney-general of South Australia, 27 Sept., 1878; entered Parliament in 1871. Held office as minister of justice and education, 2nd July, 1874 to 15th Mar., 1875; third judge of the Supreme Court, 1884.
RUNDY, Fred E.—Ed. St. Mary's Coll., Hammersmith; head master, St. Mary's Coll., St. Lucia, 1890; inspr. of schools, St. Lucia, July, 1895.

BURBIDGE, G. W., Q.C.—Born Feb. 6th, 1847; called to the bar, New Brunswick, 1871; secretary to commission to consolidate statutes of New Brunswick, 1876-7; deputy minister of justice, Canada, and solicitor for Indian affairs, May, 1882; one of the commissioners to revise the consolidated statutes of Canada, June, 1883, to June, 1885; judge, exchequer court of Canada, Oct., 1888.
BURDON, Right Rev. John Shaw.—Bishop of Hong Kong, 1873.
BURGESS, Alexander MacKinnon, M.A. (Abdn.).—Born 1850; private secretary to Minister of Interior, Canada, Oct., 1876; secretary of the dept., Feb., 1882; deputy minister, July, 1883.
BURLEY, E.B.—Appled after competitive exam., elk. 2nd Divn. Civil Service, and assigned to Registrar Genl.'s Off., 21st Jan., 1896; transferred to C.O. 20th June, 1896.

BURNS, James.—Third clerk, Antigua treasury, 1870; clerk, audit office, 1872; clerk and quarantine officer, St. Kitt's treasury, 1873; 1st clerk, Antigua treasury, and accountant of saving bank, 1874; acting magistrate, 1875; acting landing surveyor, 1876 and 1878; landing surveyor and harbour master, 1879; acting treasurer, Antigua, in 1882, 1885, and 1886; and Dominica in 1882-3; treasurer, St. Kitts, 1893.

BURNS, John Fitzgerald.—Member of legislative assembly N. S. Wales, 1861; postmaster-general 9th Feb., 1875, to 21st Mar., 1877, 12th Dec., 1877, to 20th Dec., 1878; colonial treasurer 22nd Dec., 1885, to 25th Feb., 1886, and Feb., 1887, to Jan., 1889, as a member of the Paris Ministry; has been a member of various congresses of ministers and of the Colonial and Indian, Melbourne and Adelaide Exhibitions; in connection with Sir H. Parkes represented N. S. Wales on Chinese question at Sydney, June, 1888; vice-president, Melbourne Exhib., 1889.

BURNSIDE, Sir Bruce Lockhart, Kt., F.R.S.—Born, July 26, 1833.—Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, April, 1856; solicitor-general, Jan., May 26, 1864; was acting attorney-general in 1866, 1869, and 1875; member of Executive Council in 1866, and of Legislative Council in 1869; member of the local legislature from 1859; also speaker of the House of Assembly in 1860; Queen's counsel in 1874; attorney-general, September, 1875; Queen's advocate, Ceylon, chief justice, 1883; retired, 1893.

BURNSIDE, Nigel Bruce.—New Provinces, Bahamas, third clerk, surveyor-genl.'s dep., 1879 and 1881; actg. clerk, board of public works, 1880 to 1882; clerk, legislative council, 1882-88; second clerk, surveyor-genl.'s dep., 1882-83; 2nd clerk, col. sec.'s dep., 1883 to 1885; private secretary to administrator E. R. A. Taylor, C.M.G., 1885; clerk, col. sec.'s dep., and clerk of p. wks., 1887 (still holding same); actg. secy., July to Nov., 1889; July to Oct., 1890; again in 1891.

BURROWES, F. W.—Appointed to customs department, Cape of Good Hope, by the lord commissioners of H.M. treasury, Dec. 18, 1845; re-appointed magistrate and sub-collector of customs, St. Town, 19th April, 1865; sub-collector of customs and controller of customs and navigation laws, and register of shipping, Cape Town, May, 1876; collector and principal controller of customs, 1883; is chairman, Table Bay Harbour Commission.

BURROWS, ACTON.—Deputy minister and inspector of agriculture, statistics, and heat; secretary-treasurer of the board of agriculture; deputy of the lieutenant-governor for signing marriage licences, &c., Winnipeg, Manitoba.

BURROWS, Stephen Montague, M.A.—Son of Eton, scholar of Exeter College, Oxford, 1876; graduated in classical honours, 1879; went to Ceylon service, Oct., 1880; private secretary to Lieut.-Governor Douglas, June, 1881; J.P.; additional coroner for Colombo, Nov., 1881; attached to the Queen's advocates' department, Aug., 1882; J.P. and additional police magistrate, Aug., 1882; acting office assistant to the government agent, N. W. province, April, 1884; acting office assistant to the government agent, N. W. province, May, 1884; acting office assistant to the government agent, north-central province; additional district judge, commissioner of revenue and police magistrate, Anuradhapura, Aug., 1884; acting office assistant, Badulla, July, 1885; assistant-adjutant, Matale, June, 1887; author of "Visitor's Guide to Kandy," and "The Bureau of Ceylon," Editor, "Ceylon Miscellany;" asst. to Govt. agt., Kandy, July, 1889; dist. magistrate, Tangalla, Oct., 1895.

BURT, Alfred Earle.—Clerk to chief clerk Western Australia, June, 1871; clerk, comptroller's office, Dec., 1873; to governor, in audit office, Jan., 1875; draughtsman, survey department, 1875; and in Crown department, Sept., 1877; actg. land registrar, 1878. titles, Jan., 1880, to Mar., 1881; registrar of titles and deeds, June, 1890.

CURT, OCTAVUS.—Clerk in governor's office, Western Australia, May, 1872; Jan., 1874, clerk to executive council and private secretary to acting governor; April, 1874, to Dec., 1874, private secretary to Governor Weld; May, 1875, clerk to executive council and private secretary to Governor Weld; Wm. Robinson, K.C.M.G.; Sept., 1877, chief clerk and keeper of records in survey office; resident magistrate, Newcastle, April, 1880; ditto, April, 1887; asst. col. sec., April, 1887; col. sec., 1890, with seat in exec. and legislative councils, under sec. for W. Australia under responsible govt., 1891.

SHE, G. F.—Fourth clerk in colonial secretariat's office, Trinidad, Oct., 1873, third clerk, Jan., 1874; second clerk, 1877; private secretary to administrator, May to Oct., 1874; private secretary to lieutenant-Governor Des Vaux, Feb., 1877, to July, 1878; assistant to the sub-intendant and commissioner, northern province, has acted on several occasions as commissioner and sub-intendant.

SHE, ROBERT GERVAIS.—Educated at King's College, Cambridge, where he obtained an exhibition on entrance; elected to a foundation scholarship, 1874; was 27th wrangler in the mathematical tripos, 1875; graduated B.A., 1875; 2nd master of men's Royal College, Trinidad, 1878; inspector of schools, 1890.

SHE, R. J. SCOTT.—Assoc., M.I.C.E., Queen's Roy. Coll., Malvern Coll., Lancing Coll.; articled pupil to J. E. Taylor, M.I.C.E., 1879-82; ag. and special draftsman, P.W. Dept., Trinidad, various occasions, 1879-86; draftsman light railway Feb., 1880; manager of loan wks., Grenada, Jan., 1899; director, pub. wks., Mar., 1899; engaged on public works, Trinidad, 1893; and in charge of northern division, P.W.D., 1894-6; draftsman, 1897.

STILLER, AUTHUR.—Asst. to Squid, Lower Rock, Feb., 1889; ag. collr. and mag., Bermuda, Jan., 1889; ag. collr., id. rev., Larut, Aug., 1889; passed in Malay, Oct., 1889; collr., id. rev., Kuala Kangsar, Jan., 1889; 1st asst. collr., id. rev., Kinta, Jan., 1889; ag. state commiss., id. rev., Nov., 1888; collr. and mag., Kuala Kangsar, June, 1889; commiss. of lbs., Aug., 1889; ag. state audr., Sept., 1895.
STILLER, CAPT. T. J. H.—Late 3rd Bat., E. India Regt.; entered Natal Civil Service, Jan., 1889, as locker and tide waiter, Customs dept.; adj.-at-arms, House of Assembly, Natal, Nov., 1889.

STILLER, CAPT. VERE ALBAN.—Late R.M.L.I., 7th Roy. Lanc. Militia; 2nd class inspr. of police, Mauritius, June, 1889; pol. officer, with mag. of police, Diego Garcia, Sept., 1889; reverted to former post on abolition of latter, Mar., 1889; ag. 2nd class inspr. of police, Jan., 1892; inspr., Oct., 1894.

STUTTERFIELD, ABBREY G.—Clerk in British Consul's office, New York, April, 1862; paid vice-consul, Key West, Florida, October, 1862 to 1868 as unpaid vice-consul to 1871; treasury clerk, Bermuda, 1871; colonial postmaster, 1880.
BUTTERWORTH, ARCHIBALD WILLIAM.—Govt. storekeeper, Br. N. Guinea, Oct., 1890; head-qrs. officer, armed constable, Feb., 1891; also port and harbour master, and native mag.

BUXTON, SYDNEY CHARLES, M.P.—Born Oct., 1853; educated at Clifton College and Trinity College, Cambridge; was a member of the London school board from 1876 to 1882; honorary secretary to Mr. Tuke's Irish emigration fund, 1882-4; author of the "Handbook to Political Question," the "Political Manual," "Finance and Politics," "An Historical Study, 1783-1883," &c., and editor of the Imperial Parliament Series; M.P. for Peterborough from June, 1883, to Nov., 1885, when he was unsuccessful; contested Croydon unsuccessfully in Jan., 1886; elected July, 1886, and re-elected in 1892, for Poplar (Tower Hamlets), for which he still sits; parliamentary under-secretary of state for the colonies, 17th Aug., 1892, to 28th June, 1895.

BUXTON, SIR THOMAS POWELL, BART., K.C.M.G. (1895).—Born 1837; 3rd Bart.; M.A., Camb.; Dep. Lieut. and J.P. for Essex and Norfolk; Sheriff, 1875; M.P. for King's Lynn, 1865-68; Lieut.-Col., 2nd Tower Hamlets Rifle Vol. Brigade, 1864; Hon. Col. since 1884; Governor of South Australia, 1895.
BYNOE, A. D.—Clerk, G.P.O., Barbados, 1874; revenue officer, Jan., 1879; landing waiter, May, 1879; 2nd clerk, customs, June, 1887; chief clerk, customs, Dec., 1890.

BYRDE, MABERLEY DURAND EVAN.—Educated at Queen Elizabeth's college, Guernsey; writer, Ceylon Civil Service, 1867; police magistrate, Kalpitiya, Dec., 1871; acting landing surveyor, Galie, April, 1873; acting commissioner of requests, Colombo, 1874; police magistrate, Colombo, 1876; district judge, Matara, 1877; assistant adjutant, &c., Mannar, Jan., 1885; district judge, Negombo, Jan., 1886; fiscal, cent. prov., 1891; dist. judge, Batticaloa, Feb., 1891; gov't. agent, N. Central Prov., Dec., 1895.

BYRNE, A.—Governor's clerk, Natal, Mar., 1878; June, acted sec., secret defence committee; July, acting clerk, executive council; during 1880-81 was private sec. to the various officers administering the government; Feb. 9, 1881, private sec. to Sir G. Pomeroy-Colley, and on his personal staff during Transvaal war, Mar., 1882; clerk, executive council; 1883 to 1886, private secretary to Sir C. B. H. Mitchell, administrator, and priv. sec. to gov. Natal, Feb., 1890; accompanied governor to England when he went to discuss affairs of Natal with S. of S., Jan., 1893; priv. sec. admnr., July, 1893; agr. priv. sec. to gov., July, 1896.

BYRNE, J.—Clerk in the audit office, Natal, 1859; clerk in treasury, 1863; first clerk and warehouse-keeper Customs department, 1873; asst. coll. of Customs and landing surveyor, 1889; collr. of customs; regr. of shipping and emigr. officer, 1894, J.P. for the colony, 1894.
BYRNE, M.—Secretary to law department, Victoria, Oct., 1894; Formerly chief clerk in same department.

CADDY, DUNCAN JAMES, M.D., F.R.G.S., M.B.—Govt. medl. offr., Carriacou, 1892; ed., Merchant Taylors' and King's Coll., Lond. and Dub. Univ.; member Pathol. Soc. Lond., British Med. Assoc., and other learned societies; has contributed papers on medical subjects to the Lancet, Br. Med. Journal and other journals.

CADE, Charles Sherwood.—Captain in the West Cork Artillery Militia; served through the Zulu War, 1878-9; present at the battle of Ulundi; assistant-inspector, Gold Coast Constabulary, Mar., 1880; inspector, 11th Dec., 1882; adjutant and musketry instructor of the Gold Coast Constabulary, Sept., 1882, to May, 1883; local commandant of police, Cyprus, 29th Sept., 1883; also assistant to commissioner, Nicosia, and governor, central prison, Oct., 1884; has acted several times as commissioner.

CADOGAN, 5th Earl (Great Britain), George Henry Cadogan, eldest son of the 4th Earl; born, 1840; succeeded his father in 1873; was M.P. for Bath for a few months previous to his succession to the title; Parliamentary Under Secretary for War, May, 1875, to 2nd March, 1878; Parliamentary Under Secretary for the Colonies, 2nd March, 1878, to 28th April, 1880; Lord Privy Seal in Lord Salisbury's 1st and 2nd ministries; Lord Lieut. of Ireland, 28 June, 1895.
CALLCOTT, John Hope—Sup. of works and surveys, S.S., 1883; has four times acted as Dep. Col. Engineer and Surveyor-Gen., Penang. Is a J.P.
CALDER, Charles Maclear.—Admitted solicitor, sup. ct., Jamaica, 1881; cl. of cts., par. of Portland, Apr., 1888; ditto, par. of St. Mary's, Feb., 1889.
CALDER, William McCaul.—Magistrate, Berbice River, Br. Guiana, 1880; stip. mag., 1886.

CALVERT, John J.—Clerk, col. sec's. office, N.S. Wales, 1853; clerk of Select Committees, legislative assembly, May, 1856; first clerk legislative council, April, 1859; clerk assistant, Jan., 1860; clerk of the Parliaments, April, 1871.

CAMERON, Maj.-Gen. Donald Roderick, C.M.G.(1877).—Entered R.A. 1856; capt. 1866, maj. 1875, lieut.-col. 1882, col. 1886, retired 1888; instr. in gunnery, 1859-62, 15th Bde. R.A.; served throughout Rhodesian campaign, 1864-66, as adjutant and as staff officer of officer commanding R.A. Doorn Field Force (medal with clasp, thrice mentioned in despatches); adjutant 1st Bde. R.A. 1867-8; accompanied the Hon. W. McDougall, C.B., to Fort Garry, as a member of the executive council, N.W. Territories, 1869; was commissioner international boundary commission, and superintended the expedition which marked the international boundary from the Lake of the Woods to the summit of the Rocky Mountains, 1872-6; reported on the British Alaskan boundary, 1874; secretary to Canadian delegation at the Paris international conference, 1883, for the protection of submarine cables; reported on the eastern boundary of British Columbia, 1884; and again on the Alaskan boundary in 1886; private secretary to Sir C. Tupper, Canadian plenipotentiary to the fishery confer., 1887; principal, Royal Military College, Kingston, Canada, 1888. Retired 1896.

CAMERON, Donald Charles.—Clerical asst. Ind. rev. B. Guiana, 1890; 5th cl. clk. secretariat, 1891; 4th ditto, 1895; sec. to bd. of enquiry shipping casualties, Orde, 1893; to special commissioner to convict settl., 1893; to spirits comsn., 1894; minor industries comte., 1895; and to select comte. of legislature on railway tariff; 3rd class and despatch clk., 1895; sec. special committee on loans to planters on sugar crops, 1895; priv. sec. to ag. governor, Oct. to Nov., 1896.

CAMERON, Edward J.—Ed. at Shrewsbury Schol., Clifton Coll., and at Merton Coll., Oxon.; private secretary to Sir C.C. Lees, governor, Bahamas, Jan., 1882; and as governor of the Leeward Islands, Jan., 1884; assistant colonial sec. and treasurer, Sierra Leone, Nov., 1881; J.P. for the Settlement, and visiting justice, Freetown Gaol; acting sec. and treas., Dec., 1885, to July, 1886; president, Virgin Islands, Feb., 1887; member Leeward Islands Leg. Coun., 1887, and Ex. Coun., 1888; commissioner, Turks Islands, March, 1889; judge sup. ct., May to Dec., 1893, and Jan. to June, 1895.

CAMERON, H. H.—Educated at Charterhouse and Univ. Coll., Oxf.; writer Ceylon civil service, 1868; commissioner of requests, &c., Hajran; acting assistant government agent, Kandy, confirmed April, 1872; acting assistant government agent, &c., NuwaraKadawila, Nov., 1872; acting second assistant colonial secretary, 1873; private secretary to the governor, 1874; fiscal, central province, 1875; police magistrate, Colombo, 1875; assistant government agent, Matale, 1876; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, 1879; acting at Badulla, 1881; and acting government agent western province, 1886; chairman municipal council and mayor Colombo, Nov., 1887; dist. govt. agt., Puttalam, 1893; govt. agt. N. Central Prov., 1894; dist. judge, Jaffna, 1895.

CAMERON, Major Maurice Alexander R.E.—First commission in Royal Engineers, Aug., 1874; deputy colonial engineer, New Settlements, Dec., 1883, to April, 1892, which acted for about two years and a half; colonial engineer and surveyor-general, and as a member of executive and legislative councils appointed crown agent for the colonies, 2nd July, 1895.
CAMERON, Sir Roderick William, K.C.M.G. (1883).—Commissioner for Canada to the Australian International Exhibitions.
CAMPBELL, Capt. Archibald David.—L.t. S. Leon police, Mar., 1891; wounded at Tanga severely; ag. inspr.-gen., Sept., 1895.
CAMPBELL, C. C.—R.M., Hopetown Division, Cape Colony, Dec., 1882; ditto, Colesberg, 1883.
CAMPBELL, Ernest D.—Sub-inspector constabulary, British Honduras, Dec., 1886.

CAMPBELL, Sir George W. R. K.C.M.G. (1891), C.M.G. (1887)—Ensign, Argyll and Sutherland Highlanders, Jan., 1855; lieutenant same year, assistant-superintendent, Bombay revenue service, 1856; on the outbreak of the mutiny, 1857, appointed adjutant of the Ahmedabad Corps, assistant superintendent of police, assistant magistrate (has mutiny medal), superintendent of police, and magistrate of the 1st class and commandant of the Ratnagerry barracks, Dec., 1859; promoted in 1863, and selected to reorganize the Canara police force; in 1864 command of the Belgaum police. Received numerous commendations from the Bombay Government and from the governors-general and secretaries of state for India for special services, and received two special donations of 3,500 rupees and 1,000 rupees for special service. Passed examinations in Hindostanee, Googerathi, Marathi, and Calare. In 1866 was selected to take police charge of Goa and to reorganise its constabulary, receiving special gratuity of 1,000l. for this service. In 1870 confirmed as inspector-general; ag. lieut. gen. Penang, 1872-3. Cholera commissioner to India in 1866, and again in 1877; inspr.-gen. of prisons from 1st Jan., 1885; was in charge of Egyptian exiles. Retired 1891.

CAMPBELL, H. C.—Second clerk in the registry office, Natal, April, 1861; second audit office, January, 1863; first clerk, court, county of Pietermaritzburg, Mar., 1864; R.M., division of Ixopo, Mar., 1876; act. 1st division of Umgeni, Nov., 1876; R.M., Ixopo... for colony, 1880; master and registrar, sup., 1882; colonial auditor, April, 1889; has acted judge native high court, and as 2nd puisne judge and atty. general; auditor general, 1894; iman civil service board.

AMPBELL, JOHN WILLIAM.—Appointed till, 1852, to the customs at Hobart, Tasmania, collector of customs, Launceston, registrar of shipping and warden, marine board; is a magistrate commissioner of the supreme court.

AMPBELL, JOSEPH ALEXANDER.—Late sergeant-major, Cameron Highlanders; served in the India campaigns 1858-9, and in Egyptian campaign, 1882; medal for Indian service and medal clasp for Tel-el-Kebir and Khedive's star; has for long service and for distinguished fact; drill instructor, Western Australia; lieutenant and staff adjt. of volunteers, 1886.
AMPBELL, WILLIAM BENJAMIN.—In postal, navy, and harbour depts., Sierra Leone, since 1879; chief clerk, survey dept., 1879.

APE TOWN, 2ND BISHOP.—The Right Rev. JAMES WEST JONES, D.D.—Consecrated, 1874; bishop of the church of the province of Africa; formerly fellow of St. John's College, Oxford, preacher at Chapel Royal, Whitehall, rural dean of Oxford.

APPER, ALFRED HOUSTRON.—Ed. Royal Coll., Armagh, and Trin. Coll., Dub., scholar in classics; B.A. in 1883; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1883; J.P. and magistrate, May, 1884; passed examination in Chinese, and attached to chief councillor's office, Penang, 1886; 2nd prot. of Chinese, Singapore, 1888; district mag., 1889; ag. sen. dist.off. Butterworth, P.W., 1890; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., April, 1890; protector of Chinese, Singapore, 1893; ag. mag. Penang, Sept. 1893; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Sept. 1894; ag. asst. col. sec., Nov., 1894, to Feb., 1895; asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang, April, 1895.
APPER, THOMAS.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Camb.; (Lond.), 1876; B.A., Camb. (Math. and Nat. Triposes), 1879; inspector of schools, Jamaica, 1880; official member of leg. coun., 1882.
ABERY, JOSEPH, M.B.C.M.—Assistant colonel surgeon, Ceylon, 1867.

ABRONE, SIR JOSEPH, LL.D. (1862), K.C.M.G. (1867), K.C.M.G. (1891).—Received at the bar, 1863; reporter, court of commerce, 1868 advocate and govt. legal adviser, 1880; member executive and legislative councils ex officio; justice, Malta, 1885.
ADDEW, COL. FRED., C.M.G. (1894).—remor S. Leone, 1894; formerly resident magistrate in Zululand.

AEBEY, W. S.—Special agent to interior tribes, Van Leu, Fiji, Nov., 1874; stipendiary magistrate, 1875; land titles commissioner, and under native regulations board, 1877; native boundary commissioner, Nov., 1880; member comm., Sept., 1882; is now rest. commr., Colo. and stipendiary magistrate, Rewa.

ARILIE, EDWARD, gold medallist in law, Melbourne Univ., 1868.—Clerk, census office, Gloria, 20th May, 1861; registrar-general's clerk, Jan. 1862; crown law offices, 1st Mar., 1865; called to the bar, Victoria, 1871; parliamentary and professional assistant to crown law officers; parliamentary draftsman, July, 1879; clerk-assistant, legislative assembly, April, 1882; re-appointed parliamentary draftsman with higher duties, April, 1889; author of "Notes on Victoria legislation," "Victorian Year Book, 1883-4."

CARLING, THE HON. SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1893).—Born 1828; member for London, Ontario, 1857-74; elected to Dominion house of commons on confederation, 1867; receiver-general of Canada, 1862; minister of works and agriculture, Ontario, 1867-71; again elected for London, 1878; sworn of the privy council and made postmaster-general in Sir J. MacDonald's government, 1882; minister of agriculture, 1885 to 1892; member of cabinet without portfolio, 1892; retired, 1894.

CARLINGFORD, BARON (United Kingdom) (1874), CHICHESTER PARKINSON FORTESCUE.—1st baron, P.C. (England 1864, Ireland 1865).—Educated at Christ Church, Oxford, first class in classics in 1844, and obtained the chancellor's prize for the English essay in 1846; was a lord of the Treasury from Jan., 1854, to April, 1855; under secretary of state for the colonies, May, 1857, to Feb., 1858, and re-appointed June, 1859. M.P. for co. Louth 1847 to 1874. Privy councillor, 1861; chief secretary for Ireland, Nov., 1865, and again 9th Dec., 1868; president of Board of Trade, Dec., 1870, to Feb., 1874; lord privy seal, 1881; lord president of the council, 1883, to June, 1885.

CARMODY, P.—Government analyst, Trinidad, and professor of chemistry, Queen's Roy. Coll., and Coll. of the Immaculate Conception, June, 1890; member central agricultural bd., 1890; member bd. of health, 1891; entered imperial service after open competition, 1876; inland rev. laboratory scholarship, 1880; diploma (1st class) Roy. Sch. of Mines; asst. analyst and subsequently analyst, Somerset House, 1881-90. Is F.I.C., F.C.S., member of soc. of chem. industry, and author of prize paper on "Recent Progress of Invention and Science," and of other published papers on scientific subjects.
CARON, L. B.—Puisne judge, Superior Court, Quebec, 1874.

CARON, THE HON. SIR J. P. R. A., K.C.M.G. (1885), Q.C.—Born 1843; called to the bar of Lower Canada (now Province of Quebec) 1865; created a Queen's counsel 1876; entered the Canadian House of Commons, 1873, where he still sits; minister of militia and defence for the Dominion of Canada, 8th Nov., 1880; postmaster-general, 1892; one of representatives of Canada at Colonial conference, Ottawa, 1894.
CARPENTER, PERCY TRANTER, M.R.C.S., Eng., L.R.C.P. Lond. (St. Mary's Hosp., Lond.), dist. surg., Corosol, Br. Honduras, 1892; ag. dist. commr., 1893; asst. col. surg., Staun Creek, 1894.

CARPMAEL, CHARLES, M.A., F.R.S.C., F.R.A.S. (late Fell. St. John's Coll., Camb.).—Deputy superintendent, meteorological service, Canada, Oct., 1872; director, magnetic observatory, Toronto, and superintendent, meteorological service, Feb., 1880.
CARR, CAPTAIN GEORGE LYON, R.N.—Superintendent of ports, Malta, 1880.

CARR, MARK WILLIAM, M.Inst., C.E.—Born 1847; Ed. King's Coll., London; employed on India Rlys., 1866-76; dist. eng., Natal Rlys., Aug., 1876; resident eng., April, 1882; chief redt. eng. constr. and maintenance, Aug., 1888; eng.-in-chief of railways, Sept. 1889.

CARRINGTON, EARL (created 1895), CHARLES ROBERT CARRINGTON (THIRD BARON), P.C., born 1842; G.C.M.G.—joint hereditary Lord Great Chamberlain of England; late captain Royal Horse Guards, lieut.-colonel 3rd battalion, Oxford Light Infantry; captain Queen's Body Guard, 1881; governor of N. S. Wales, 1885-90; Lord Chamberlain, 1892.

CARRINGTON, Major-Gen. Sir Frederick, K.C.M.G. (1887), C.M.G. (1890).—Ed. Cheltenham Coll.; 24th Regt.; commander of the "Frontier Light Horse" in the Transkei War, South Africa, 1877-78; commanded Transvaal volunteers against Sikukuni, 1878-79; commanded left wing C. M. R., 1879 to 1883; commanded colonial forces in Basuto war, severely wounded; commanded 2nd Mounted Rifles, Bechuanaland Field Force, 1884; commandant of police, British Bechuanaland, 1885; commandant native forces, Zululand, in 1888; again in command Bechuanaland Police, Dec., 1888-93; and again on special service, Nov., 1893, in connection with Matabele war; commanding brigade at Gibraltar, 1895; commanded forces in Matabele war, 1896.

CARRINGTON, John Worrell, Q.C., F.R.G.S., C.M.G. (1888). D.C.L., LL.D.—Ed. Codrington Coll., Barbados (S.C.C., 1866), and Linc. Coll., Oxon (B.A., 1872; M.A., 1891); called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, June, 1872; acted as judge, assistant court of appeal, Barbados, 1874-75; J.P., 1874; member house of assembly, 1874-8, and again 1881; M.L.C., 1878-81; solicitor-general, 1878; again acted as judge, assistant court of appeal, 1879; and as attorney-general, 1880; Hon. D.C.L., Durham, 1879; Hon. L.L.D., St. Andrews, 1894. Was presdt. of the education board, and of the board of health; and received the thanks of govt. for services in connection with education; chief justice, St. Lucia and Tobago, 1882; on special duty as ag. col. sec., Grenada, 1882; administered govt. of Tobago, 1883 to 1885; prepared revised edition, Tobago laws; acting chief justice of Grenada, 1886; in 1887-9 prepared a new edition of the Laws of St. Lucia; att.-gen. Br. Guiana, Dec., 1888, chancellor of the diocese, 1890; engaged, 1891-2, in drafting for the govt. of Br. Guiana bills relating to the courts, criminal law and procedure, &c., of that colony; chairman central board of health, 1889, of the opening-up-the-country commission, 1891-2, and of the spirits ordinance commission, 1893-4; major in the Br. Guiana militia, 1893. In 1894 prepared a new and revised ed. of the laws of Br. Guiana; chief justice, Hong Kong, 1896.
CARRINGTON, J.A.—Sub-inspector of schools, Barbados, Jan., 1879; inspected schools, St. Lucia, July, 1885.

CARTER, The Hon. Sir F. B. T., K.C.M.G. (1878).—Barrister, Newfoundland, 1842; Q.C., 1859; member house of assembly, 1855 to 1878; speaker, 1861 to 1865; premier and latter, gen., 1865 to 1870, and from Feb., 1871, to April, 1878; delegate to Quebec, 1864, in arranging terms for establishing the dominion of Canada; again at Ottawa, 1869, for the admission of Newfoundland into the dominion; and several delegations respecting treaty rights of the trade on the Newfoundland coast; chief justice, 1878; administrator by Royal Sign Manual, 30th Sept., 1878, and on several occasions since during the absence of and vacancies in office of governor.

CARTER, Sir Gilbert T., K.C.M.G. (1893); C.M.G. (1890).—Entered Navy 1864; paymaster of Colonial steamer "Sherbro," Sierra Leone, Aug., 1870; served on the Gold Coast during a part of the Ashanti campaign; was one of the commissioners for valuing the ordnance and stores left behind by the Dutch at the transfer of Elmina; private secretary to governor Loward Islands, Sept., 1875; collector of customs and treasurer, Gold Coast, Aug., 1879; treasurer and postmaster, Gambia, Oct., 1882; administered the government several times; administrator of Gambbia on separation from S. Leone, Nov., 1880; governor, Lagos, 1890, retired, 1896.
CARTER, W.—Ed. at Bradfield Coll., Bradcadet, S. S., 1890; passed final examination in Malay, Dec., 1892; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Jan., 1896.
CARTWRIGHT, Capt. J. T.—Lieut. in army, 1856; capt., 1862; sold out 1868; agent of immigration, Jamaica, 1872; stipendiary magistrate, 1874; do. British Guiana, 1888.

CARTWRIGHT, The Hon. Sir Richard John, K.C.M.G. (1879).—Born 1835; entered Canadian Parliament, 1863; sworn of the privy council, and was minister of finance, 7th Nov., 1873, until the resignation of the Macdonald cabinet, Oct., 1878; Minister of Trade and commerce in Mr. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.

CARUANA, A. A., O.P.—Secretary to Univ., Malta, July, 1886; acted also as assessor to the rector in the Lyceum of the Three Cities and teacher of history and geography, Oct. 1886; acting rector, Nov., 1879; principal of the University, June, 1880; librarian to the government public library, 15th Nov., 1880; dir. ed. 1897.

CARVALHO, J. A. DE.—Second clerk accountant in the treasury, Hong Kong, Aug., 1855; first clerk and cashier, Jan., 1860; assistant superintendent fire brigade, Sept. 1867 to Dec., 1883; J.P. for the colony, Dec., 1883.
CASAUT, Sir L. N., K.T. BACH. (1894).—Puisne judge, superior court, Quebec; chief justice, superior court, Quebec, 1894.

CASEY, The Hon. James Joseph, C.M.G. (1878).—Barrister-at-law; M.L.A., Victoria, 1867; member ex. council, 1868; minister of justice, 1868; solicitor-general, 1869; president of board of land and works and minister of agriculture, 1872 to 1875; president of Victoria commission, Paris Exhibition, 1878; executive vice-president of Melbourne International Exhibition, 1880; president of Federal Bank of Australia; is judge of insolvency court and chairman general sessions; judge of courts of mines and county courts. Is Knight Officer of the Legion of honour and of the Crown of Italy.

CASOLANI, Edward Robert.—Apprentice clerk, Cyprus service, Sept., 1878; and housekeeping clerk, Mar., 1883; on the high commissioner's establishment, Cyprus; transferred to the chief secretary's establishment, March, 1884.

CASOLANI, William.—Clerk, port dept., Malta, May, 1863; customs dept., Jan., 1867; 2nd class clk., ch. sec. office, June, 1880; 1st class clk. and assist. clk. to the coun., Jan., 1886; deputy secy. to Govt. and ch. clk., July, 1891; assisted to Gov. and clk. to ex. coun. and coun. of Genl., July, 1894.

CASSELS, Ront., B.A., Q.C.—Admitted to bar of Lower Canada, June, 1864, and of Upper Canada, Aug., 1866; a notary public for Ontario, Nov., 1868; registrar supreme court of Canada, Oct., 1875; one of the masters, court of chancery, Oct., 1876; Q.C., 1885.
CASSERLY, James Michael.—3rd class clerk, colonial secretary's office, Jamaica, 1878; 2nd class clerk, Dec., 1882; confirmed Nov., 1885.
CASTOR, C. F., M.B., C.M., F.R.G.S.—Ass't col. surgeon, Gold Coast Colony, Feb., 1878; medical officer, British Guiana, 1885; med. superint. Lepros Asylum, 1887; is J.P. and coroner.
CATT, The Hon. A.—Commissioner of crown lands, South Australia, 24th June, 1881, to June, 1884; commissioner of public works June 11, 1884; chairman of committees, leg. ass., 1886.
CAVE-BROWNE-CAVE, Bernard.—Served 39th regiment, 1882-1890; lieut. 99th regiment, 1890; inspr. S. Leote frontier police, 1895; J.P. colony.

CAYLEY, Sir Richard (Knt., 1882), M.A., and formerly scholar of St. John's College, Cambridge. Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 11th June, 1862; became supreme court of Ceylon, 7th June, 1863; being deputy Queen's advocate from Jan., 1867, to Aug., 1868, when he was confirmed in that appointment; puisne judge of the supreme court, 1873; Queen's advocate, 1876; chief justice, 1879; resigned, 1883; is a magistrate for Rutland.

CELESTIN, Louis Albert.—Ed. govt. normal l. Mauritius; teacher in Edn. Dept., 1874; ag. pr. of schls., Nov., 1886, to Aug., 1889; dist. clerk Dec., 1889, to Nov., 1893; chief insp. of distilleries, Nov., 1893; ag. supt. of distilleries, Oct. to Oct., 1895.

HADWICK, John Courtenay Chasman.—Work and interpreter to the R.M., Inanda division, Jan., 1868; clerk of the court Oct., 1870; as R.M. and administrator of native law on various occasions, 1872-7, at Inanda; J.P. for Inanda division, Aug., 1875; registrar of native high court, and sec. to native administration board Nov., 1876; admitted an attorney of supreme court, 1878; first class interpreter or staff of Maj.-Gen. Newdigate during Zulu War 1879 (medal and clasp); administrator of native and border agent, Upper Tugela, 1880; R.M., river division, and J.P. for the colony, 1882; admtr. of native law, Ixopo, Sept., 1883.

HADWICK, John.—Assistant-superintendent instrument room, telegraph department, Cape Town, March, 1879; superintendent of telegraphs, Natal, July, 1879; general sub-manager telegraphs, Natal, 1880; postmaster-general, 1881; postmaster-general and general manager telegraphs, Jan., 1884.

HADWICK, Osbert, C.E., C.M.G. (1886). Has been employed by the colonial office to report on the drainage and water supply of various colonies; was con. eng. for the Malta water works and Hong Kong water supply and drainage.

HALMERS, Sir David Patrick, Knt. Bach. (1875).—Member of the Scottish Faculty of Advocates, and called to the bar, 1860; appointed magistrate of the Gambia, 1867; magistrate of the Gold Coast and judicial assessor to the native courts, 1869; queen's advocate of Sierra Leone, 1872; queen's advocate, Gold Coast, 1874; prepared codes of civil and criminal procedure, measures for the abolition of slavery, and other important measures necessary on the foundation of the Gold Coast Colony; in 1876, knighted, in recognition of his services on the West Coast of Africa; chief justice, Gold Coast Colony, 1876; chief justice, Br. Guiana, 1878; mem. of commission to inquire into charges against the attorney-general of Jamaica, 1893; retired 1894.
HALMERS, E. B.—Resident magistrate, Ngha division, Cape Colony, Jan., 1880.

HALMERS, William.—Ag. asst. cr. surveyor, revenue officer in charge of rivers and creeks, Demerara, Nov., 1866; asst. cr. surveyor, city, Dec., Dec., 1866; junior asst. cr. surveyor, 1875; senior do., Dec., 1877; asst. cr. surveyor, Dec., Dec., 1886; cr. surveyor, Feb., 1888.

HAMBERLAIN, The R. Hon. Joseph, P.C., P. Hon. LL.D. (Camb.).—Eldest son of the Mr. Joseph Chamberlain; born in London, and educated at University College School, after which he was engaged in business in Birmingham till 1874, when he retired. In 1870 he was elected a member of the Birmingham town council, and in the same year a member of the school board, of which he became chairman in 1873. In each of the three following years, 1874-5-6, he was elected mayor of the borough, and in June, 1876, on the retirement of Mr. Dixon, one of the three representatives of Birmingham, he was returned to Parliament. At the general election of 1880 he was again returned with the late Mr. John Bright and Mr. Philip Muntz as his colleagues, and on the formation of Mr. Gladstone's administration he was appointed President of the Board of Trade with a seat in the cabinet. While at the Board of Trade he prepared and succeeded in passing through parliament the bankruptcy act which is now in force, the patents act of 1883, and other measures. After the redistribution of seats in 1885 he was returned as member for West Birmingham, and joined Mr. Gladstone's third administration in 1886, as president of the local government board, but resigned in consequence of his disagreement with the premier's home rule policy. At the general election of 1886 he was returned unopposed for West Birmingham, and at the end of 1887 proceeded to the United States as plenipotentiary to negotiate, in conjunction with H.M.'s minister there and Sir Charles Tupper, a treaty for the settlement of the Atlantic fisheries question. The result of these negotiations was the convention of 1888, and though it was rejected by the U.S. senate, the modus vivendi established by it still regulates the fisheries. On the defeat of Lord Rosebery's government in June, 1895, Mr. Chamberlain joined Lord Salisbury's government as secretary of state for the colonies. At the general election of 1895, in which he took a very prominent part, he was again returned for West Birmingham.

CHAMBERLAYNE, Major Tankerville James.—Ed. Cheltenham Coll.; Ensign, Ceylon Rifles, June, 1868; transferred to 80th regt., Oct., 1873; adjutant, Hong Kong, June, 1875, to Mar., 1877; acting-brigade-major, Nov., 1875, to Feb., 1876; officiated twice as assistant military secretary, China command; served in Transvaal and throughout Zulu war, 1878-79; present at Ulundi (medal and clasp, 1878-79); A.D.C. to Governor of Natal (Sir H. Bulwer, G.C.M.G.), 3rd Feb., 1882; private secretary, Mar., 1882; and also acting clerk executive council, July, 1885; private secretary to high commissioner of Cyprus, and clerk, executive council, Mar., 1886; local commr. of police, 1892; col. of customs and excise and asst. to commr. Larnaca, 1894; ag. commr., Larnaca, June, 1895.

CHAMBERLIN, Brown, Lt.-Col., C.M.G. (1870).—Member House of Commons of Canada for county of Mississquoi, 1867 to 1870; in command 60th Bat. Canadian Active Militia, 1869 to 1871; in 1870 commanded battalion, and for a time the whole force, engaged in repelling Fenian invasion at Eccles Hill, for which created C.M.G.; appointed Queen's printer, and retired from Parliament, 1870; Queen's printer and controller of stationery, with rank as Deputy Minister, July, 1886.
CHAMBERS, C. Pearson, A.I.E.E.—Supt. telephones, Leeward Islands (Antigua), 1889.

CHAMBERS, John Ratcliffe.—2nd clerk to president St. Kitts, May, 1875; 3rd landing waiter, treasury, Feb., 1878; 1st clerk to registrar and provost marshal, April, 1881; secretary of the commission to enquire into the general condition of Dominica, Nov., 1881; private secretary to governor, Leeward Islands, 1882 to 1883; clerk to magistrate district C, St. Kitts, July, 1883; deputy coroner May, 1885; quartermaster with St. Kitts' Militia, June, 1885.

CHANCELLOR, W. G.—Second officer of customs, Maryborough, Jan., 1861; locker, Brisbane, April, 1861; landing waiter, April, 1862; chief landing waiter, Jan., 1867; landing surveyor, Nov., 1869; chief inspector, distilleries, and excise April, 1886. Is a magistrate for the colony.

CHANDLER, WILLIAM KELLMAN, B.A., LL.D.—St. John's Coll., Cambridge (1873-79); law tripes, 1879-80; called to the bar, Inner Temple, June, 1879; acting escheator-general of Barbados, 1880-81; J.P., 1880; acting police magistrate of Bridgetown, Aug. to Nov., 1880; acting solicitor-general, Nov., 1880, to Mar., 1881; acting judge of assistant court of appeal, Mar., 1881, to June, 1883; master-in-chancery, May, 1882; judge of assistant court of appeal, June, 1883; member of the House of Assembly, Mar., 1881; represented the parish of St. Peter until Nov., 1884; M.L.C., 1884; represented Barbadoes in negotiations at Washington in connexion with McKinley Tariff Act, 1891.

CHAPLEAU, THE HON. SIR J. A., K.C.M.G. (1866), D.C.L., and Q.C.—Born 1840; called to the bar of Lower Canada, 1861; created a Q.C., 1864; entered the Quebec assembly, 1867; was solicitor-gen., from 1873 to 1874; provincial secretary from 1875 to 1878; became premier of Quebec, 30th Oct., 1879, a position he retained until July, 1882, when he resigned, was sworn of the privy council, and appointed secretary of state for Canada; knight commander of the Order of St. Gregory the Great, 1881, and commander of the Legion of Honour, 1882; is president of the credit foncier, France-Canadien, and director of the Montreal and Western, and of the Pacific Junction railways; minister of customs, 1892; lieutenant-governor, Quebec, Dec., 1892.
CHAPMAN, ARTHUR.—Assessor of taxes, Hong Kong, 1889; ag. asst. sup'td., fire brigade, 1891-2; J.P., 1894; lieut. Hong Kong volks.

CHAPMAN, GENERAL SIR FRED. EDWARD, G.C.B. (1875) K.C.B. (1867), R.E.—Entered the army as lieut., 1835; became captain, R.E., 1846; lieut.-colonel in that corps, April, 1859; colonel in the army, 1855; proceeded on a special mission to Constantinople, Jan., 1854; was present at the battles of the Alma and Inkerman; served throughout the siege of Sebastopol, during the early part of which he was director of the left attack, and in the latter part was executive engineer to the forces; was several times mentioned in despatches (medal with three clasps); made officer of the legion of honour; received the Sardinian and Turkish medals, and the third class of the Medjidiye; created, for his military services, C.B., 1855; K.C.B., 1867 and G.C.B., 1877; Governor and Commander-in-chief at Bermuda, 1857, and major-general same year. Inspector-general fortifications and director of works, 1870 to 1875; director of military intelligence, 1890-6.
CHAPMAN, PHILIP EDWARD.—Entered Trinity, Jamaica, Feb., 1855; clerk, Oct., 1856; lock-keeper, Rum Dept., May, 1858; asst. keeper, Tresy., Jan., 1859; commissir. of salt, Sept., 1861.

CHARLOTTETOWN, BISHOP OF THE RIDEAU, REV. P. MCINTYRE.—Educated at St. Albert's College, Prince Edward Island, St. Hyacinth College, Canada, and the Grand Seminary, Quebec. Ordained priest and appointed pastor of the Hash Mission, Prince Edward's Island, Feb., 1863; consecrated Bishop of Charlottetown, Aug., 1860.

CHASTELLIER, E.—Surgeon superintendent, Cannonier Point, Mauritius, 1866; poor-law medical officer, Sept., 1869; surgeon superintendent, Sanatorium, 1 Feb., 1873; acting sanitary warden, 1873 and 1887; acting poor law commissioner, 1873; acting registrar-general 1885-86; ag. med. officer, Immig. dept., Oct., 1890, and Nov., 1892; chief med. offr., Dec., 1898, Apr., 1892, and Jan., 1893; confirmed Nov., 1893; title changed to director med. and health dept., Nov., 1895.

CHATHAM, WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. at High Sch. and Univ., Edin.; asst. to Messrs. Macmillan and Son, C.E., Edin., 1880; resd. eng. at Bolesworth Drainage Works, 1883; asst. eng. engr's, off., Bristol, 1885; exec. eng. P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1890; ag. dir. of Pub. Wks., 1893-94.

CHILD, A.—Called to the bar, Middle Temp., Nov., 1876, went south-eastern circuit; supplementary magistrate, county Victoria, Trinidad; acting ditto, Port of Spain, July to Dec., 1881; from Aug., 1886; ag. puisne judge, 1887; ag. cong. San Fernando R. Vol., 1888; chief jus. St. Lucia, 1890, adm'd. govt. Nov., 1894 to Feb., 1895.
CHOMLEY, A. W.—Judge of county courts of mines, and courts of insolvency; chairman of general sessions, Melbourne, Victoria; formerly Queen's prosecutor.
CHOMLEY, H. M.—Chief commissioner of police, Victoria.

CHRISTIE, JAMES JOHN.—Clerk to C.C. R.M., Fort Beaurtort, and distributor of sugar, 1866; served in customs department Cape Town, 1870 to 1877; secretary to special commissioner Trans-Gariep tribes, April, 1876, to May, 1877; charge in Damaraland, Dec., 1876, to April, 1877; C.C., Cathcart Dec., 1879; C.C. and R.M., H.M. Jan., 1881.
CHURCH, THE HON. C. E.—Member of Lunenburg in Nova Scotian Leg. Ass., commr. for public works and mines.

CHURCHILL, JOHN SPENCER.—Educated at Winchester; ensign 46th regt., 1854; served in siege of Sebastopol (medal and clasp); capt. 36th regiment, 1866; A.D.C. to Lord Lisgar, lord high commissioner of the Ionian Islands, president of Virgin Islands, 1879; acting presdt., Nevis, 1879, and of Dominica, 1882 to 1884; presdt., Montserrat, 1887; commr. of St. Kitts, Nevis, 1888; col. sec., Bahamas, 1893.

LANWILLIAM, 4th Earl of, created 1766; Count Clanwilliam and Baron Gilford, 1766; on Clanwilliam, 1828 (United Kingdom), B., K.C.M.G. (1882); succeeded his father in 1859; entered the Royal Navy 1845; rear-admiral 1863; vice-admiral 1881; naval A.D.C. to the Queen 1872 to 1876; a lord of the admiralty 1874-80; in command of detached squadron, 1880-82.

LAPIN, ADOLPHUS P.—Clerk in office of commissioner of crown lands, Aug., 1850; clerk, legislative council, 1853; clerk, legislative assembly, 1856; first clerk, legislative council, 1860; clerk assistant, April, 1871.

LARE, HENRY LEWIS.—Ed., Rathmines and Trin. coll., Dub.; B.A. (1879), M.B., and Ch. (1880); junr. resid. med. offr. publ. hosps., Kingston, Jamaica, Apr., 1881; dist. med. offr. River dist. and hosp., July, 1882; transferred to St. James dist., 1883; to Chapelton, 1888; to Spanish dist., 1892; surg. to St. Catherine dist. prison to Middlesex and Surrey cty. gaol; dist. med., Kingston, June, 1896.
LARENCE, BEVERLEY CHARLES.—Clerk to star, supreme court, Natal, April, 1875; assistant clerk of the peace, and prosecutor of the vice high court, Natal, April, 1880; clk. of the r., 1896.
LARK, ANDREW INGLIS.—Attorney-general of mania in the Fysh government, 29th Mar., 1842; member of the federal council of Australia, Jan., 1888; and of the federation convention, 1891.

LARK, FRANCIS W., M.B., D.P.H. (Camb.), L.C.S., M.R.C.P.—Ed. St. Paul's Sch. and Dieses Hosp., med. offr. of health and suptd. Hosp. Lowestoft, 1893; health offr., Hong Kong, May, 1895; memb. sanit. bd., J.P., 1896.
LARK, HENRY JAMES.—Government printer, Natal, Oct., 1873; govt. statistician, Feb., 1887; editor of Trinidad—A Field for Emigration, 1888; is fellow of the Royal Statistical Society.

LARK, JAMES HENRY, M.R.C.P., R.C.S., L.M., L.S.A.—Prizeman St. Thomas's Hospital, London, Jan., 1870; government medical officer, St. Elizabeth (1870 to 1877), Jamaica; G.M.O. (1877 to 1878), Mandeville District of Manchester, in charge of hospital, prison and constabulary; medical superintendent lepers' house, parochial hospital, Spanish Town, District of St. James's, Mar., 1878; transferred to Kingston hospital, Sept., 1878, to April, 1879 (acting senior medical officer); acting medical officer, Kingston constabulary force, (1878 to 1879); member quarantine board; re-appointed G.M.O., Santa Cruz, district of St. Elizabeth, 1st May, 1879.

LARK, ROBERT, M.A., Edin. (First Class honours, 1873), Ferguson scholar (Classics); exhibitioner and M.A., New College, Oxford, 1877; Class Classical Mods., 1874, 2nd Class, final classical School, 1877; assistant professor of Latin, Edinburgh Univ., 1878; head master, govt. high school, Maritzburg, Natal, Oct., 1879; act. secy. to council of education, 1882; called to the bar, Inner Temple, May, 1886.

LARK, WM.—Ed. Roy. High Schl., Edin.; called to the bar (Mid.Temp.) June, 1883; scholarships intern. and constit. law, 1882, and common and criminal law, 1882; admitted Br. Guiana bar, 1883; acted on several occasions as crown prosecutor; revising barrister, Oct., 1892; master sup. and regt. gen. S. Leone, Sept., 1894; J.P., S. Leone, 1895; dep. judge sup. ct., Feb., 1896; ag. ass. just., Mar., 1895; pol. mag., coroner and judge of requests, May, 1895; cr. proscr., Jan., 1896; dep. judge, Jan., 1897.

CLARKE, Lieut.-General Sir Andrew, R.E., G.C.M.G. (1885), C.B. (1869), C.I.E. (1876).—Entered R.E. 1844; A.D.C. to Sir William Denison when governor of Van Diemen's Land, 1846; served in New Zealand during 1848-9, and was at the close of the war sent on a mission to Hiki; in the same year, rejoined Sir William Denison as private secretary; M.I.C., 1851; surveyor-general of Victoria, 1853; on the proclamation of the new constitution he was returned to the assembly for the city of Melbourne, and joined Mr. Haines' cabinet as minister for public lands, resigning with it in 1857; on its second resignation, in 1858, he declined to form a new administration, and returned to Europe. He was commanding royal engineer of the eastern and midland districts till 1863, when he proceeded on special service to the West Coast of Africa; director of works of the navy, 1864 to 1875; governor of the Straits Settlements, 1873, when he initiated the operations which led to the pacification of the Malay peninsula; public works member of Council of India, March, 1875; commandant of school of military engineering, Chatham, 1881-2; inspector-general of fortifications and director of works, 1882; ag. agent-gen. for Victoria, 1892-4 and 1897.
CLARKE, ERNEST D.—Dep. regtr., Sup. Ct., Trinidad.

CLARKE, Sir FIELDING, KT. BACH. (1894) LL.B.—Called to the bar, Middle Temple, Easter Term, 1876, north-eastern circuit; atty.-gen. of Fiji, 1881; acting chief justice and chief judicial commissioner for the Western Pacific, 1882 to 1883, and again 1884, confirmed 1885; puisne judge, Hong Kong, 1888; chief justice, 1891; chief justice, Jamaica, 1896.

CLARKE, Lieut.-Col. Sir GEORGE S., R.E., K.C.M.G. (1893), C.M.G. (1887).—Entered R.E., 1868; Capt., 1880; Major, 1887; served in Egypt, 1882 (Medal and Star); in Snakin Expedition, 1885, mentioned in despatches (clasp); Sec. Colonial Defence Committee, 1885-92.

CLARKE, JOSEPH.—Formerly a non-commissioned officer in the Army; served three years on the West Coast of Africa; intendant and dispenser colonial hospital, British Honduras, Jan., 1872; quarantine officer, and in charge of the station, 1874; chief warder, 1876; acting keeper of the common gaol, 1877; sub-inspector of police, 1878; keeper of the common gaol, 1878; clerk, colonial engineer's office, 1883; and keeper of cemeteries, 1885; inspector of nuisances, 1885.

CLARKE, Lieut.-Col. Sir MARSHAL JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G., 1880, (late R.A.).—Resident magistrate, county Pietermaritzburg, Natal, 1874; A.D.C. to Sir T. Shepsone, H.M.'s special commissioner, South Africa, 1876; political officer and special commissioner, Lydenburg, 1877; served in Transvaal campaign, 1880-1, in command of Landrost's office, Potchefstroom; mentioned in despatches; resident magistrate, Quithin, Basutoland, 1880; commissioner, Cape police, King William's Town, 1882; colonel commanding Turkish regiment, Egyptian gendarmerie, 1882; third class order of Medjidie; retired from the army, with rank of lieutenant-colonel, 1883; resident commissioner, Basutoland, 1884; resdt. commissn., and chief mag., Zululand, 1893.

CLARKE, WILLIAM JAMES.—Joined Natal Mounted Police, 1878; served through Zulu war of 1879 (medal with clasp); accompanied ex-Empress Eugenie to Zululand, 1880; served in Transvaal Campaign, 1881; present at battle of Laing's Neck; accompanied mission to Pondoland, 1887; mission to Tongaland, 1888, and Portuguese- Swaziland Boundary Commission, 1898; acting R.M. and administrator native law, Ixopo division, 1889-90; Criminal Investigation offr., 1895.

CLARKE, Sir W. John, Baronet, 1882.—A distinguished and wealthy colonist of Victoria; member of the legislative council; president of the commission and executive committee for the Melbourne International Exhibition, 1880.

CLAVIER, Alexander.—Ed. Stonyhurst; super. clerk col. sec. office, St. Lucia, Mar., 1860; clerk, audit office, Jan., 1862; comptroller and auditor, June, 1868; also registrar royal court and of deeds and mortgages, Mar., 1870, with the title of deputy registrar and accountant; registrar and accountant, July, 1875; prothonotary and registrar, Oct., 1879; also sheriff, April, 1886; is also registrar-general of births and deaths, a member of the legislative council, Castries town board, and a J.P.
CLAYTON, A. G.—Ed. Marlbro. Col.; 3rd class clk. Col. Sec. office, Jamaica, Sep., 1886; chief clerk, Col. Sec. office, Br. Honduras, July, 1892.
CLAYTON, L. H.—B.A., Camb.; Cadet S.S., Nov., 1895.
CLEVELAND, R. A.—Dist. med. offr., Cyprus, 1894.

CLEMENTS, W. Lee.—Supervisor of Customs, Cape Coast, 1891; pay and qr. mr. G. Coast constably, Aug., 1891; in charge of specie and stores with Attabubu exped., and recd. thanks of S. of S. for colonies, 1893-4.

CLIFFORD, Hugh Charles.—Joined Perak service, 1883; passed in Malay, Feb., 1885; edlr. land revenue, Kwila Kangsar, March, 1885; on special service to Pahang, 1887; act governor's agent there, 1887-8; collr. and mag. 1889; supt. Ulu Pahang, 1889; ag. Br. result. Pahang, Sept., 1890 to March, 1891, Apr. to Dec., 1891, and from Feb., 1893; sec. to govt. Selangor, Dec., 1894, but continued to act as result. Pahang. Resdt. Pahang, July, 1896.

CLIFTON, Robert Cecil.—Landing waiter and postmaster, Bunbury, Western Australia, 1873; 2nd clerk, lands and survey department, 1874; 2nd clerk, customs, 1876; chief clerk, land and survey dept., April, 1880; under secy. for lands, 1891.

CLINCKETT, R. J.—Entered the provost marshal's office, Barbados, as junior clerk in 1869; accountant in 1870; acted as provost marshal from Feb. to Sept., 1879, and again in 1880; resigned 1880; appointed official assignee 1st Oct., 1882.

CLOETE, Sebastian Valentyn.—Clerk to resident magistrate, Bathurst, Cape of Good Hope, 1853 to 1854; ditto R. M., Albany, 1854 to 1858; acting C. C. and R. M., Bathurst, Jan. to July, 1858; clerk to C.C., Worcester, 1858 to 1860; clerk of the peace, Somerset and Bedford, 1860 to 1865; acting C. C. and R. M., Somerset, 1865 to 1866; C.C. and R.M., Richmond division, 1866; C.C. and R.M., Somerset East, May, 1873; C.C. and R.M., Paarl division, 26th Nov., 1877.

CLUTSAM, S. H. O.—Called to bar, Bahamas, July, 1868; acting sec. to bd. of ed., 1882; acting stipendiary and circuit magistrate in 1887; and assist. clerk and sergeant-at-arms house of assembly, 1866; ch. clk., 1890.

COBHAM, C. D.—Ed. Rugby and Univ. Coll., Oxon; B.A. (honours), 1866; B.C.L., M.A., 1869; M.R.A.S. asst. commissioner, Larnaca, Cyprus, Oct. 1, 1878; commissioner of Larnaca, March 18, 1879; acting chief secretary to Government, 1880; acting chief collector of customs, 1883.
COBLEY, Walter Henry, Assoc. M.Inst.C.E.—Assistant engineer, Natal railways, 5th Feb., 1876; acting maintenance engineer, Jan., 1881; maintenance engineer, 1882; district engineer, 1883.

COCHRANE, C. S., M.Inst.C.E.—Graduate Trinity College, Dublin, 1868; was employed in railway works in South America, 1871-74; assistant engineer on the Cape Government railways, 1874; acting district engineer, 1877; engineer in public works department, Trinidad, 1878; captain in charge of surveys, 1879.
COCKBURN, The Hon. John Alex., M.P. (Long.), Member of House of Assembly, S. Australia; minister of education, 1885, to 1887; premier, 1889-90; again minister of education and agriculture, 1893.
COCKS, James.—Third clerk, colonial secretary's office, Fiji, 1875; chief clerk and interpreter to armed native constabulary, 1877.
CODE, A.—Land surveyor, St. Lucia, May, 1882.
COHEN, The Hon. H. E.—Colonial treasurer, New South Wales, 18th December, 1877, to 25 Dec., 1878; Judge, Metropolitan Court, May, 1881; minister of justice, 5th Jan., 1885, 1886.

COKER, S. D. A.—Assistant clerk, treasury, Gambia, May, 1873; 2nd landing waiter, cste., Aug., 1875; assistant clerk, treasury, Feb., 1876; chief clerk, treasury and post office, Mar., 1876; actg. gov's. clk.; clk. to leg. com. and dep. ser., June to Nov., 1889; cashier, Jan., 1892; charge of the treasury, June to Nov., 1892.

COLE, Fred. Edward.—Ed. at Marlboro Coll. and King's Coll., Lond.; called to the Middle Temple, Feb., 1886; dist. commiss. Gold Coast Colony, May, 1888; clk. of Ct. Elizabeth, Jamaica, Nov., 1879; ag. res., April, 1891.
COLE, G.—Inspector of public schools, Falmouth, and secretary to the education board, and Sept., 1882.

COLE, Joseph W.—Temporary clerk in colonial secretary's office, Sierra Leone, Nov., 1868; clerk to the queen's advocate from Jan., 1868, to July, 1869, transferred to the cast. department as second clerk and keeper, in Feb., 1869.

COLE, Rowland.—Supernumerary clerk—advocate's department, Sierra Leone, Aug., 1870; postmaster, Cape Coast, Nov., 1870; ditto, Apr., 1879; colonial postmaster, Gold Coast, 1879; acted as director of telegraphs for a short period in 1892; postmaster-general, Gold Coast Colony, 1892.

COLE, Samuel Silvanus.—Government head printer, Lagos, 20th January, 1865; acted as head printer from December, 1866, to February, 1867; and again from May to October, 1874; government head printer, Gold Coast, March, 1875.

COLENBRANDER, Herman James.—For Immig., Zulu Border Agency, Lower Tugela Division, Natal, Nov., 1881; dep. clk. of police, Umsinga, Nov., 1887; clerk and interpreter, R. M. Umvoti, Aug., 1889; clerk of the Clan River Divn., 1895.
COLES, The Hon. Sir Jenkin, K.C.M.G. (1834).—Speaker leg. assembly, S. Australia, 1879.

COLL, A. M.—Ed. at Haileybury and King's Coll., Oxon; B.A., 1885; M.A. and B.C.L., 1886; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1887; admitted to the Gibraltar bar; administrator general, 1891; acting police magistrate, Feb., 1891.

COLLYMORE, H. W.—Supernumerary, general post office, Barbados, October, 1876; acting third clerk, 1878; third clerk, record branch, colonial secretary's office, March, 1879; third clerk, correspondence branch, January, 1881; acting second clerk, 1883; acting public librarian, 1884; second clerk, correspondence branch, colonial secretary's office, July, 1884; ag. chief clk., June, 1893, to April, 1894; Aug. to Dec., 1895, and July to Nov., 1896; secy. to Highways Commn., 1893-6.

COLMER, JOSEPH G., C.M.G. (1888).—Private secretary to first high commissioner for Canada, and secretary of the emigration department of the Canadian Government Offices in London, 1880; appointed secretary to the office of the high commissioner, 1881; acted as interim secretary to Colonization Board since its formation, Dec., 1888, and formally appointed as such Nov., 1891.

COLOMB, SIR JOHN C. R., M.P., K.C.M.G. (1888), (C.M.G. 1887).—Was M.P. for Bow and Bromley, 1886 to 1892; formerly captain R.M.A., in which corps he served 1854-69; has been variously employed with naval, military, and auxiliary forces; author of many works and lectures on imperial defence and protection of commerce in war; received the thanks of gov. of Br. Columbia, 1877.
COLOMBO, 4th BISHOP OF.—RIGHT REV. REGINALD STEPHEN COPLESTON, D.D.—Formerly fellow and tutor, St. John's College, Oxford. Consecrated, December, 1875.
