COLTON, THE HON. SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1892).—Was a member of the house of assembly, South Australia; commissioner of public works, Nov. 3, 1868, to May 12, 1870; treasurer, June 3, 1875, to March 25, 1876; commissioner of public works, June 6, 1876, to Oct. 26, 1877; chief secretary, June 16, 1884, to June 16, 1885.
COLUMBIA, BRITISH.—1st BISHOP, RIGHT REV. GEORGE HILLS, D.D.—Was perpetual curate of St. Nicholas, Great Yarmouth, 1848; honorary canon of Norwich cathedral, 1850; Bishop of British Columbia, 1859.
COLVILLE, J. W. — Clerk, lands depart., Victoria, appointed 1863; head of registry of that depart., Jan., 1878; secretary central board of health, May, 1885.
COMMISSIONG, ALBERT.—Clerk, colonial secretary's office, Grenada, Oct., 1879; 2nd clerk, registrar's office, Jan., 1883; clerk, audit office, May, 1883; chief clerk, ditto, June, 1885.

COMMISSIONG, W. S.—Admitted to the bar, Grenada, 1867; escheator-general, July, 1872; has acted on various occasions as attorney-general and as chief justice; is an unofficial member of legislative council.
CONROY, J. G.—Central District Court judge, Newfoundland, 1880.
COOK, ALEXR.—Auditor Br. N. Borneo, Oct., 1880; treas. and aud. gen., Aug., 1881; member of coun., June, 1883.

COOKE, EBENEZER.—Accountant (afterwards manager), English and Australian Copper Company, S. Australia, 1862-82; represented Flinders in Parliament, 1875-82; J.P., 1875; member of various select committees; chairman Royal Commission on Finance, 1880-2; member Royal Commission on education, 1882-3; first commissioner of audit, 1882.

COOKE, JOHN HAMILTON, B.A., M.B., B.Ch.—Trin. Coll., Dub., L.M. Rotunda Hosp., med. offr. dist. C. Dominica, 1891; med. offr. dist. 8, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1892; member bd. of health, med. offr. Yaws Hosp., med. examiner Standard Life Assnse. Co.

COOPER, BART. (1863), G. C. M. G. (1888), K.C.M.G. (1890) (KNIGHT BACH, 1857).—Sir DANIEL.—Elected to the legislative council of N.S. Wales 1849; chosen 1st speaker of the legislative assembly under the new constitution, 1856; is member of the senate of the Sydney University, president of the Bank of N. S. Wales; acting agent-general for N. S. Wales in 1888.

COOPER, FRANCIS ALFRED, M.I.C.E.—Fellow Sanitary Inst., Gt. Britain; educated at Rossall and Loughborough Gram. sch.; Assistant to Mr. James Mansergh, C.E., Westminster, 1879; sanitary surveyor, P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1887 to 1890; surveyor to sanitary board, 1888 to 1892; inspector of buildings, P.W.D., 1888 to 1890; acting asst. engineer, 1889; acting surveyor general, 1889; acting asst. surveyor general, 1889 to 1890; resident engineer in charge of water and drainage dept., 1890 to 1892; director of public works, Oct., 1891; member exec. and legis. councils, Mar., 1892.
COOPER, P.A.—Attorney-general, Queensland, 31st Dec., 1880; Northern judge, 5th January, 1883.
COPELAND, THE HON. H.—Member Legis. Assembly, N.S. Wales; sec. for lands in Dibbs' ministry, 1891-4.

CORK, PHILIP CLARKE.—Appointed clerk, immigration Department, Jamaica, 1875; Hindustani interpreter to the agent general of immigration, 1875; sub-agent of immigration of the 2nd class, 1876; inspector of immigrants of the 1st class, 1880; protector of immigrants, Grenada, 1881; acting inspector of police, 1883; protector of immigrants, Jamaica, 1884.
CORK, WM.—Clerk and locker, Jamaica, Aug., 1868; 3rd class asst. coll. of taxes, Jan., 1869; 3rd class coll. of taxes and parochial treasurer, 1870; 2nd class coll. of taxes, 1879.

CORNWALL, C. F.—Born 1836; graduated B.A. Magdalen College, Cambridge, 1857; called to bar of Inner Temple, 1861; many years member of legislature of colony of British Columbia; on confederation of that province with Canada, in 1871, summoned by Her Majesty to the Senate of the Dominion; lieut.-governor of British Columbia, 1881-6.

CORNEY, BOLTON GLANVILL.—Educated at Fontainebleau in London, and at Schwerin; St. Thomas's Hospital, 1870; M.R.C.S., England, 1874; entered the colonial service in 1877 as government medical officer, Fiji; also health officer, Suva, 1877; medical officer for the immigration department, 1881; acting chief medical officer, 1882, 1883, 1884, 1885, and since June, 1887; acting agent-general of immigration, Jan., 1885, to Mar., 1887; M.L.C. and of native regulative board, 1885; confirmed as ch. med. offr., June, 1887; M.L.C., June, 1888; ag. supdt. lun. asylum, 1887; ag. rec. gen. Jan. to Mar., 1892; commiss. to enquire into decrease of native popn. 1893; commiss. to enquire into misgovernment of Prov. Tailevu, May, 1895.
CORNISH, CHARLES COX.—Clerk, land titles office, S. Australia, 1864; chief clerk, criminal business, 1865 to 1877; sec. to attorney-general, law dept., 1888.
COSTIGAN, THE HON. JOHN.—Member of Canadian house of commons and of privy council; min. of inland rev., 1885; sec. of state for Canada, Dec., 1892; min. of marine and fisheries, 1894.
COTTLE, H. C.—2nd asst. govt. printer, Ceylon, Nov., 1885; asst. govt. printer, 1888.

COULL, W.—Clerk, colonial secretary's office, Leeward Islands, Mar., 1878; acted on more than one occasion as chief clerk, and as clerk of the Antigua and federal executive and legis. councils; deputy coroner for District &c., 1880; magistrate and coroner for District Roseau, and registrar-general of births, marriages and deaths, Dominica, Aug., 1882; member of Board of Poor Law Guardians; a visiting juror of the gaol, and nominated M.L.A., 1883; ex-secretary-general, 1885; member ex. coun., 1895.
COURT, HENRY.—Called to the bar Md. Temple, 1844; graduate Law Univ. of Paris; legal coun., Trinidad, 1866; 2nd puisne judge civil and crim. ct. of Trinidad, 1879; resigned in pension in 1887.

COURTNEY, JOHN MORTIMER.—Ass. secretary and accountant to the treasury, 1869; deputy minister of finance, deputy receiver-general and secretary to Treasury, 1878; mem. of commission of inquiry into the Civil Service of Canada, 1891.

COURTNEY, Rt. Hon. LEONARD H.—St. John's Coll., Cam.; B.A. (2nd wrt.), 1855; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1855; presented Liskeard, Dec., 1876, to Dec., 1885; was returned for the county division when borough was merged; Parliamentary under-secretary for the Home Department, Dec., 1886; Parliamentary under-secretary for the Colonies, 1881; secretary to the Treasury, Mar., 1885; resigned, 1885; chairman of ways and means, 1886-92.

COWAN, JAMES.—Clerk to resident magistrates, York, Western Australia, 1864; clerk to magistrate, Perth, 1870; clerk to legislative council, 1874; registrar and master supreme court, Nov., 1878; also curator of intestate estates, 1883, R.M., Guildford, Mar., 1886; gov. rec. Northern dist., 1889; pol. mag. Perth, 1890.
COWAN, W. D.—Clerk to R.M., Newcastle, 1858; R.M., Bunbury, 1886.
COWARD, CAPT. GEORGE F.—Sub-agent of immigration, Jamaica, 1873; inspector of stables, 1873; 2nd-class inspector, 1881.

COWPER, SYDNEY.—Clerk in office of commissioners, exhibn. of 1851, in 1871, in edinb., S. Kensington, 1875; 3rd-class clerk in customs off., Capetown, 1879; priv. sec. to premier, 2nd-class clk., 1881; priv. sec. to premier, 1st-class clk., 1885; sec. to prime minister, retired on abolition of office, 1891; priv. dept. of agri., 1892; ag. sec. to prem. (office re-established), 1896. Is a J.P. for District.

COWPER, VERY REV. WILLIAM MAGUIN.—Magd. Hall, Oxon.—B.A., 1833; M.A., 1833; p. 1834; dean and archdeacon of S. Patrick, 1858; vicar-general, 1877; formerly c. of St. Peter's, Dartmouth, 1833-6; chapl. to A. A. Co., R. Stephen's, 1836-56; principal Moore Coll., Liverpool, 1856; I. of St. John's, Bishopsthorpe, 1857; I. of St. Philip's, Sydney, 1858.

COX, CHARLES T.—Third clerk, governor's secretary's office, British Guiana, 1st July, 1874; second clerk, 1876; record clerk, 1879; sec. clerk, 1883; chief clerk and secretary to quarantine board, 1888; was secretary to commissioners appointed in Jan., 1882, to inquire into the state of the administrator-general's department of British Guiana; student Mid. Temp., 1880; compiled "Index to Laws of B. Guiana," (2nd ed., 1891), also "Some Notes for Govt. Officers," 1894; ass't, govt. secy., and clerk to policy and combined ct., 1889; also clerk of ex. coun., 1892; ag. govt. secy., Mar. to July, 1893.

CREASY, E.—Private secretary to his father Sir Edward Creasy, Chief Justice of Ceylon, in 1868; assistant to the surveyor general, 1871; district surveyor, 1876; acting police magistrate, commissioner of court of requests, and deputy fiscal at Balapeta—Modera, 1878; assistant super, in the Ceylon police force, 1879; passed the prescriptive examination in the Sinhalese language, 1882; acting head-quarter superintendent, and superintendent of police of the northern, eastern, and north-western provinces, and J.P. for those provinces in 1882-5; act. assistant supt., central province, 1885; supt. of police, Colombo, Aug., 1892.

CREASY LEONARD, A.M.I.C.E.—Pioneer offir., Ceylon, 1874; superintending offir., 1877; district engineer, Feb., 1886; provl. engr. N. Prov., July, 1890; financial and office asst., Nov., 1894; prov. engr. E. Prov., Jan., 1895; ditto Prov. Uva, May, 1895; on deputation to Seychelles, Oct., 1896, to Feb., 1897.

CRESWELL, MARGARET S.—Apptd. postmistress of Gibraltar and suptdt., of govt. telegraph imperial P.O., Sept., 1877, as a mark of the estimation in which the services of her late father, the dep. postm. gen. at Gibraltar, had been held; transferred to col. govt. in Jan., 1886, when the colony assumed the control of the local P.O.

CRISSON, JOHN C.—Cashier of the public bank, Turks Islands, Mar., 1864; clerk of the crown and prothonotary, and clerk, colonial secretary's office, June, 1867; postmaster, July, 1868; auditor, 1869; acting assistant commissioner, April, 1884, to Sept., 1886; registrar of shipping, Aug., 1885; is a J.P.

CROFTON, FRANCIS BLAKE, B.A., Trinity College, Dublin, 1862.—Provincial librarian, Nova Scotia, Jan., 1882; author of "The Major's Big-Talk Stories," "Halibutton, the Man and the Writer," "Hairbreadth Escapes of Major Mendex," &c.
CRONIN, J. D.—Probationer, treasury, Sydney, Feb., 1854; third class clerk, 1855; second ditto, 1857; first clerk, pay branch, 1864; paymaster, 1865; acted suptdt., money order office in 1865.

CROOK, JOHN ROWLAND, A.M.I.C.E.—Entered Crystal Palace Engineering School, 1881; asst. to E. F. Griffith, C.E. (consulting sanitary engineer), 1883; supt. of drawing office, C. P. Engineering School, 1888; gained 1st prize for civil engineering, City of London Coll., 1889; asst. engr. water and drainage department, Hong Kong, 1891; in charge of new sewerage works, city of Victoria, 1891-94; sanitary surveyor, 1892; executive eng. P. W. D., 1893; and water authority during absence of dir. of pub., wks., 1893-94.
CROPPER, JAMES BASSNETT.—Assistant protector of immigrants, St. Lucia, Sept., 1880; acting protector of immigrants, 1882; acting chief clerk, govt. office, and clerk of councils, 1884.
CROPPER, ROBERT P.—Superintendent of emigration, Barbados, 1873; protector of immigrants, St. Lucia, 1878.

CROSBY, JAMES MICHAEL.—Clerk to resident magistrate, Simon's Town, Cape Colony, 1857; clerk to C.C. and R.M., Prince Albert, 1860; 2nd clerk, attorney-general's office, 1864; master and registrar eastern districts court, 1869; C.C. and R.M., Humansdorp, 1872; chief clerk, attorney-general's office, and clerk of the peace for Cape Town, Dec., 1872; appointed high sheriff of the colony, Oct., 1877; taxing officer, supreme court, Jan., 1882; R.M., Cape Town, and district and visiting magistrate to Breakwater, Clifton, and Tokai convict stations, May, 1884.

CROSS, J. W.—Clerk, Zulu interpreter, and sub-distributor of stamps in the resident magistrate's office, Ixopo, Natal, 23rd Oct., 1876; deputy-clerk of the peace for Ixopo, 2nd Mar., 1877; and on 3rd Mar., 1879, appointed in command of the Ixopo, Natal native foot contingent, 800 strong; and subsequently transferred to command of the mounted contingent during the Zulu war; acted on several occasions as resident magistrate of Ixopo; clerk to R.M., Weenen, July, 1886; admnr. of native law, Pagadi's locn., 1889; magistrate, Lower Tugela divn., 1894; R.M., Alfred Divn., 1895.
CROSSKILL, HERBERT.—Deputy provincial secretary, Nova Scotia, 1868-78; and again, 1882.

CROSSMAN, COL. SIR WILLIAM, R.E., K.C.M.G. (1884), C.M.G. (1877). M.P.—Visiting magistrate, West Australia, 1852-6; secretary of Canada Defence Commission, 1862; commissioner to inquire into the resources, finances, and administration of Griqualand West, 1875; member of the West Indies Finance Commission, 1882; returned to Parliament for Portsmouth, Dec., 1885, and again 1886-92.

CROWE, THOMAS.—Served in inland revenue department (England) to Oct., 1882; temporary employment as inspector of excise, Cape, Oct., 1882; chief inspector of excise, July, 1884; controller of licences and stamps, Dec., 1887, and administrator of foods, drugs and seeds act, Dec., 1890.

CUDDEFORD, WM.—Employed on financial mission in Bahamas, May, 1885, to May, 1886; supervisor of customs, Gold Coast, Jan., 1887; local auditor, Lagos, under C. and A.G., London, Dec., 1889; ditto, S. Leone and Gambia, Sep., 1893.

CULBERT, JOHN ALEXANDER.—Tidewaiter, Nassau, Oct., 1853; acted as warehouse keeper, Feb., 1856; second clerk, receiver-general and treasurer's department, Dec., 1856; acting market clerk, Oct., 1859; warehouse keeper from Aug., 1863; also examining officer, 1869; collector, Mar., 1870; acted as president justice, Inagua, from Dec., 1873, to June, 1874; member House of Assembly for Harbour Island, May, 1875; acting registrar of records, June to Dec., 1883, and Jan. to Feb., 1884; ag. rec. gen. and treas., July to Oct., 1887, and June, 1888, to May, 1889.
CULL, J. B., M.A.—Principal of the Royal College, Colombo, Ceylon, Aug., 1878; director of public instruction, Ceylon, 1880.
CULLEN, E. B.—Under secretary to the treasury, Queensland, 1st October, 1877.
CUMMINGS, HENRY.—Ed. at Derby and Ober Lalinstein, Germany; admitted solr. supreme et., May, 1880; dist. commr. G. Coast Colony, May, 1891; asst. Queen's advocate, Accra, May to Aug., 1891.
CUNLIFFE-OWEN, EDWARD, C.M.G. (1886). For services in connection with the Colonial and Indian Exhibition, 1886.

CUNNINGHAM, HENRY HUTT.—Educated at Charterhouse; obtained open scholarship (nat. sci.) Clare Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (math. tripos), 1874; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1876; stipendiary magistrate, British Guiana, 1884.
CUNYNGHAME, PERCY F.—Cadet, Sarawak service, July, 1886; asst. result., May, 1890; result., 2nd class, 4th divn., March, 1896.

CURRY, CHARLES.—Entered the col. sec.'s office, Cape, June, 1871; appointed 3rd class clerk, Aug., 1872; 2nd class, July, 1874; promoted dept. of crown lands and public works, Oct., 1879; chief clerk, office of crown lands and public works, April, 1881; secretary to the diamond mining commission, Sept., 1881; from 1882 has frequently acted as asst. commissioner of crown lands and public works asst. commissioner of crown lands and public works, Apr., 1892; secy. for agriculture, 1893.

CURRIE, SIR DONALD, K.C.M.G. (1881), C.M.G. (1877).—Chairman of the Castle M. Packets Co. In July, 1876, at the time of negotiations between the Earl of Carnarvon and President Brand, he assisted by his good offices bringing about the success of these negotiations and thus ending the long dispute between the British Government and the Orange Free State in reference to the Diamond Fields; has been MP for Perthshire since 1880.
CURTIS, ALFRED P.—Third class clerk, 1864, in public works department, Western Australia; removed to survey office, 1865; 1st class clerk, post office, 1872; 1st class clerk, 1875.

CUSACK-SMITH, T. B.—Educated as barrister, Middle Temple, 1884; law scholar, 1883; formerly lieut. 1st V.R., Essex Regt.; appointed H.M. Consul at Samoa, Jan., 1890; H.M. deputy commissioner for the Western Islands of Samoa, May, 1890; proceeded in H.M.S. "Curaçao" to Union Group to report on claims, Aug., 1892; deputy commissioner for Samoan and Union Groups, and authorised to perform the duties of resident in the Union Group, Oct., 1892; special judicial commr., April, 1893; apptd. to license and superintend employment of Gilbert Islds, natives in Samoa, Jan., 1894.

CUSCADEN, W. A.—Senior Sophister, Trinity Coll., Dublin captain, 4th Royal Dublin Fusiliers; assistant inspector of constabulary, Gold Coast, 1879; civil comm., Tacquah, 1881; ch. insp. of police, Singapore, 1883; asst. suptd. of pol. Force, 1884; act. pol. supt., Malacca, 1889, and Sarawak, 1891; asst. suptd. of pol., prov Wellesley, 1893.
CUTHBERT, THE HON. HENRY, M.L.C.—Minister of justice, Victoria, 1886-90; subsec., 1894.

DAETH, JOHN.—Educated at King's College, London; assistant draughtsman, P.W.D., Jan., 1873; chief ditto, Mar., 1875; district engineer, Northern (now Western) District, 1878-83; engineer, Kingston District, 1886.

DALE, AUGUSTUS CHARLES.—Entered the civil service 1st Aug., 1880, promoted to 2nd class, 1st Jan., 1883, and to 1st class, 1881; principal colonial secretary's office, 1881; registrar of statistics, colonial secretary's office, 1891.

DALE, HAROLD Ross.—Entered attorney-general's office, Cape, Dec., 1879; 2nd class clerk, July, 1883; chief clerk to solicitor-general, Hamilton, Mar., 1885; chief clerk to attorney-general, Nov., 1886; J.P. for district of Allandale.

DALE, SIR LANGHAM, K.C.M.G. (1889).—BA. of Queen's College, Oxford, 1847, selected by Sir John Herschel to be professor of classics, St. John's College, Capetown, 1848; superintendent-general education for the Cape Colony, 1859; vice-chancellor of the university of the Cape of Good Hope, 1877; M.A., Oxford, 1873; Hon. LL.D., Glasgow; retired, 1892.
DALE, LANGHAM.—Chief clerk, health and police, government branch, colonial secretary's office, Cape, 1891.
DALE, WILLIAM.—Served in the police department, Western Australia, from 1859 to 1862; immigration agent and officer in charge of police houses, 1874; superint. charitable institutions, 1874.

DALGARNO, JAMES.—Clerk in the general post-office at Sydney, 1860; chief clerk, 1862; now asst. sec.; visited England, America, and New Zealand, as secretary to Sir Saul Savory, who was specially commissioned by the N. Wales government to organise a mail service between the United Kingdom and the Australian colonies by way of the United States; vice-president, art society, N.S.W., 1885; president, Sydney mechanics' school of arts.

ALTON, E. H. G.—Clerk to stipendiary magistrate, Demerara River, 1857; clerk, customs, 1859; clerk, registrar's office, 1860; notary public, 1861; first clerk, 1864; acted as registrar in 1867, 1868 and 1872; assistant government secretary, and assistant receiver general for Berbice, 1874; colonial postmaster, 1875; J.P., and commissioner of affidavits; registrar of the supreme court, May, 1876; and registrar-general of births and deaths; acting receiver-general, 1886.

ALTON, THE REV. CANON JOHN NEALE, M.A., (1882.)—Was Governor to their Royal Highnesses the Princes Albert Victor and George Charles, and acting chaplain to H.M.'s Ship "Chanticleer," chaplain in ordinary to Her Majesty, and canon of Windsor, 1885.
ALY, T.—Lieut., Georgetown Militia, Br. Guiana, Oct., 1867; adjt.-gen., Sept., 1868; also chief of villages, April, 1875; sec. to central board of health, Mar., 1883.
ALY, T. THE HON. THOMAS MAYNE, Q.C.—Member for Selkirk, Manitoba, in Dominion house of commons. Minister of interior, Canada, 1892-6.

ALY, T. ST. FELIX.—4th-class clerk, Post Office, British Guiana, March, 1879; 3rd-class clerk, 1881; 2nd-class clerk, 1883; acted as chief clerk, 1884-85, and 1887; chief clerk, December, 1888; acted as postmaster-general, 1888-9 and 1890.

ANAVALL, JOHN MARTIN.—Barrister Middle Temple, 1877; acting solicitor-general, Leeward Islands, 1883, and again in 1884; acting magistrate, district "C," 1883, and again in 1885; second assistant judge, Leeward Islands, June, 1888; also judge of the Vice-Admiralty Court, 1889.
ANDOLO, CHARLES A.—Clerk, dist. ct., Larne, Cyprus, Mar., 1883; asst. regr. and inter. agent, 1886; ag. regr., July, 1886, to Sept., 1887.
ANE, ROBT., M.R.L.S., L.S.A. (1887) —C.P. (1889).—House Physician, Middlesex hospital, 1890; house surg., general hospital, Liverpool, June, 1890; col. surg., Prov. Wellesley, June, 1893.
ARLEY, CECIL W.—Engineer-in-Chief for rivers and river navigation and water supply, Wales, 1891.

ARLEY, SIR F. M., KNIGHT BACH. (1887.)—Attorney, 1858; Queen's counsel, 1878; vice-president executive council, New South Wales, 1878-9; chief justice, New South Wales, 1886; lieutenant-governor, 1891.
ARNETT, MAJOR J. G., C.M.G. (1881).—Commandant of the 27th and 86th regiments; commandant of volunteer and mounted police, Natal, 1874.
ARNETT, CHARL. W. (late 3rd bat., Chesh. L.)—Joined Sarawak civil service, April, 1878; officer in charge, Barian, April, 1885; inspector of police and prisons, Aug., 1888.

AVENPORT, SIR SAMUEL, KNIGHT BACH, K.C.M.G., and I.L.D. (1886).—Crown attorney of the legislative council, South Australia, 1879; non-official member of the partly elective legislative council which passed the constitution bill, 1876-8; elected member of the legislative council, 1876-8; twice minister of public works; executive commissioner at the London International Exhibition, 1851; Philadelphia, 1876; Melbourne, 1879; Melbourne, 1880; Colonial and Indian, London, 1885; seven years president of the Royal Agricultural and Horticultural Society of South Australia, and nineteen years president of the Adelaide Chamber of Manufacturers.
DAVEY, WILLIAM B.—Second clerk, Antigua treasury, December, 1878; landing waiter, September, 1879.

DAVIDSON, ANDREW, M.D., F.R.C.P., Edinburgh; M.R.C.S., England; Fellow Royal Physical Society.—Formerly physician to the court of Madagascar; visiting and superintending surgeon civil hospital, Mauritius, Feb., 1877, and government analyst, Dec., 1877; acted as police and prison surgeon in 1878; professor of chemistry, royal college, April, 1879; superintendent, lunatic asylum, Nov., 1879; author of numerous medical works; secretary to cattle plague commission, &c.

DAVIDSON, WALTER EDWARD.—Scholar and exhibitioner of Christ's Coll., Camb.; writer, Ceylon civil service, Aug., 1880; attached to Kandy Kachcheri, Dec., 1880; writer, colonial secretary's office, April, 1881; transferred to Colombo Kachcheri, Sept., 1881; attached to the Negombo Police Court, May, 1882; acted as assistant agent, Negombo, July to Oct., 1882; extra office assistant, Colombo Kachcheri, May, 1883; and acting office assistant, Nov., 1884; secretary to the Ceylon Commission, Colonial Exhibition, 1886.
DAVIDSON, WM. — Surveyor-General, Queensland, 1890.

DAVIDSON-HOUSTON, J. H., M.A.—Ed. in Germany and Trin. Coll., Dub.; entered Roy. Innis. Fus., Jan., 1888; served in Cameron Highlanders, 1892; extra certificate Schol. of Musketry, Hythe, and Instr. of Musketry; R.A. (1889); M.A. (1893); sub-insp. Br. Honduras Constab., Feb., 1893; bronze medal Roy. Hum. Soc., 1896.

DAVIES, REV. JOHN HENRY.—Late Exhib. of Jesus Coll., Oxford; math. honours in mods., 1876, and in finals, 1878; B.A., 1879; M.A., 1881; D., 1879; P., 1880 St. D.; curate of Aberystwyth, 1879-86; curate in charge of Merthyr, Carmarthenshire, 1886; curate of St. Martin, Birmingham, 1887-88; author of "Letters on Christianity and Mahommedanism," "Intermediate Education," &c., &c.; col. chaplain, Accra, Gold Coast; secy. to Board of Education, registrar of deaths, and manager of govt. schools.

DAVIES, HON. LOUIS HENRY.—Born Charlottetown (P.E.I.), May 4, 1845; called to the Bar, 1866; Q.C. in 1880; he was one of the Canadian counsel before the International Fishery Commission at Halifax in 1877; solicitor-general of P.E.I. Island, 1869, and again in 1872-73; led the Opposition in P.E.I. Legislature until Sept., 1876, when he became premier and attorney-general; resigned in March, 1879, and was defeated at the general elections which followed; he was first returned to the House of Commons at the general elections in 1882, and has since sat for Queen's; minister of marine and fisheries in Mr. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.

DAVIES, THE HON. SIR MATTHEW HENRY, KT., BACH. (1890).—Solicitor; late mayor of Prahran; member exec. com. Victoria, minister without portfolio, Feb., 1886; speaker leg. ass., 1887-92; vice-president and an executive commissioner for the centennial international exhibition, 1888.

DAVIS, NICHOLAS DARNELL, C.M.G. (1895).—Was for some years in Grenada, in the private sec.'s office; private sec. to Administrator Baynes for a short time. In British Guiana served as a clerk in several departments; Aug., 1870, to Feb., 1871, secretary to the commissioners of inquiry into the treatment of immigrants; secretary to the royal commissioners of inquiry into the treatment of immigrants in Mauritius, Feb., 1872; commandant, British Sherbro, Sierra Leone, 1874; postmaster-general, British Guiana, 1876; acted as receiver-general, Dec., 1880, to Sept., 1881; comptroller of customs, 1st Oct., 1881; acting auditor-general, a member of the court of policy, and senior commissioner of the Vlissingen Estate, 1882; member, court of policy, again in 1886.
DAVIS, S. SPRINGER.—Extra Rev. offr. St. Kitts, 1893; 4th ditto, 1895.

DAVIS, SOLOMON S.—3rd clerk, customs, Lagos, October, 1871; acting second clerk of customs, February, 1874; acting chief clerk and warehouse keeper, September, 1875; 1st clerk, customs and treasury, 1877.

DAVSON, CHARLES SIMON, B.A., LL.B.—Educated at Westminster and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; called to the bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1881; admitted to bar of British Guiana, 1882; acted as solicitor-general, 1887, 1888, and 1890, and June, 1891 to Nov. 1892; stipendiary magistrate, 1888.

DAWSON, A. R.—Writer, Ceylon civil service 1866; police magistrate, &c., Point Pedro, May, 1867; assistant government agent, Colombo, June, 1868; police magistrate, &c., Jaffna, Jan., 1869; district judge, Kegalla, June, 1873, assistant government agent, Kegalla, 1875; acting assistant government agent, Ratnapura, 1876; assistant agent, Mannar, 1878; grain commissioner, 1879; assistant government agent, Puttalam, Jan., 1885; principal assistant, colonial secretary, 1886; govt. agt., W.P., 1890.

DAWSON, GEORGE MERCER, C.M.G. (1892), D.S., F.G.S.—Assistant director and geologist, Geological Survey of Canada, July, 1883; director, 1895; entered the Canadian service, July, 1875; joint commissioner with Sir G. Baden Powell on the Behring's Sea Seal Fishery, 1891-2.

DAWSON, JOHN EUGENE, F.P.S., F.R.G.S.—Educated at the Catholic Institution, Freetown, and at King's College, London; extra writer, governor's office, Sierra Leone, 1878; asst. clerk of police court, 1879; 3rd clerk, colonial secretary's dept., 1881; 2nd clerk, 1881; chief clerk, 1889.

DAWSON, SIR JOHN WILLIAM, LL.D., F.R.S., F.G.S., KT. BACH (1884), C.M.G. (1881).—Principal and vice-chancellor of McGill University Montreal; president, R. S. Canada, 1882; president American Association for Advancement Sc., 1882-3; president, British Assoch., 1886; author of many memoirs and books on geology; retired 1893.
DAY, H.R.A.—Cadet, Sarawak service, July, 1886; asst. result., June, 1893; resdt. 2nd class, 3rd divn., Mar., 1895.

DEAKIN, THE HON. ALFRED.—Commissir. of public works, and minis. of water supply, Victoria, Mar. 8, 1883; and also vice-presid., b.d. of land and works; solicitor-general, Nov. 13, 1883; president of Royal Commission on water supply, Dec., 1884; chief secretary and minister of water supply, 1886; resigned Nov. 1890; senior delegate to Colonial conference, 1887.

DEALTRY, W., C.M.G. (1881).—Educated at Eton and Trinity College, Cambridge; clerk in the office of the secretary of state for the colonies, April, 1837; assistant clerk, April, 1845; senior clerk, May, 1867; principal clerk, 30th September, 1872; chief clerk, 1879; retired, 30th June, 1879.

DEALY, THOMAS KIRKMAN.—Educated at St. Mary's College, Hammersmith, Marquis of Rutland's first prize and a double first (head of each division) 1880; matriculated (first division) Lord Lytton's 1883; first B.A. (first division) 1884; head-master, St. Mary's, Derby, 1881-84; asst. master, Manchester Central School, now Victoria Coll., Hong Kong, 1884; Hon. Examiner in Physics to college of Medicine for Chinese, 1888; passed 2nd stage higher grade Chinese, Aug., 1890; ag. registrar, Victoria Coll., April, 1890, to Sept., 1891; again April, 1894.

DEANE, W.M., C.M.G., 1870.—R.A., Trin. Hall, Camb., 1862; M.A., 1866; student interpreter, Hong Kong, Mar. 28, 1862; extra A.D.C. to Sir Henry Robinson, 1864; passed as interpreter, 1865; secretary to administrator, 1865; J.P., 1865; registrar-general, June, 1865; captain superintendent of police, July, 1868; wounded in duty, 1878; acting colonial treasurer, 1881; colonial secretary, 1881 and 1882; mem. of leg. coun., 1887, and of leg. coun., 1888; 1892.

DE BOUCHERVILLE, ANATOLE.—Fourth clerk, Registry Supreme Court, March, 1868; confirmed Dec., 1868; clerk, registration and mortgage department, Jan., 1869; inspector of Roman Catholic aided schools, 1872; supt. of schools, July to Oct., 1886; ag. junct. coll. sec., April to July, 1889; ag. regis.-gen. June to Oct., 1895.
DE CELLES, ALFRED DUCLOS.—Librarian of Parliament, Canada, 1885.

DE CHARMOY, L. G. J. DEEMER.—Admitted an attorney-at-law, supreme court of Mauritius, May, 1879; clerk to the puisne of the supreme court, April, 1884; also interpreter procurer general's department; 1st class clerk, registr. sup. ct.; ag. ass'tant, ins. of bankruptcy, Feb., 1890, to Sept., 1891, and April to May, 1895.
DEERING, JOHN W.—Entered survey department, South Australia, 1855; surveyor, New South Wales, 1860; district surveyor, Metropolitan District, New South Wales, 1872.

DE GRAST, RUDOLPH.—Called to the bar, Middle Temple, June, 1884; elected sub-recept., combined ct., Br. Guiana, 1887 and 1889; ag. stip. mag. W. Coast dist., Demerara, 1891 to Nov., 1892; dist. commissar. G. P. G., June, 1893; master sup. ct., and registr. sup. Leonie, May, 1895; ag. dep. judge, June, 1895; ag. pol. mag., supt. of pol., &c., st. times; soi.-gen. S. Leonie, May, 1895; ag. ass't gen., Sept., 1895.

DEIGHTON, H., M.A., F.R.A.S.—Former scholar of Queen's Coll., Camb., 21st wrt., 1854; 2nd class in moral science trip, 1856; principal, Queen's Collegiate School, Truro, 1859; principal of Harrison College, Bath, Aug., 1872; sent in 1879 to inspect the school report on the education of Grenada; examined reported on the Government Grammar School, St. Vincent in 1885; and received the thanks of the secretary of state for the colonies.

DE LA BRUERE, P. B.—Speaker, legis. council, Quebec; editor of the "Courier de la Hyacinthe;" has written several historical political pamphlets; was one of the chief pres. of the dairymen's association of prov. Quebec; has been its president since its formation in 1872; was also one of the chief factors in the establishment of root beer sugar factories in Canada; cal. comm. of Quebec in 1877; speaker of the house and member of the cabinet in 1882.

DELAFAYE, AUXILE LOUIS VICTOR, Q.C.—admitted to the Bar, Middle Temple, 1863; municipal councillor, St. Louis, 1870; provost judge, Mauritius, Mar., 1879; member, prison board, 1884; member, civil service enquiry commission, 1884; member, civil service commission, 1884-1895; served as puisne judge sup. ct. in 1884, 1885, and July, 1890, to July, 1892; Q.C. Aug., 1892; continued as puisne judge, Nov., 1894.
DELAKONNY, ALBERT.—Volunteer Engineers' Railway Workshops, Mauritius, 1st April, 1868; goods clerk, Aug., 1873.

DE LA MARRE, FRANCIS.—Inspector of police, Mauritius, Aug., 1864; poor law guardian, and acting sanitary warden, Plaines Wilhelms District, in addition to police duties, Jan., 1874; inspector of emigrants, Jan., 1875; acting British consul for union, Nov., 1877—June, 1879; emigration agent, Calcutta, for Mauritius and Fiji, April, 1884; emigration agent for Mauritius, 1884.

DE LIVERA, F. J.—Writer, Ceylon civil service, 1868; acting magistrate, Panadure, 1869; police magistrate, Balapitimodara, 1870; acting magistrate, Jaffna, Mar., 1873; acting commissioner of requests, Colombo, May, 1873; police magistrate, Jaffna, June, 1873; acting district judge, Ratnapura, 1875; acting police magistrate, Colombo, 1878; confirmed, 1879; district judge, Colombo, 1880; commissioner of requests, Colombo, Jan., 1886; police magistrate, Colombo, Nov., 1886; district judge, Batticaloa, Nov., 1888; ditto Jaffna, Dec., 1891; ditto Jaffna, Oct., 1895; ditto Galle, Dec., 1895.

DE MOLE, GEO. EDWARD.—Assistant marine surveyor, S. Australia, June, 1867; secretary, marine board, June, 1862; secretary to minister of justice and education, July, 1874; secretary, attorney-general, commissioner of patents, registrar of copyrights and trade marks, July, 1879.

DEMPSTER, T. ERSKINE.—Educated at Gladstone House Academy, Jersey, and Hartley Institute, Southampton; entered the "non-colonial commission," Bengal, 1872, as extra assistant commissioner, Hayaribagh; in 1874, civil duty during Bengal famine, deputy magistrate and deputy collector, Durbhanga; chief in charge treasury judicial and revenue dept., 1875, extra assistant commissioner, Nyakawaka; Southall, Pergunnahs; the same year civil duty as assistant settlement officer during Natal settlement, determining measurements and adjudicating land disputes; 1876, subdivisional officer in charge Jauntara, Southall, Pergunnahs; stipendiary mag. Mauritius, 1877.

DENNEHY, CHARLES, M.R.C.S.I., L.A.R.C.S.I., A.M., Rotunda, Dublin, F.R.C.S. (Edin.), 1883.—medical officer, St. Mary's District, Antigua, Jan., 1871; medical officer and registrar, St. Philips, Aug., 1875; colonial surgeon, St. Lucia, May, 1877; health officer, Castries, Sept., 1877; principal medical officer for immigration, 1878; J.P., 1885.

DENTON, GEORGE CHARDIN, C.M.G. (1891).—Ensign, 57th Regt., Oct., 1869; lieutenant, May, 1871; lieutenant, Aug., 1876; captain, Jan., 1878; chief of police, St. Vincent, April, 1880; appointed one of the commissioners to enquire into the police force, Barbados, Oct., 1880; member of executive council, Oct., 1881; represented St. Vincent at the telegraphic conference at Barbados, May, 1882; administrator, St. Vincent, May to July, 1885; again in 1886, and from Nov., 1887, to Mar., 1888; acting col. sec., 1886 to 1888; col. sec., Lagos, Mar., 1888; admin. govt., 1889, 1890, 1891, 1893, and 1895.

DE PETRI, EMILIO.—Entered the Malta civil service, after competitive examination, as clerk, Dec., 1857; assistant secretary to government, and clerk to the council of government, Jan., 1886; acting chief secretary, May to Sept., 1886, Oct. to Dec., 1887, June to Oct., 1890, July to Sept., 1892; rec-gen. and director of contracts, July, 1894, with a seat in exec. coun. and in coun. of govt.
DE PIRO, GIUSEPPE LORENZO, MARCHESINO, C.M.G. (1887).—Late hon. secretary, Committee of Privileges, Maltese Nobility.

DERBY, 16TH EARL OF (England, created 1845); BARON STANLEY, 1832 (United Kingdom); BART. 1627 (England); BARON STANLEY OF PRESTON, 1886; G.C.B. (1886); A.D.C.—THE RIGHT HON. FREDERICK ARTHUR STANLEY, younger son of the 14th Earl of Derby; born 1841; ed. at Eton; entd. the Grenadier Guards in 1858, and was appointed lieutenant and captain in 1862; retired from the army, and entered Parliament as member for Preston in 1865; was a Lord of the Admiralty from Aug. to Dec., 1868; elected member for North Lancashire at the general election of 1868, and again in 1874, 1880, and 1885; was financial secretary for war from Feb., 1874, to Aug., 1877, when he was appointed financial secretary to the treasury; Mar., 1878, secretary of state for war, which office he held till the retirement of Lord Beaconsfield's administration in 1880; secretary of state for the colonies, June, 1885, to Feb., 1886; resigned Feb., 1886; president of the Board of Trade in Lord Salisbury's second administration, Aug., 1886; governor-general of Canada, 1888-93; succeeded his brother as Earl of Dorby, 1893; Lord Mayor of Liverpool, 1895.
DERHAM, THE HON. FREDERICK THOMAS.—Postmaster-General, Victoria, 18th Feb., 1886-90.

DESARAM, J. H.—Writer to the government of Ceylon, 1865; police magistrate of Balapitimo-dara, 1866; district judge, Badulla, Oct., 1867; commissioner of requests, Colombo, 1868; district judge, Kurunegala, 1875; regr. gen., Colombo, 1886; dist. judge, Kalutara, Dec., 1883; regr.-gen., May, 1886; dist. judge, Jaffna, Aug., 1891; ditto Galle, Dec., 1891; ditto Kandy, Aug., 1893.

DESHON, HARRY FITZGERALD.—Cadet, Sarawak civil service, Feb., 1876; A.D.C. to H.H. the Rajah, G.C.M.G., Sep., 1882; resett., 2nd class, 3rd divn., Jan., 1883; resett., 4th divn. and member supreme council, June, 1892; resett. 3rd divn., July, 1896.

DESHON, EDWARD.—Member of land board, Queensland; ensign in 68th Light Infantry, 6th June, 1854; lieutenant, 1st Dec., 1854; served with the 68th Light Infantry at siege and fall of Sebastopol, from 14 Nov., 1854, to the end of the war; medal and clasp, and Turkish medal; instructor of musketry to the 68th Light Infantry from Nov., 1857, to Oct., 1861; passed competitive examination for admission to Staff College, in July, 1861; retired by sale of commission in Oct., 1861; auditor gen., Queensland, 1890.

DESMARAIS, HENRY EUGENE.—Called to the bar, Middle Temple, April, 1866; police magistrate, Rodrigues, June, 1875; district judge and stipendiary magistrate, Seychelles, Nov., 1879; acting chief civil commissioner on several occasions; acting magistrate, Moka, April to Sept., 1882; district magistrate, Moka, Aug., 1884; acting police and additional magistrate, Port Louis, Nov., 1884; district magistrate, Moka, Dec., 1885; acting junior district magistrate, Port Louis, May to Oct., 1886; Mar., 1887, to Jan., 1888; district and stipendiary magistrate, Moka, July, 1889.

DE SMIDT, HENRY, F.S.S.—Permanent under colonial secretary of the colony of Cape of Good Hope, and accounting officer, colonial secretary's department. Holds in addition the offices of controller of printing and stationery, member of tender board, and custodian of the main stock of stamps. Was specially charged with the duties of director of the census of 1891, on the successful accomplishment of which, received the congratulations of the government, and was awarded an honorarium of £500, specially voted by parliament. Entered the service in Mar., 1865, as a clerk in the colonial office, in which department he has served ever since. Elected fellow of Royal Statistical Society, 1892.
DE VEITTEUIL, SIR LOUIS ANTOINE AIMÉ, K.C.M.G. (1895), C.M.G. (1889).—Senior unofficial member of the legislative council, Trinidad.
DE VILLIERS, J. N. P.—Civil commissioner and resident magistrate, Victoria West, Cape of Good Hope, 1st Sept., 1878.

DE VILLIERS, Sir JOHN, KNT. BACH. (1877), K.C.M.G. (1882).—Chief justice of the supreme court, Cape of Good Hope, 1874; attorney-general from 1872 to 1874; for several years member of the legislative assembly; president of the legislative council and member of the council of the university of the Cape of Good Hope in 1873; one of the royal commission for the settlement of the affairs of the Transvaal 1881; represented the Cape at the Colonial conclave, Ottawa, 1894.

DEW, ARTHUR TOMKYNS.—Naval cadet. 1867; took part in the expeditions to the Linggi and Lukut Rivers in 1874 (Indian medal, with Perak clasp); left the Navy, 1876; magistrate and collr., Perak, 1881; superintendent of the Bindings, 1884; deputy master attendant, shipping master, &c. Singapore, 1886; is a J.P.; mag. for Singapore and Penang, commissioner Ct. of Requests, S.S. mag. and collr., and hrbr. mr. Matang and Port Weld, Perak, 1888.
DEWDNEY, THE HON. EDGAR.—Lieutenant-governor of North West Territories, 1881; minister of interior, Canada, 1888; lieut.-governor, Br. Columbia, 1892.

DE WET, THE HON. SIR JACOBUS ALBERTUS, K.C.M.G. (1890).—Member Cape house of assembly and exec. council; minister for native affairs, 1884; British Resdt., S.A. Republic, June, 1890; retired on pension, Sept., 1896.

DE WET, SIR JACOBUS PETRUS, KNT., 1883.—educated at the South African College, Capetown, and University College, London (B.A., 1860); barrister, Inner Temple, 1863; solicitor-general, Cape, 1873; recorder, Griqualand West, 1878; chief justice, Transvaal, 1880 to 1881; acting chief justice of Ceylon, 1882 to 1883, when he retired.

DE WINTON, MAJOR-GENERAL SIR FRANCIS WALTER, R.A., G.C.M.G. (1893), K.C.M.G. (1884), C.M.G. (1882).—Entered Royal Artillery in 1854; served in the Crimea, and was military attache to the embassy at Constantinople, 1877-8; minister secy. to the governor-gen. of Canada, 1878 to 1880; administrator-general, Congo Free State, 1884; commander, Order of Leopold, 1886; commissioner expedition against the Yonnies, 1887; commissioner inquire into Swazi affairs, 1889; comptroller of the household of the Duke of York, Feb., 1892.

DE WOLF, JAN. A.—Medical offr., St. Joseph, Trinidad, 1876; ag. health offr. of shipping, inspr. of immigrants, Pt. of Spain, 1877-8; 1883-4; ag. surg.-gen., Aug., 1884, to June, 1885; health offr. of shipping, med. inspr. of immigrants &c., Aug., 1890.
DEWS, ALBERT, M.A.—Classical master, Rington Coll., Barbados, 1893.

D'HOTMAN, HENRI JULES JOSEPH.—Mauritius civil service in 1865 as volunteer, curer's general's department; March, 1865, examination, and appointed extra mechanic (same office); 1866, acting jurist or clerk, magistracy office; 1867, acting clerk, police court; May, 1868, acting assistant-clerk, magistracy's office; second clerk, magistracy, Plaines Williams; 1876, acting chief clerk; August, 1876, registrar and clerk (same office); 1878, clerk and interpreter to R.M., Inanda, Natal, 1878; secy. pollution commission, 1880; clerk of court, 1884; registrar, Victoria county, 1884; clerk, R.M. Durban, 1887; 1st clerk to R.M. registrar, circuit court, Mar., 1891.
DIAS, FELIX REGINALD, M.A., L.L.M.—Trinity Hall, Camb.—Called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1887; pol. mag. and commr. of requests, Ceylon, Oct., 1889; crown counsel for the island, July, 1893.
DIAS, SIR HARRY, KNT. BACH., 1893.—Barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1847; jun. puisne judge, Ceylon, 5th July, 1879; retired, July, 1892.
DIAS, W., M.D., M.R.C.S., L.S.A., L.M.S.—colonial surgeon, Ceylon, 14th Oct., 1892.

DIBBS, THE HON. SIR GEORGE RICHARD, K.C.M.G., (1892)—Colonial treas., New South Wales, Jan., 1883, to Oct., 1885; premier and colonial secy., Oct., 1885; treas. and premier, Dec., 1885; colonial secy. and premier, Feb., 1886 to Feb., 1887, and Jan. and Feb., 1889; premier and colonial secy., 1891-4; one of the representatives of N.S.W. at federation convention, 1891.

DICK, GEO. ROYER, M.A.—Educated Pat Coll., Mauritius, and Gonville and Caius Coll. (late Fellow); called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Mar., term 1869; professor of mathematics in Pat Coll., Mauritius, 1879; registrar-gen. for member, leg. coun., 1890; census commissioner, 1890; ag. auditor-gen., 1893; ag. rec-gen., to Oct., 1895, and April, 1896.
DICK, JOHN NORMAN.—Govt. engineer, Penang, 1888; ag. Govt. Marine Surveyor, June, 1895, to June, 1896.

DICK, THE HON. THOMAS.—Colonial secy., New Zealand, 5th Mar., 1880; and still holding office, minister of education, 15th October, minister of justice, 23rd April, 1881, and master-general and commissioner of post, 21st April, 1882, resigning ministry of post, resigned post office and telegraphs, 11th Oct., 1882.

DICKEN, CHARLES SHORTT, C.M.G. (1893).—Educated at Charterhouse; ensign 87th Royal Fusiliers, July, 1859; lieutenant, 1861; first certificate, school of musketry, Hythe, Nov., 1861; retired in 1861; acting sub-inspector of of the government, and ex officio member of council, Jan., 1854; chief justice and President of the Court of Appeal, 1880; vice-president of council of govt., 1893; retired, 1894.

DIX, THOMAS H.—Supernumerary clerk, colonial secretary's office, St. Lucia, Jan., 1862; acting chief excise department, May, 1867; acting stipendiary magistrate, Jan., 1871; magistrate, Dominica, Sept., 1871; stipendiary magistrate, 2nd district, St. Lucia, May, 1872; is ex officio judge of the court of requests, chairman poor law committee and district coroner, member of road committee; called to the bar Jan., 1879; member leg. comm., 1883; is local corresponding agent for Imperial Institute; promoted to 1st district, Nov., 1892; was chairman quarantine bd., Nov., 1892, to Aug., 1894.

DOBIE, EDWARD DAVID.—Crown solicitor, Tasmania (also clerk of the peace and registrar of building societies), May, 1887; barrister, &c., supreme court, Tasmania, admitted July, 1882; joined civil service, Tasmania, Mar., 1883; was parliamentary draftsman and afterwards solicitor-general from 25th Mar. to 30th May, 1887.

DOBELL, HON. RICHARD REID.—Born at Liverpool, 1837, his father being a prominent merchant of that city; went to Quebec in 1857, and, entering into the lumber business, became the head of one of the largest firms in the country; elected member of House of Commons for Quebec West, and became member of Mr. Laurier's govt. (without portfolio), June, 1896.

DOBSON, ALFRED.—Called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1873; member, legislative assembly, Tasmania, 1877-87; attorney-general and member executive council, 1877; speaker, legislative assembly, July, 1885; solicitor-general, June, 1887; member of council of education, and church advocate of the diocese of Tasmania.

DOBSON, THE HON. SIR WILLIAM LAMBERT, K.T. BACH. (1886).—Called to the bar of the Middle Temple, June 6, 1856; attorney-general, Tasmania, Feb. 11, 1861; also member of the executive council; puisne judge, 1870; chief justice, 1885.

DOBSON, THE HON. FRANK STANLEY, B.A., LL.D. (Camb.).—Barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, Q.C., F.L.S. (Lond.), M.I.C., Victoria, since 1870; member executive council; solicitor-general, July, 1881, to Mar., 1883; lecturer on law, Melbourne University; trustee Melbourne public library and national gallery; official visitor of the observatory; chairman of committees, leg. comm.
DOCKER, E. R.—District Court judge, New South Wales, 7th June, 1884.
DODD, J. H.—Engineer, Eastern District, Jamaica, 4th Dec., 1880.

DODDS, THE HON. JOHN STOKELL, C.M.G. (1889).—Attorney-general, Tasmania, 20 Dec., 1878, to 29 Oct., 1879, and in the Giblin ministry, 30 Oct., 1879; colonial treasurer from 1st Dec., 1881, to 15th Aug., 1884; attorney-general in the Douglas ministry from 15th Aug., 1884, to 8th Mar., 1886, also premier, Mar. to Dec., 1886; judge, supreme court, 1886; delegate, colonial conference, 1887.
DODDS, WILLIAM JOHN, M.D. (Edin.), 1879, gold medallist, M.B. and C.M., 1876, 1st class honours, D.Sc., 1878.—Inspector of asylums and med. supt. of new asylum, Mowbray, Cape, 1889.
DOHERTY, C. J.—Puisne judge, superior court, Quebec, 1891.
DONNAN, J.—Master attendant, Colombo, Ceylon, 1863; was commander of government steamers "Manchester" and "Pearl," from July, 1859.
DONNELLY, Hon. W. J. S.—Surveyor-general, Newfoundland, to 1882; receiver-general, 1882; resigned, 1889; M.L.A. for Placentia, 1893; receiver-general and member exec. coun., April to December, 1894.

DONOUGHMORE, 5th Earl of, K.C.M.G., (1879)—John Luke George Hely Hutchinson, educated at Balliol College, Oxford; second class in law and modern history, 1870; honorary private secretary to the Earl of Carnarvon, May, 1876, to Feb., 1878. Deputy-Lieutenant for the county of Tipperary. Assistant-commissioner to Sir H. Drummond Wolff, G.C.M.G., H.M.'s commissioner on the European commission for the organization of Eastern Roumelia under the 18th Article of the Treaty of Berlin in 1878.

DONOVAN, Justin Foley, M.D., M.Ch., L.M.—Medical officer and health officer, Kilkeagh dispensary, co. Cork, 1876 to 1879; surgeon, R.N., 1879; received the thanks of the admiralty, 1882 and 1888, "for zeal and devotion to duty" at R.N. hospitals, Port Royal and Malta; and of the medical director general, Royal Navy, for professional work, 1882, 1884 and 1886. Retired from the navy 1889; medical attendant, lepers' home, public general hospital and constabulary training depot, Spanish Town, Jamaica, 1889; medical examiner, civil service, widows and orphans pension fund, 1890.

DOUGLAS, The Hon. John, C.M.G. (1877).—Born 1828; educated at Rugby and Durham University; has filled many important political offices in Queensland, including those of agent general and prime minister; special commissioner for British New Guinea, 1885-8, when he returned to duty as resident magistrate, Thursday Island.
DOUGLAS-DE-FENZI, C. W. P.—Clerk assistant and librarian, Natal, leg. council, Feb., 1889; clerk, leg. council, 1893.
DOW, The Hon. John Lamont.—Minister of lands and agriculture, president board of lands and works, Victoria, Feb., 1886-90.

DOWNER, The Hon. Sir J. W., K.C.M.G. (1887), Q.C.—Attorney-general, South Australia, 24 June, 1881, to 16 June, 1884, and att.-gen. and premier, 1885-7; delegate to Colonial Conference, 1887; chief secretary and premier, 1892-3.

DOWNES, Major-Gen. M. Francis, C.M.G. (1885), late R.A.—Retired, Oct., 1884; served in the Crimea till June, 1856, medal and clasp and Turkish medal; instructor of fortification, R.M. Coll., Sandhurst, for two years; commanded artillery in Mauritius and St. Helena for four years; instructor of gunnery at Woolwich, five years; colonel commandant, South Australia, June, 1877; member of Royal commission on defences, Sydney, 1881; secretary of defence, Victoria, April, 1885, to Mar., 1888; commandant, S. Australian forces, April, 1888-92.

DOWSON, R. W.—Accountant, Mauritius railways, 19th Sept., 1867; junr. audit. examiner, June, 1878; chief clerk rly. dept., April, 1882; ag. asst. manager, July, 1884, to April, 1885; head agent, Sept., 1891; ag. gen. manager, July to Sept., 1896.

DOYLE, E. A. Gaynes, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lond.; Obstet. Soc., Lond.; Memb. Br. Med. Assoc.; late assist. house surg., Westminster Hosp.; joined medical service, Trinidad, 1884; ag. dist. med. offr., 1886; ag. res. surg., San Fernando Hosp., 1889 and 1892; confirmed Nov., 1892; ag. res. surg. Col. Hosp., Pt. of Spain, 1893.
DRAGE, Fred. S.—Cadet Sarawak Service, Jan., 1886; result, 2nd class, 3rd divn., June, 1887.
DRAYSON, Matthew John.—Appointed after competitive examination, a clerk in the lower (now second) division of the civil service, and assigned to the colonial office, 16th May, 1883.

DRAYTON, Edward Rawle.—Clerk, colonial secretary's office, Barbados, Sept., 1878; second clerk in office of governor-in-chief of the Windward Islands, July, 1879; acting chief clerk, Mar., 1880, and May, 1881, to Jan., 1882; chief clerk, Jan., 1882; acting colonial secretary, Grenada, April, 1883; private secretary to Major-General Browne, July to Oct., 1884, provost marshal, Canada, April, 1885; acting colonial registrar, Sept., 1885; registrar supreme court, J.P., July, 1886; member leg. coun., May, 1887; inspnr. of prisons, Mar., 1888; member exec. coun., Nov., 1888; treasurer, May, 1890; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1890; June, 1892; administered govt. in 1890, 1893, 1894, and 1895, and govt. of St. Vincent, June, 1893; col. sec. and regtr. gen., Jan., 1892.

DREW, W. L. G., C.M.G. (1890).—Served in Royal Navy, Feb., 1842, to June, 1856; subsequently employed under the government of N. S. Wales, under treasurer of Queensland, Feb., 1867; auditor-general, Oct., 1877; chairman Civil Service Board, 1890; holds the rank of fleet paymaster, R.N.

DRIVER, J.—Ed. at St. Mark's College (Chelsea) and Owen's College (Man.).—Matric. L.C. Univ., 1878; Int. B.A., 1880; head master, Government school, Seychelles, Jan., 1891; auditor, inset schools, and secretary of councils, Sept., 1891.

DRURY, Albert Victor.—Superintendent, colonial secretary's office, Queensland, Mar., 1887; transferred to executive council, Oct., 1887; clerk to executive council, Jan., 1887; private secretary to Gov. Sir W. W. Cairns, 1875-7; and to Albert Sir A. H. Palmer, May to Dec., 1886.

DUBERLY, Frederic.—Educated at Chelsea College, served in Eastern Extension Telegraph Company, and Straits Settlements, July, 1878, to July, 1882, when appointed cadet in the service of the British North Borneo Company; returned May, 1883; chief clerk treasury, Perak, Nov., 1883; assistant-collector and magistrate, Krian, July, 1883; ag. collr. land rev., Larut, July, 1887; ass't to sec. to govt., June, 1892; ag. commissioner of lands, April, 1894; dist. mag. K. Kangsar, Feb., 1895; but continued to act as commissioner of lands.

BUFF, Benjamin.—Postmaster, Swellendam, Cape, April, 1863; telegraphist in charge of Swellendam, July, 1864; chief clerk and accountant, telegraph department, Mar., 1875; in active service in Transkei, as lieu. P.A.O.'s Artillery, Nov., 1880, to April, 1881; acting general manager of telegraphs, June, 1881, to Feb., 1884; chief clerk, postal department, and supdt. of telegraphs, Jan., 1886; acting secretaries, general post office, June, 1890, to Jan., 1891; secretary, general post office, and supdt. of telegraphs, 1892. Is member of the institution of electrical engineers.

BUFF, Grant, The Rt. Hon. M. E., P.C., G.C.M.G.; C.I.E., F.R.S. (1880).—Educated at Balliol College, Oxford, B.A. 1850; under-secretary of state for India, 1868 to 1874; lord rector of Aberdeen University, 1866 to 1872; parliamentary under-secretary of state for the colonies, April 29, 1880, to Aug. 5, 1881; M.P. for Eden district, 1857 to 1881; governor of Madras, 1881-6.

FF, ROBERT.—Record clerk, immigration department, British Guiana, April, 1883; 4th clerk, Oct., 1884; 3rd clerk, Oct., 1885; first class clerk in Hindu-tai, Mar., 1885; 2nd clerk, 1893; chief clerk, Jan., 1894; has acted as immigr. agt. on several occasions, and also as sub immigr. agt.

UFFERIN AND AVA, 1st MARQUESS OF (created 1876); Viscount Clandeboye, 1871 (United Kingdom); Baron Dufferin, 1880; Baron Clandeboye (Ireland); Baron Clandeboye, 1850 (United Kingdom); Bart., 1863; Privy Councillor, 1868; K.P., 1863; G.C.M.G. (1876); G.C.B. (civil), G.M.S.I.; G.M.I.E.; Frederick Temple Clinton Blackwood, appointed governor-general of Canada, and governor of Prince Edward Island, 1872; Ambassador at the Court at St. Petersburg, Feb., 1879; ambassador at Constantinople, proceeded to Egypt on special service, 1882; admiral of Ulster, 1884; viceroy of India, 1885; chancellor of the Royal University, Ireland, 1886; ambassador to Italy, 1888; ambassador and warden of the Cinque Ports, 1891-1896.
FIELD, THOMAS.—Entered Crown Lands Office, S. Australia, Mar., 1867; chief clerk, July, 1867; secy. to commissioner of crown lands, Mar., 1869.

UFFY, THE HON. SIR CHARLES GAVAN, M.G. (1877). (K.T. BACH, 1873).—Was called to Irish bar in 1845, but was early engrossed in politics as editor of the "Nation" newspaper, one of the leaders of the national party known as Young Ireland. Was a state prisoner along with O'Connell in 1844, and Smith-O'Brien in 1848. Was member for New Ross in 1852, defeating Thomas Redington, chief secretary for Ireland. Retained his seat and emigrated to Australia at the commencement of 1856, and practised for a short time at the bar in Melbourne. Was elected a member of the first parliament of Victoria, and in 1857 became minister of public works in the first responsible government. In 1858 became president of the council of land and works. In 1861 was recalled to the same office. In 1867, after two years' absence in Europe, was re-elected to Parliament for the first time, and shortly afterwards was appointed chairman of a royal commission to devise the best means for effecting a federation of the Australian states. In 1871 became prime minister; and in the same year was chairman of an intercolonial conference of Australian cabinet ministers to urge on the colonial office the removal of restrictions on intercolonial legislation, which change has since been effected. In 1876, after two years' absence in Europe, was again re-elected to Parliament by one of the largest constituencies in the colony without personally visiting the district, and on the assembly of a new parliament in May, 1877, was unanimously chosen to the office of speaker. After three years' service he resigned, in order to return to Europe. Has since published "Young Ireland—a Fragment of History," "Four Years of Irish History," and "The League of North and South."

UFFY, THE HON. JOHN GAVAN.—Eldest son of the above (Sir Charles Gavan Duffy). Educated at Stonyhurst. Vice-chancellor's prize, English essay, Melbourne Univ. Succeeded his father in presentation of Dalhousie in legislative assembly of Victoria, 1874. President of board of land and works of Victoria, 1889; postmaster-general of Victoria, 1890; also attorney-general, 1892; member of cabinet without portfolio, 1892; resigned, Jan., 1893; again postmaster-general, 1894.
DUFFY, WILLIAM.—Engineer of roads, Tasmania, 13th February, 1883.

DUGGAN, CHARLES WILLIAM.—Insp. of Police, Hong Kong, Oct., 1869; 2nd clerk, police department, 1874; 1st clerk and acct., 1881; was assistant master police school, Oct., 1877, to May, 1882; acting chief clerk col. secy's. office, April, 1891.

DUMARESQ, JOHN D'AVERGE.—Extra clerk, governor's office, Natal, Feb., 1880; 3rd clerk, registrar general's office, April, 1880; 3rd clerk, colonial secretary's office, Feb., 1881; 2nd clerk, Jan., 1882; 1st clerk, Jan., 1888; registrar to special judicial commission, Zululand, 1889; acting clerk, exec. council, Natal, 1889; 1st clerk regtr. of deeds dept., 1889.

DUNCAN, ANDREW H. F. (late Lieut. R.N.)—Joined R.N., July, 1868; served in Mediterranean, S. America, Cape, India, Australia, and West Coast of Africa; resigned Nov., 1883; passed examination in theoretical survey, July, 1883, and practical survey, 1884, at the Cape Univ., in honours (first of his year on both occasions); from April, 1884, employed by the surveyor-general of the Cape, and in private practice; member, land commission of Br. Bechuanaland, Sept., 1885; surveyor-general of that colony, July, 1886; surveyor-general to the Br. S. Africa Company, 1891.

DUNCOMBE, H. FEVRENSHAM.—Pay clk. army pay off, commissariat, Bahamas, 1889; 2nd master, Nassau gram. schl., 1890; 4th clk. treas., 1891; 3rd ditto, 1895; ag. dist. justice and collr. of rev. in various districts in 1895 and 1896; J.P., 1896.
DUNEDIN, 2nd BISHOP OF (founded 1854).—Rt. REV. SAMUEL T. NEVILLE, D.D.—Appointed, 1871.
DUNKERLEY, REV. W. H., M.A.—Educated Pembroke Coll., Oxon.; col. chaplain, Malacca, 1891; ag. col. chaplain, Singapore, May to Oct., 1892, and May, 1896.

DUNLOP, C. E.—Writer, Ceylon civil service, April, 1871; police magistrate, Galadara, 1873; ditto, Kalpitiya and Puttalam, 1874; landing surveyor, Galle, 1874; office assistant to government agent Eastern Province, 1877; district judge, Badulla, 1878; office assistant to government agent, N.C. Province, 1881; assistant collector of customs, Galle, 1883; deputy ditto Colombo, 1884; dist. judge, Tangalla, 1887; ditto, Kalutara, 1885.

DUNLOP, COLONEL SAMUEL, R.A., C.M.G. (1884)—Acting commissioner of police, S. S., Nov., 1870; acting police magistrate and commissr. of Ct. of Requests, P.W., April, 1871; acting deputy commissioner of police and coroner, Apr., 1871; acting deputy commissioner of police, Singapore, fire commissioner and deputy coroner, Aug., 1871; acting inspector-general of police, Dec., 1872; commissioner to Perak, Jan., 1874. In Nov., 1874, commissioner with the forces sent to quell the disturbances in the native state of Sungai Ujong; inspector-general, Straits police, May, 1875. In Nov., 1875, on the murder of Mr. Birch, appointed special commissioner (temporarily) for Perak affairs; organized the expedition which captured the Passir Salak stockades, and was present at the capture, Nov., 1875; commissioner to forces during operations in Perak, Dec., 1875; accompanied General Collborne's force up the Perak River, and across country to Kinta; acting resident councillor, Penang, 1884 to 1885; president of Singapore municipal commission, 1889; retd., 1890.
DUNN, ROBERT ERNEST.—Clerk and interpreter to the R.M., Umgeni Division, Natal, 1880; ditto to R.M., Lion's River, Mar., 1883; ditto to R.M., Alfred, Oct., 1887; ditto to R.M., Upper Umkomazi, 1894.
DURRANT, CAPTAIN FRANCIS, R.N., C.M.G. (1884).—Captain of H.M.S. "Canada," and governor of H.R.H. Prince George of Wales until 1885; now captain of H.M.S. "Ajax"; retired from the navy, 1893.
DYER, WILLIAM T. THISELTON, M.A., F.R.S., C.M.G. (1882), Christ Church, Oxon, 1873.—Assistant director, Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew, 1873; director, 1885.
DYETT, RICHARD HENRY KORTRIGHT.—Served in president's office, Virgin Islands, 1879 to 1881, and 1882 to 1884; clerk, registrar's office, Nevis, 1886; 2nd clerk, registrar's office, Antigua, 1887.

EARDLEY-WILMOT, COLVILLE.—Writer, Oeylon Service, Sept., 1879; in Colombo Kachcheri, Dec., 1879; police court, Badulla, Aug., 1881; in Colombo Kachcheri Aug., 1882; ditto Kegalla, May, 1883; ditto, Anuradhipura, Aug., 1883; assistant collector and landing surveyor, Trincomalee, June, 1886; ag. ass't collr. of customs and landing surveyor, Galle, April, 1888; ag. pol. mag. Gallo, Mar., 1892; ag. dist. judge, Kegalla, Nov., 1894; confirmed as pol. mag. Galle, May, 1895.
EARL, ROBT. STEPHEN, M.A., M.B., B.C. (Cantab., 1891).—Med. offr. Lasoye dist., Dominica, July, 1892.

EASMON, JOHN FARRELL.—Assistant colonial surgeon, Gold Coast Colony, 7th Sept., 1880; health officer Accra, 1884; chairman, central committee, Gold Coast section Colonial, and Indian Exhibition, 1886; acted several times as chief med. officer; chief medical officer, 1893.

EASTON, JAMES.—Asst. accntt. dept. of crown lands and public works, Cape, Nov., 1877; secy. to general manager of railways, Jan., 1881; subsequently also secy. to railway board; chief accntt. dept. of crown lands and public works, Nov., 1882; chief accntt. and accounting officer for the crown lands and public works division, April, 1892.

EATON, HENRY FRANCIS.—Clerk, colonial storekeeper's office, Melbourne, Jan., 1853; transferred to civil commissariat, Feb., 1854; accountant, government stores, Feb., 1855; transferred to treasury, Mar., 1865; acting accountant, treasury, 1869-70, 1876, 1886-7; accountant to treasury, 1887; captain, Volunteer Artillery, 1876; ag. under treas., June, 1889, confirmed, Sept., 1889.
ECKEL, E.—First clerk and accountant, receiver general's department, Trinidad, 1st May, 1879; first clerk, audit office, 1884.

EDMUNDS, ARTHUR JOSEPH.—Emigrated to South Australia 1838; barrister, supreme court, 1860, and practised for 16 years; appointed stipendiary magistrate, Port Pirie, Jan., 1877, and also returning officer, northern electoral district, in 1883, and still holds both offices.
EDWARDS, ARTHUR ELLIOTT, M.R.C.S. Eng.—Medical officer, Antigua, 1875; second medical officer of the Holberton Institution, 10 June, 1879.
EDYE, H. M.—R.M., Riversdale Division, Cape Colony, Jan., 1882; ditto, Clanwilliam, 1890.

EGERTON, RICHARD.—Ed. Westminster and Ch. Ch., Oxford, B.A., 1867; called to the bar, Middle Temple, Nov., 1869; assistant resident magistrate, Kingston, May, 1892; resident magistrate, Jamaica, Feb., 1893.

EGERTON, WALTER.—Ed. Tonbridge School; cadet, S. S., Oct., 1880; assistant to the Chinese Protector, Jan., 1881; magistrate, Singapore, Jan., 1881; passed final exam. Malay, May, 1882; ass't collector of Land Revenue, Penang and P.W., June, 1882; confirmed Jan., 1883; 2nd magistrate of police, Penang, May, 1883; also official ass't under the Bankruptcy Ordinance (Sept., 1882); sen. dist. offr. Butterworth, P.W., April, 1884; J.P. and coroner for S. S., and a visiting justice for Penang; officer in charge, Sungei Ujong, 1889; ag. 1st mag. Singapore, Dec., 1893; result., Pahang, May to July, 1894; off. assignee and reg'r. of deeds, Singapore, June, 1895.
EGGETT, W. H.—Appointed, after competitive examination, to be a clerk of the (now second) division of the civil service assigned to the Colonial Office, 10th Nov., 1878.

EITEL, ERNEST JOHN.—Educated at Württemberg at the Pedagogium of Esslingen (1857), in the Cloister School of Schenenthal (1858), and in the Stift of Tübingen (1858-60); passed the M.A. examination at Tübingen (1860); member of the State Church of Württemberg (1860); member of the board of examiners in Hong Kong; director of Chinese studies (1875); acting insp. of schools (1878); translator to the Chinese secretary, and acting Chinese secretary to the governor (1878); confirmed as inspector of schools (1879).

ELCUM, JOHN BOWEN, B.A., Oxon.—Ed. Highgate School and Queen's Coll., Ox.; scholar of Queen's Coll., 1878; cadet, S. S., Oct., 1880; passed in Malay, 1886; actg. dist. offr. Seri Malacca, 1887; dist. offr. Bukit Mertajam, P.W., Jan., 1890; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Dec., 1890; sheriff, Penang, Mar., 1892; ag. sheriff, Singapore, June, 1893; 2nd mag. Penang, Nov., 1893; ag. collr. ld. rev., Singapore, Dec., 1893 to Feb., 1894; ag. insp. of Schools S.S., June, 1895; also off. assignee and reg'r. of deeds, Singapore, Mar. to June, 1896.
ELDER, SIR THOMAS, G.C.M.G. (1877), KNT. BACHEL (1878).—Senior partner in the mercantile firm of Elder, Smith and Co., Adelaide; member of the legislative council of S.W. Australia, 1871-8.

ELDRIDGE, A. E. G.—Clerk, P.O., Antigua, Mar., 1880; clerk to president and dist. sec'y, assistant excise officer, 1873; landing warrant clerk, water commissioners, 1878; acting 1st clerk, 1878; 1st clerk, Sept., 1879; has acted on several occasions from 1880-6 as harbour master, and treasurer in 1885, and 1894; harbr. mr., Antigua, Oct. 1894.

ELIOT, GEOFFREY F.—Clerk to magistrate &c., Bunbury, West Australia, 1872; insp. of sheep, Wellington, 1876; temporary clerk, colonial secretary's office, 1878; customs clerk, 1880; clerk, colonial secretary's office, 1884.

ELIOT, LAURENCE S.—Clerk to magistrate, Western Australia; landing and tide-waiter at Bunbury, 1863; also postmaster and assistant registrar; clerk in colonial secretary's office, 1872; secretary to central board of education, and assistant clerk in legislative council, 1873; registrar-general, registrar of deeds, and registrar of births, 1876; acting chief clerk colonial secretary's department, Jan., 1878, to Jan., 1880; first clerk registrar-general, colonial secretary's departme...

ELLIOT, CHARLES BLETTERTON, LL.B., J.G. (1894).—Clerk, colonial secretary's office, w. 1859; clerk to Justice Watermeyer, 1863; secretary to examiners in literature and science; clerk, colonial secretary's office, 1867; chief clerk, department of lands and works, Dec., 1872; advocate, supreme court, 1875; asst. commissioner, crown lands and public works, 1876; general manager, railways, Dec., 1880; is mem. of coun. of Univ., 1881; examiner and moderator of examiners in science.
ELLIOT, MAJOR HENRY GEORGE, C.M.G. (1873).—Chief magistrate of Tembuland, Cape of Good Hope; in command of Tembu levies during the war.

ELLIOT, JOHN WILLIAM.—Entered public service at Tobago, Feb., 1869; acting colonial secretary, Feb., 1872; clerk of peace, district court, April 24, 1872; provisional colonial secretary, registrar, and clerk of enrolments, Oct., until July, 1874; private secretary to the administrator of the Government, July 12, 1874; stipendiary magistrate, May, 1876; clerk petty sessions, district No. 1, Aug., 1876; 1st police officer, May, 1879; district magistrate, Dominica, Feb., 1880; acting police magistrate, Lagos, Dec., 1884; magistrate, St. Lucia, 1886.

ELLIOTT, COLONEL JOHN, R.M.L.I., C.B., C.M.G. (1891).—Entered the Royal Navy in 1872; second lieutenant, June, 1846; colonel, 1869; served in the China expedition, 1842 (medal); served in H.M.S. "Fox" during the Burmese war, 1842 (officially thanked); mentioned in despatches; commanded the Royal Marines in the steam flotilla on the Irrawaddy (mentioned in governor-general's dispatch, medal with clasp for Pegu). Served at bombardment and surrender of the forts of Marsund, in Aug., 1854 (Baltic medal), and during the siege of Sebastopol in 1855, the reduction of Kertch and Yeni-Kale (medal with sp. 5th class of the Medjidie, and Turkish medal); has also the medal for the Kaffir war. Served as major of brigade to the force of Royal Marines landed in Mexico in 1863; lieutenant-colonel, Devonshire Rifle Volunteer Corps, 1870; J.J.P. for Plymouth; in 1874 called to the Middle Temple, and joined the western circuit; 1876 inspector of prisons and stipendiary P. British Guiana; inspector-general of police, Trinidad, 1881; admind. govt. of St. Vincent and 1889; and acted as col. secy., Barbados, 1886; and again 1894; retired, 1895.

ELLIOTT, THOS., C.M.G. (1890).—Clerk, customs, London, Nov. 1858; clerk, treasury, London, April, 1859; registrar, colonial secretary's office, Mauritius, July, 1860; acting chief clerk, April, 1866; acting secretary to council, Mar., 1868; chief clerk and secretary to council, Feb., 1870; acting collector of immigrants, 1873; auditor general, 1874; acted on several occasions as receiver general and col. secy.; retired Nov., 1894.

ELLIOTT, T. E.—Held various acting appointments in Ceylon from 1858; writer in the Ceylon civil service, 1863; appointed to act as commissioner of requests, Jaffna, Sept., 1864; commissioner of requests and police magistrate, Point Pedro, 1865; assistant government agent, Nuwara Eliya, Mar., 1867; at Galle, April, 1867; at Matara, June, 1867; inspector-general of prisons, 1878; acting government agent, east province, 1883; senior grain commissioner, 1883; acting government agent, south province, 1886; govt. agt. east prov., Mar., 1889; ditto south prov., Feb., 1892.
ELLIOTT, W. J. P.—Supervision of customs, Gold Coast, July, 1881; sub. treas. and coll., Br. Sherbro, Oct., 1884; coll. of customs Lagos, 1886; collector of customs, Sierra Leone, 1892.

ELLIS, F.—Writer in the Ceylon civil service, July, 1871; additional police magistrate, Kurunegala, 1874; office assistant to government agent northern province, 1876; acting police magistrate, Kandy, 1878; acting assistant agent, Matale, 1879; ditto, Mullaitivu, 1880; district judge, Ratnapura, 1st April, 1883; police magistrate, Colombo, 1885; director and inspector-general of prisons, 1891.

ELLIS, WALTER DEVONSHIRE.—Educated at Winchester and New College, Oxon., scholar, 1890-1895; prox. acc. Hertford University Scholarship, 1891; 1st class classical moderations, 1892; University junior Greek testament prize, 1893; 1st class Litera Humaniorei 1894 B.A. 1894; chancellor's prize, Latin essay, 1895; appointed after competitive examination to be a clerk in the Colonial Office, April, 1895.
ELLIS, WILLIAM GILMORE, M.D.; M.R.C.S.—Medical officer, lunatic asylum, Singapore, 1888; ag. also as municipal health offr. Singapore, May, 1889, to Feb., 1892, and again 1894.

EMBERSON, HORACE G. C.—Assoc. of Arts, Oxon.; notary public; stip. mag., Fiji, Oct., 1874; member Lands Commission, Oct., 1875; registrar-general, chief police magistrate, and commr. supreme court, 1876; registrar of titles, 1877; acting commissioner of crown lands, Feb., 1880; acting member executive council, Feb., 1880; deputy commissioner stamps, 1880; acting member of legislative council, June, 1881; acting receiver general and comptroller stamps, and member ex. coun., 1887, and along with other duties act. agent-genl. immigr., 1888; confirmed 1888.

EMERSON, GEO. HY., Q.C.—Speaker, House of Assembly, Newfoundland, 1890-94; member of official delegation to London, 1890, and of delegation from legislature in 1891 on the fisheries question; member executive council, 1895.

ENGELBACH, A. H. H.—Appointed 29th December, 1869, after passing an examination before the Civil Service Commissioners, clerk in the accounts branch in the office of the secretary of state for the colonies, having acted in that capacity since March, 1868; first-class assistant clerk, 1st Sept., 1879; book-keeper and senior assistant to the financial clerk, 1881; acted for financial clerk, June, 1886, to April, 1887.
ESCOMBE, HON. HARRY, Q.C., M.L.A.—Atty.-Gen., Natal, 1893.

ESNOUF, E. AMAND.—District magistrate, Seychelles, 1870; Grand Port, Mauritius, 15 May, 1878. District magistrate, Moka, 1883; junior district magistrate, Port Louis, Aug., 1884; ag. mr. sup. ct., May, 1886; and Dec., 1887, to Jan., 1888; ag. senr. dist. mag. Pt. Louis, Mar., 1890, to Mar., 1892; Dec., 1892, to Feb., 1893; May to Aug., 1896.
EUSTACE, J. T.—Civil commissioner and resident magistrate, Namakwaland division, Capo of Good Hope, 1st July, 1879.
EVANS, SIR DAVID, K.C.M.G. (1892).—Lord Mayor of London, 1891-2; raised funds for relief of distress caused by the Mauritius hurricane and the fire at St. John's, Newfoundland.

EVANS, FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1881).—Entered the civil service, Admiralty, Somerset House, 13th Dec., 1867; transferred to the secretary's department at Whitehall, Jan., 1872; assistant colonial secretary and treasurer Sierra Leone, May, 1878; private secretary to Sir S. Rowe, K.C.M.G., Feb., 1879, to Feb., 1881; acting clerk of legislative council, Sept., 1879; acted as colonial secretary, Dec., 1879, to Feb., 1880; chief secretary and chief of the staff to Sir S. Rowe, during threatened hostilities with Ashanti, 1881; assistant colonial secretary, Gold Coast, 1882; acting collector of customs and treasurer, Oct., 1882, to Jan., 1883; acting colonial secretary, Aug. to Dec., 1883, and on other occasions; clerk of legislative council, Feb., 1882, to April, 1884, deputy governor, Lagos, July to Aug., 1883, and May, 1885, to Jan., 1886; deputy governor, Gold Coast, Sept., 1883, and April, 1885; colonial secretary, Lagos, Jan., 1886; acting administrator, July, 1886; col. secy., Gold Coast, 1887; ditto, Leeward Islands, 1888; ditto, Jamaica, 1889.

EVANS, WILLIAM.—Cadet, S.S. Nov., 1882; ed. King's Coll. Camb.; acting assistant protector of Chinese, Singapore, Dec., 1885; acting collector of land revenue, Malacca, May, 1886; 2nd assistant protector of Chinese, May, 1887; act. asst. prot. Chinese, Singapore, Feb., 1888; act. 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1888; had passed exams. in Chinese and Malay; J.P., actg. dis. officer, Dindings, 1888; asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, June, 1890; ag. prot. of Chinese, Penang, July, 1890; ditto, S.S. Nov. and Dec., 1891, to June, 1893; asst. prot. Chinese, Penang, 1893; prot. of Chinese, S.S. Ap., 1895.

EVELYN, CHARLES GREY.—Acting 2nd master, grammar school, St. Kitts, Jan., 1867; 4th clerk revenue department, June, 1869; 3rd landing waiter, treasury, Dec., 1873; clerk to magistrate, district C., May, 1875; acting magistrate on various occasions; deputy coroner, district C., May, 1881; escheator general, 1881; magistrate, Dominica, 1883; magistrate, Nevis, 1889.
EVELYN, E. F.—Accountant of post office, Barbados, Sept., 1880.

EVERARD, W.—Supernumerary col. secy.'s office, Barbados, Sept., 1868; landing waiter, customs, Dec., 1868; ag. inspr. of inland rev. offrs., Jan., 1884, to Dec., 1885; ch. elk. P.O., Mar., 1885; senior landing waiter, customs, June, 1886.
EVES, C. WASHINGTON, C.M.G. (1890).—For services in Jamaica.
EYLES, C. H.—Assistant colonial surgeon, Gold Coast Colony, 1883; colonial surgeon, British Honduras, 1887.
FABRE, HECTOR, C.M.G. (1887).—Formerly senator, Canada; honorary commissioner for Canada, Indian and Colonial exhibition, now Canadian agent, Paris.

FADELLE, FRED. STEWART.—French interpreter to the government, Dominica, 1871; excise officer, 1873; ag. inspector of schools, Jan. to July, 1882; dep. marshal V. A. Ct., 1886; govt. offr. Roseau dist. 1889; postmtr. 1893.
FAIK, KADIZADJ, MAHMED.—Clerk Evkaf dept., Cyprus, 1880; clerk, audit office, Aug., 1883.

FAILLE, GEO. CUNNINGHAM.—Ed. Stonyhurst Coll.; ag. 2nd elk. to Gov. Leeward Islds., May, 1889; 2nd elk. to col. sec., Jan., 1890; 2nd elk. governor's off., June, 1890; ag. chief elk. col. sec. and elk. exec. comm., June to Oct., 1890; permanently apptd. to that post, Aug., 1891.
FAIRBAIRN, J. A.—Clerk to legislative council, Cape of Good Hope, 30th May, 1861.

FAIRBAIRN, ROBERT.—Second master, school, Perth, Western Australia, 1859; clerk bench of magistrates, landing waiter, and master, Vasse, 1862; resident magistrate, magistrate of local court, and district registrar, ditto, Newcastle, 1875; ditto, Vasse, April, Kimberley, 1883; ditto, Fremantle, 1886.

FAIRFIELD, E. C.B. (1895), C.M.G. (1895).—Educated at Harrow; appointed, after a competitive examination, clerk in Colonial Office, Nov., 1872; 2nd class clerk, 30th September, 1872; called to bar by the Inner Temple, Michaelmas, 1872, having been previously awarded a certificate of the 1st class (Trinity, 1872), and an exhibit (Michaelmas, 1872) by the council of legislation; sent by the Secretary of State on a mission to Gibraltar in 1873, to inquire, in conjunction with the Senior Control Officer, into the constitution of the civil departments; acted as secretary to London South African Conference, August; assistant private secretary to the Earl of Kimberley, 30th April, 1880, to 30th Sept., 1880; first clerk, 1st Oct., 1880; sent on a mission to India in 1881, to confer with the high commission upon the subject of the civil establishment of the island; was Editor of Colonial Office List, 1874; acting principal clerk, 1889; assistant secretary of state for the colonies, Feb., died, 1897.

FALCONBRIDGE, WILLIAM GLENCHURCH.—Ed. Toronto Univ.; B.A. (gold medallist), M.A., 1870; barrister, 1871; registrar, University of Toronto, 1872 to 1881; elected member of senate of university, 1881; Q.C., 1885; bencher law society of Upper Canada, 1885; judge supreme court, Ontario, Q.B. Division, 1887.

FANNIN, JOHN EUSTACE.—R.M. and administrator native law, enty, Umtvoti, Natal, March, 1883; had previously acted as R.M. of Umtali, Umgeni divisions; was special border in Umtvoti, during the Zulu war of 1879; a member of Natal Native Commission of 1881, and of commission to define boundary between Nsuzi Orange Free State, 1884; received thanks of secretary of state for services on this occasion; member of special judicial commission for trial of chiefs, 1888.

FARNUM, CHARLES.—Assistant storekeeper, Trinidad, Sept., 1850; acting deputy assistant missionary-general, 1856 to 1859; assistant keeper, 1860; fourth clerk in the receiver-general's office, Sept., 1863; third clerk, May, 1872; clerk in receiver-general's department, Nov., chief clerk, July, 1874; and in charge of branch of that department from 1866 to 1877; clerk, customs, Feb., 1877; acting collector of customs, on several occasions.
FARRAR, NICHOLAS.—Super. P.W. dept., Guiana, 1883; 3rd elk., Jan., 1885; 5th class rec. gen.'s office, May, 1889; 4th class, A. 1891.
FARRELL, WILLIAM OWEN.—Assistant to Mauritius railways, 1882; goods clerk, 1884.
FAULKNER, E.—Educated at the grammar school, Freetown; brigade office clerk, S. Leone, 1876 to 1883; chief clerk, colonial tariar, 1883; asst. col. secy., 1889.
FAURE, J. C.—Civil commissioner and resident magistrate, Calvini division, Cape, 1882.
AUER, P. H.—Sec. for native affairs, Cape, 1890.

AWCETT, COLONEL MORRIS J.—Ensign Royal Fusiliers, Aug., 1854; lieut., Aug.; capt., Mar., 1867; major, Oct., 1875; re- May, 1883; was colonel in the Turkish army, to 1881; inspector, and general superintendent of constabulary, Newfoundland, 1885; insp- ral of constabulary, Jamaica, 1895.
AWCETT, WILLIAM, B.S.C. (LOND.), F.L.S.—Assistant botanical department, British Museum; director public gardens and plantations, India, 1886; member board of governors India Institute, 1887.
