KNIGHT, JOHN LOCKE.—Clerk and Zulu interpreter to the Upper Tugela magistracy, Feb., 1876, to May, 1878; ditto, Umsinga magistracy, May, 1878, to Aug., 1881, during which time he acted for the R.M. and administrator three times; Aug., 1881, clerk and Zulu interpreter, and registrar to the circuit court, Newcastle district; April, 1882, acting resident magistrate and administrator of native law, Newcastle; Feb., 1883, ditto, Umsinga division; Oct., 1883, administrator of native law, and Zulu border agent to Lower Tugela division; has held the honorary appointments of crown prosecutor and officer for the sale of stamps at the several magistracies named; commanded a native levy of over 1,000 strong during the Zulu war; acted for two months in 1884 as R.M., Lower Tugela division; sub-commissioner, Zululand, April, 1887; assist. commiss., and R.M., Entojamani district, June, 1887; R.M., Nkandhla district, Jan., 1888.
KNIGHT, M. T.—Financial secy., Newfoundland, 1886-9; surveyor-general, 1894.

KNOLLYS, C. C., C.M.G. (1888).—Educated at Mag. Col., Ox., open scholarship in 1868; 1st class math. mod., 1870; 2nd class final schools, 1872; B.A., 1873; M.A., 1877; sub-receiver, Trinidad, 1874; auditor-general of Barbados, 1879; president of the education board, 1882-3; acting colonial secretary, 1882, confirmed 1883; administered the government of Barbados, 1883; of St. Lucia, May, 1884, to May, 1885, and Nov., 1890, to May, 1891; of Grenada, 1892; col. sec., Trinidad, 1894.

KNOLLYS, MAJOR LOUIS FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1877).—Marlborough School; ensign, 32nd regt., 1866; captain, May, 1880; major, 26th Cameroons, 1881; A.D.C. to governor of Mauritius, 1872-74, to governor of Fiji, 1875; commanded force sent to reduce mountain tribes, 1876 (promoted to company and C.M.G.); commandant armed native constabulary; and commissioner in mountains, 1877; A.D.C. to governor, New Zealand, 1880, and to governor of Ceylon, 1883; inspector-general of constabulary and director of prisons, Jamaica, 1886; inspr.-gen. of police, Ceylon, Oct., 1891.

KNOLLYS, REGINALD F.—Ed. Oxford Military Coll. and Heidelberg Univ.; private secretary and clerk ex. and leg. councils, Br. Honduras, July, 1887; inspr. of schools, Jan., 1889; 3rd assist. col. secy., G. Const., Feb., 1893; member bd. of edn., chief interpreter Ashanti expd., 1896; is author of "Brit. Honduras, outlines of its Geography."

KNUTSFORD, 1ST VISCOUNT (creat. 1895).—CREATED BARON (1888). THE RIGHT HON. SIR HENRY THURSTAN HOLLAND, BART., G.C.M.G. (1886), K.C.M.G. (1877), C.M.G. (1875).—Succeeded to the baronetcy on the death of his father in Oct., 1873; edu. at Harrow and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1849, and went the Northern Circuit; legal adviser to the secretary of state for the colonies, Jan., 1867, and assistant under secretary of state, Mar., 1870. Resigned, 3rd Aug., 1874, and elected member for Midhurst, 22nd Sept., 1874; member of the royal commission on the treatment of fugitive slaves, 1876; one of the royal commissioners on the defence of British possessions and commerce abroad, 8th Sept., 1879; secretary to the treasury, June, 1885; and vice-president of the council Sept., 1885, and again in Aug., 1886; returned to Parliament as member for the Hampstead division of Middlesex, 1885, and again 1886 and 1887; secretary of state for the colonies, Jan., 1887, to Aug., 1892; president, colonial conference, 1887.

KOCH, REV. C. A.—Assistant missionary to Bishop of Labuan, bishop's chaplain, registrar of the diocese, 1856; diocesan chaplain, Ratnapura, Ceylon, Mar., 1866; acting colonial chaplain, Jaffna, Aug., 1867; confirmed Aug., 1869; acting colonial chaplain, Colombo, Jan., 1879, to Jan., 1880, and confirmed Oct., 1880.

KOCH, HENRY C.—Admitted to Natal bar as solr., 1884, as advocate, 1887; crown prosecutor and legal adviser to Zululand Govt., Apr., 1889; is also reg'r. of deeds, reg'n-gen. of births, deaths, and marriages.

KOCH, WILFRED VINCENT MILLER, M.B., C.M. (Edin., 1884), M.D. (Edin., 1895, highest honours, gold medal, thesis)—late ass't, in eye department of Edin. Royal Infirmary; and ass't, to the lectures on diseases of the eye and mental diseases, Edin. Univ.; and assist. to the Gynaecological Dept., Edin. Sc. of Medicine; acting ass't med. officer, West Riding Asylum, Sheffield, in 1884; asst. med. officer, Hull Borough Asylum, 1884; late surgeon to Monckton and Carlin Main Collieries, Barnsley; asst. surgeon, Cobden Hospital, Port of Spain, 1889; acting med. superintendent, Leper Asylum, Trinidad, 1890; dist. med. officer, Diego Marti, 1890-92; asst. med. officer, Port of Spain, 1892-93; ag. med. officer, leper asylum, 1893; ag. dist. med. off'r, P. Martin, 1893; ag. med. supt. lunatic asylum, to Oct., 1894; lieut. 1891; capt. 1892, comdr. of Spain art. vols.; is surgeon to permanent Trinidad local forces.
KORTRIGHT, L. M.—A.M.I.C.E., M.I.M.—Surveyor of roads and superintendent of public works, St. Kitts, 1879; official member, legislative council, St. Kitts and Nevis, 1886.

KUFAL, ALBERT OTTO.—Extra clk. aud. Natal, 1889; do. col. sec. office, Feb., 1890; class clk. in same office, July, 1890; and in audit dept., Durban, Jan., 1892; sworn trans. for French and German, sup. etc., Aug., 1892; clk., registry of deeds, Mar., 1893; ag. clk., April, 1894.

KYNNERSLEY, CHAS. WALTER SMITH.—Ed. Rugby; cadet, S.S., April, 1872; sec'y, assistant lieut.-governor's office, and superintend. of schools, Penang, 1873; ass't magistratc, P.W., 1875, accompanied expedition to Perak and Sungel Ujong in that year; 2nd magistrate, Singapore, 1881; 1st magistratc, consur., ct. of requests, Penang, 1881; superintend. of prisons; agt. col. treasurcr, Penang, 1889; 1st mag., Singap., June, 1890; ag. resdt. councillor, Malacca, 1893; confirmed Jan., 1895.
KYNSLEY, W.R., F.K.Q.C.P., C.M.G. (1888).—Principal civil medical officer, and insp'g. general of hospitals, Ceylon, Feb., 1875.

KYSHIE, JAMES WILLIAM NORTON.—Downing Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Lincolns Inn, 1880; held various clerical appointments in Mauritius from 1871 to 1877; deputy reg'r. Supreme Court, Penang, 1880; has acted as registrar on several occasions and as magistratc; has published an index to the ordinances of Straits Settlements; and has reported the cases decided in the Supreme Court, 1786 to 1876; sheriff, Singapore, Jan., 1892; ag. reg'r. st. and mag., Malacca, June, 1893, to Feb. 1894; reg'r. sup. of effl. assignee, reg'r. of companies land off'r, Hong Kong, Sept., 1895.

LABORDE, EDWARD, C.M.G. (1883).—Ch. the treasury of St. Vincent, 1850; deputy treasurcr, 1852; acting colonial secretary, 1856; police magistrate, 1857; stipendiary magistrate and commr., provost-marshal, 1860; colonial sec'y from 1861 to 1880; commanded, until 1888, St. George's volunteers, which were raised by him in 1863; administered the government of Grenada in 1871, May to Oct., 1874; admin. the government of St. Vincent July, 1872, to 1873; April to May, 1874; Oct., 1874, to 1875; May to Dec., 1877; Mar., 1878, to 1879; and Mar. to July, 1880, when he was appointed to administer the government of Tobago; admin. the government of Grenada, April, 1880, to May, 1885, when he was transferred to admin. the government of St. Lucia.

LABORDE, E.D.—1st clerk, audit office, Trin., Jan., 1882; and deputy registrar, acting conf'g. clerk to administrator, and clerk of councils, 1882; 2nd clerk, administrator's office, St. L., Feb., 1883; chief clerk, Sept., 1883; acting clerk, governor-in-chief's office, Barbadoes; chief clerk to governor, Windward Islands.
LACKEY, The Hon. Sir John, K.C.M.G. (1884).—M.L.C., N.S. Wales, Sec. for pub. wks., etc., 1878, to Jan., 1883.
LACOSTE, The Hon. Sir Alexr., Q.C., Kt., B.A. (1892).—Chief justice, Court of Queen's Bench, Quebec; member of the Dominion Privy Council, 1892.

LACROIX, Wm. Laborde.—Assist. clerk, imigration dept., Trinidad, Aug., 1866; third clerk, June, 1867; second clerk, Nov., 1872; assist. warden, Diego Martin ward union, Jan., 1882; warden and supervisor, Couva and Chaguanas ward unions, p.t., 1883; and J.P. for county Caroni. In addition an officer of customs for the Unions, and manager, Couva savings bank, April, 1886.

LAFFAN, James Thomas, L.R.C.S.I., L.K., and Q.C.P.I., &c.—Asst.-col. surgeon, Larneac, Cyprus, 1883; resident medical officer, Wyndham, New South Australia, 1886; acting R.M., Wyndham, 1887; resident medical officer, Bunbury, 1889; police magistrate, Blackwood, 1889.

LAGDEN, Godfrey Yeatman, C.M.G. (1894).—Clerk, G.P.O., Lond., 1869-77, after competitive examination; clerk to secretary to government, Transvaal, 1878; private secretary to administrator, 1878-81; secretary to executive council, 1880-81; private secretary to Sir Bellairs and Sir E. Wood, while administering government 1881; secretary to Transvaal commission for compensation claims, 1881-82; gazetted as war correspondent, Egyptian campaign, 1882-3; present at all engagements, including Tel-Kebir and capture of Cairo; on special financial duty, Gold Coast, 1883; secretary and accountant, Natal, 1884; assistant commissioner, 1885; established magistracy in Masupha's country, 1886; elected as res. commr., May to Nov., 1890; ag. Br. commr., Swaziland govt. committee of administration, Apr., 1892; ag. resd. commr., Basutoland, 1893.

LAING, John.—J.P. for Stockenstrom, Cape, 1854; member of assembly for Fort Beaufort, 1854; member of ex. coun. and commiss. of own lands and public works, Feb., 1878, to May, 1881; resigned seat in parliament, 1885; again elected, 1887.

LAIRD, The Hon. David.—Born 1833; member of coun., P. E. Island, 1872-3; one of the delegates who negotiated the admission of the island into the Dominion; sworn of the Canadian bar council and minister of interior, 1873-6; lieutenant-governor, N.W. Territories, 1876-8; was chief commissioner negotiating treaty with Blackfeet Indians, 1877.

LAMB, John Cameron, C.B. (1895), C.M.G. (1899).—Entered the post-office in 1861; principal clerk, 1877; was one of the British delegates to the submarine cables protection conferece, at Paris, May and Dec., 1886; asst. sec. to the post-office, 1886; first British delegate to the international telegraph conferece, at Paris, 1890, when he was also one of the representatives of the Cape and Natal; royal commissioner on electrical communication between lighthouses and lighthouses, June, 1892; third secy. to the post-office, Apr., 1896; senior British delegate and "president de la commission des tarifs" at international telegraph conferece, at Budapest, 1896, when he was also one of the representatives of the Cape, Natal, and N. Zealand; 3rd sec. to P.O., Apr., 1896; 2nd sec. Jan., 1897.

LAMBERT, Henry Charles Miller.—Ed. at Aston Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; first class in classical moderations, 1889; first class in final classical schools, 1891; entered as student of Lincoln's Inn; apptd. second cl. clk. in the Col. Office after open competitive exam., 1st Feb., 1892; member of committee emigrants information office 1894; chairman of the committee, 1897.

LAMBERT, Richard S. F.—Acting clerk of supplies to prisons and asylums and secretary to the board of prisons, British Guiana, Mar., 1874; confirmed, Feb., 1875; acted, in conjunction with his own office, as clerk of the records, government secretary's office, April to Oct., 1876; clerk to inspector of prisons, &c., July, 1877; district commissary of taxation, Aug., 1877; transferred to customs, Mar., 1879; secretary to commission to inquire into law relating to roads, 1881; acting clerk of the records, government secretary's office, 1882; acting chief clerk in same office, 1882; special duty in same office from 1882 to 1884; 2nd clerk, government savings bank, Georgetown, 1894; chief clerk, government savings bank, Berbice branch, 1885; secy. to Lands Titles Commn., Oct., 1889, to Sept., 1891; pay and qu.-mr., B. Guiana police, with rank of cty. inspr., Sept., 1891.
LAMBTON, Stephen Harbord.—Entered postal service, N.S.W., Sept., 1852; acting secretary, Aug., 1865; confirmed Sept., 1866.

LAMINGTON, Lord (2nd Baron, creat. 1880), Sir Charles Wallace Alexander Napier Cochrane-Saillie, K.C.M.G. (1895), only son of 1st Baron; born, 1860; dep. Lt. Lanarkshire; lieut. Lanarkshire Yeomanry, 1883; M.P. for N. St. Pancras, 1886-90; Gov. Queensland, 1895.

LAMOND, George.—Postmaster at Compensation, Natal, 1853; clerk in the audit office, 1854; second clerk in the colonial secretary's office, 1859; 1st clerk, 1864; chief clerk, 1875; and registrar of deeds and distributor of stamps, and registrar-general of births, deaths, and marriages, 1878.
LAMPSON, P. E.—Ed. Wesleyan mission school, Anamaboe, G. Coast; asst. master govt. school, Accra, 1893.
LANG, Wm., M.D.—Health officer Carriacou, Grenada, 1863; medical officer, St. Andrew's parish, 1867; and health officer, Port of Grenville.

LANGEVIN, Edouard Joseph, N.P.—Admitted as a notary public, Lower Canada, 3rd Dec., 1858; a member of board of notaries of Lower Canada for several years; clerk of the Crown in Chancery, Canada, 4th Jan., 1865; to same office for dominion, 5th July, 1867; deputy registrar-general, 1st July, 1868; under secretary of state of Canada, 9th July, 1873; elected secretary to civil service board of Canada, 1876; clerk of the Senate, 1883.

LANGEVIN, The Hon. Sir Hector Louis, K.C.M.G. (1881), C.B. (1868), Q.C., I.L.D.—Born, 1826; called to the bar of Lower Canada, 1850; created a queen's counsel, 1864; mayor of Quebec from 1858 to 1861, and as such received H.R.H. the Prince of Wales on his landing in Canada, 1860; entered Canadian parliament, 1857, and held a seat, first in the old legislative assembly, and since confederation, in the house of commons of the dominion till 1892; member of the executive council, Canada, and solicitor-general for Lower Canada, 30th March, 1864; postmaster-general, Nov., 1866; at confederation, 1st July, 1867, sworn of the queen's privy council and was appointed secretary of state of Canada until 8th Dec., 1879, when appointed minister of public works, and whilst holding that office, was elected as the successor of the late Sir G. E. Cartier, Bart., as leader of the liberal conservative party of Lower Canada; resigned office, 5th Nov., 1873; postmaster-general in Sir John A. Macdonald's second administration, 19th Oct., 1878; then minister of public works; resigned 1891; was one of the framers of the present Canadian constitution, and in furtherance of the cause of confederation attended the colonial conferences at Charlottetown and Quebec in 1864, and in London in 1866, when the Act of Union was carried through the imperial parliament; created a Knight Commander of the Roman Order of St. Gregory the Great, 1870; is the author of a prize essay on Canada, and of several other works.

LANGFORD, CHARLES W.—Issuer, commissariat dept., Barbados, 1868; asst. storekeeper, control dept., Trinidad, Oct., 1869; clerk, G.P.O., Trinidad, June, 1871; 2nd ditto, Nov., 1871; 6th clerk, rec. gen.'s office, Jan., 1875; 5th ditto, Feb., 1877; 4th ditto, May, 1878; 3rd ditto, May, 1879; excise officer April, 1884.
LANGFORD, JOHN.—Clerk, col. secy's. office, Fiji, Oct., 1874; chief clerk and clerk of leg. coun., Jan., 1878; registrar, supreme court, and curator of intestate estates, June, 1884.

LANNING, CAIT. ROBERT ALFRED, R.A.—Commissary of ordnance, Cape, July, 1882; and accounting officer, colonial defence, in addition, July, 1886; asst. vol. mil. sec., in addition, 1892; entered R.A., June, 1862; garrison adjt., Cape Town, July, 1870, to August, 1872; adjt. R.A. division, in India, May, 1877, to April, 1881; served in Afghan campaigns of 1878-79-80; in first as adjt. of siege train; and in second as adjt., R.A., Khyber division (medal).

LANDSDOWNE, 5TH MARQUIS OF (G.T. BRIT.)—Creat. 1784; K.G., G.C.M.G. (1884); G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E.—Henry Charles Keith FitzMaurice, eldest son of the 4th Marquis, born 1845; succeeded his father in 1866; was under secretary for war from April, 1872, to Feb., 1874, and under secretary India Office, May to Aug., 1880; governor-general of Canada, 1883; viceroy of India, Nov., 1888-94; secretary of state for war, July 28th, 1893.
LA PRIMAUDAYE, C.—Commander, R.N.; capt. of port, Malta, Oct., 1888; superintendent of police, 1890.

LARNACH, THE HON. WILLIAM JAMES MUIRIE, C.M.G. (1879).—Late treasurer of New Zealand, and minister of public works in Grey Government. During 1878, 1879, and part of 1880 was one of the loan agents for New Zealand in London, and during 1878 assisted in making arrangements with the Bank of England to act as agents for New Zealand Government loans; during 1885, 1886, and 1887 was member of Stout-Vogel Administration, and held portfolios of minister of mines and minister of marine; published a work on the Mineral Resources and Mines of New Zealand; in 1891 was appointed commissioner and chairman of the Royal Commission for investigating the condition of the public trust office of New Zealand.

LARYMORE, CAIT. H. D., C.M.G. (1896).—Ed. Westminster; entered Att. Militia, 1886; passed qualifying exam. for commiss., Oct., 1886; asst. inspnr. G. Coast constab., Feb., 1891; adjt., July, 1891; served in Jebu expedit., as staff offr. and sec. to Sir F. Scott; mentioned in despatches, and received thanks of gov. and ex. and leg. councils of Lagos (medal and clasp).
LASBERY, PERCY V.—M.A., Lond.; head master, Govt. gram. schl., St. Kitts, Jan., 1894.

LASCELLES, ALFRED GEORGE, R.A.—Univ. coll., Oxon; 2nd class mod. hist.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1885; acted on several occasions as president of district courts in Cyprus and as puisne judge; president of district court, Papho, 1892; ditto, Larnaca, 1893; ag. puisne judge, June, 1895; ag. Queen's advocate, Feb., 1896.
LASH, JAS. MILLER.—Educated upper middle schools, Peckham; schoolmaster, Kirkprison, Liverpool, 1876; gaoler, Grenada, 1878; sup't. of prisons, 1890; governor, Glendalough prison, Barbados, 1894.

LATTOUCHE, FRANCIS PHILIP.—Elect. member leg. assembly, Dominica, 1870 to 1873; representative member for Dominica in leg. com., Leeward Islands, 1872 and 1873; acted as justice, Dominica, in 1873; commssr. for revision and compiling laws of Dominica, 1875, and revision of votes in 1876; acted as sol'r-gen. att.-gen., Leeward Is. on various occasions, 1878-1886; regr. and provost-marshal and reg. titles, Dominica, May, 1886, and dep. regt. w.c.r., 1887; also nominated mem. leg. assembly, supt'd. of crown lands; appointed regr. and provost-marshal and reg. of titles, St. Kitts and Nevis, Mar., 1891.
LATOUK, G. L., M.D., M.R.C.S., E.—Col. surgeon, Grenada, 1881.
LAUDER, CHARLES R.—Clerk, president's of Dominica, Dec., 1872; 4th revenue and quarter officer, Nov., 1874; 3rd ditto, Feb., 1880; ditto, rum department keeper, 1883.
LAURANCE, JOHN.—Third clerk, sur. department, Western Australia, Nov., 1892; registrar, col. secy's. office, Jan., 1888.
LAURENCE, P. M.—Puisne judge, Griqua West, Cape Colony, 1882; judge president, 1884.

LAURIER, HON. WILFRID.—Born at St. Quebec, Nov. 20, 1841; became a B.C.L. McGill in 1864, and a Q.C. in 1880; entered Parliament 1871, being elected for Drummond and Arthabaska, and was re-elected 1874 for same constituency; minister of inland revenue in the Mackenzie ministry, Sept., 1877, where he held until the resignation of that government in 1878. He was defeated at the general election of 1878, but was immediately afterwards elected for Quebec East. He was re-elected at the general elections in 1878, 1882, 1887, and 1892. Became leader of the opposition in Canada in 1890, and premier in June, 1896, on the resignation of the government of Sir C. Tupper.

LAW, A. F. G.—Assistant commissary, Cyprus, 1880; presdt., dist. ct., Famagusta; principal forest officer, 1886; member leg. com., 1887; ag. Queen's advocate, 1889; ag. ch. sec., 1890; Queen's advocate, 1892; puisne judge, Settlemens, 1893.
LAW, REGINALD A.—Sec. to Sir E. O'Malley, justice, S.S., 1890; mail asst., G.P.O., Singapore, Apr., 1892; ag. asst. P.M.G., Penang, 1893, to Sept., 1894; Feb., 1895, to July, 1896; from Sept., 1896.

LAWRENCE, C. J.—Clerk, public works of Barbados, Jan., 1877; secretary central poor board, Sept., 1880; and manager of government emigration funds, June, 1881; secretary to West India telegraph conference, at Barbados, May, 1882; to confidential committee to inquire into the colonial defences, May, 1883; to hard improvements commission, Jan., 1884; and to supply commission, June, 1885; acting clerk executive committee and council, Jan., to May, 1884, and on other occasions; acting police magistrate and coroner, southern district, Grenada, July to Oct., 1884; secretary to local committee, for and Indian Exhibition, and compiler of the Exodos Catalogue, Nov., 1885, and census, 1891; pol. mag. and judge petty debt, c.t., Bridgetown, Aug. to Nov. and Dec. 1889; and in dist. R.M., 1890; and D. Aug. to Oct., 1891; clk. c.t. appeal, Sept., 1891; insp. of police, and...

LAWRANCE, W. T.—Assist. priv. secy. to Baron Henry De Worms, partly sec. to the Board of Trade, 1885-6; in same capacity at the Colonial Office, Feb., 1888 to Aug., 1892; attached to the international conference on sugar bounties, 1887-88.

LAWRIE, ARCHIBALD CAMPBELL.—District judge of Kandy, Ceylon, Sept., 1873; a member of the Faculty of Advocates of Scotland; called in 1860; has acted several times as puisne judge of supreme court, and as commiss. of assize, isle judge, sup. ct., July, 1892.
LAZZARINI, MAJOR-GENERAL C. C. JAMES, M.G. (1882).—Of the Royal Malta Fencible tillery, decorated for services in connection with the expedition to Egypt, 1882.

LAYARD, CHARLES PETER.—Ed. Cheltenham Hall, and St. John's Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, June, 1873; advocate sup. ct., Ceylon, Aug., 1873; ag. solr.-gen., April, 1878; confirmed July of that year; ag. atty.-gen., May, 1879; to July, 1892; puisne judge, sup. ct., Oct., 1892; atty.-gen., Nov., 1892.

LAYARD, EDGAR LEOPOLD, C.M.G. (1875).—Whilst Her Majesty's consul in Fiji was concerned in the negotiations which resulted in the cession of the islands to Great Britain; administered the government from the cession, in 1874, until the arrival of Sir A. Gordon, in 1875; was consul in New Caledonia to 1889.

LEACH, ANDREW JOHN.—Ed. at Sir Roger Gomondelay's sch., Highgate, and St. John's L., Oxon, B.A.; called to the bar, 1876; ag. ass. judge, Hong Kong, Ap. 1887 to Feb. 1889; atty.-gen., Feb. 1889 to Mar. 1890; May, 1891 to Mar., 1892; June, 1892, to Jan., 1893; apl. to l. 1894; and Mar. to Apr., 1895; puisne judge, s., Apr., 1895.
LEACH, JOHN VINCENT.—Educ. Stonyhurst College, England; admitted solr. supreme court, Jamaica, 1881; res. mag., 1888.
LEADER, HN. FRANCIS.—Entered govt. ser. S. Australia, 1870; overseer, 1879; govt. ser., 1888.

LEAKE, GEORGE.—Called to the bar, Western Australia, May, 1880; acted as assistant clerk to legislative council, and registrar of the vice-courts; acting crown solicitor and prosecuting counsel in Jan., 1881, and again Jan., 1883; confirmed Jan., 1884; acting attorney-general and member of executive council in Sept., 1886-87.

LEARMONTTH, CAPT. JOHN HENRY, late capt. 1st Lancers.—Ed. at Eton; staff adjt., South Australian mounted forces, 1886-89, A.D.C. to lord of Kintore, 1889; private secretary and A.D.C. to Sir C. A. Moloney; Gov., Lagos, 1890; clk. to ex. and leg. councils, Feb., 1890; pr. Bahamas constabulary, Aug., 1891; pr.-gen. Leeward Islds. police, 1895.

LEASK, GEORGE.—Acting clerk to master of supreme court, Natal, Jan., 1874; confirmed July, 1874; junior clerk, treasury department, Nov., 1874; 2nd clerk to R.M., Durban, Mar., 1876; chief clerk and registrar of the circuit court, and sub-distributor of stamps, Durban, June, 1881; 3rd dining waiter and searcher, Customs, 1886.

LEASK, J. T., M.B., Ch.M. (Edin.), 1879.—Colonial surgeon, Malacca, Jan., 1891; ditto, Guang, April, 1893; ag. col. surg. resdt., Singapore, May, 1895, to Mar., 1896; ag. col. surg., Guang, Mar., 1896.

ECLEZIO, SIR E. P. J. (KNT. BACH, 1887)—Called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1858; graduate of the Faculty of Laws, Paris, 1858; acting substitute procureur and advocate-general, Mauritius, in 1868, 1869, and 1870; member of the council of government, 1869-78; 1st puisne judge, 1879; chief justice, 1883.

LEE, LIONEL FREDERIC.—Ed. Brentwood Gram. Schl., and Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey; writer, Ceylon service, June, 1864; commissioner of requests, Panadura, 1866; assistant government agent, Colombo, Feb., 1871; district judge, Kegalla, Dec., 1871; police magistrate, Colombo, 1877; regt.-gen., 1879; supt. of census, 1880; postm.-gen. and dir. of telegraphs, 1886; special mag. to repress crime, N.W.P., 1887; supt. census, 1890; dist. judge, Jaffna, May, 1895; ditto, Galle, Oct., 1895; prin. collr. of customs, 1894; ag. treas. consmr. of stps. and member of councils, May, 1895; is author of a handbook in Sinhalese and Tamil for the use of native headmen, and of Census Repts., 1881 and 1891; principal collr. of customs, Ceylon, Dec., 1895; is memb. leg. coun., consmr. Loan Board, director of Govt. Savings Bk. and of W. and O. Fund.
LEE, WILLIAM.—J.P. for Jamaica, 1860; visiting justice general penitentiary and official visitor lunatic asylum, 1878; admnr., general, 1880.

LEECH, H. W. CHAMBERLAIN, J.L.D., C.E., B.L.—Educated in the University of Dublin, where he graduated in 1867; qualified there as a civil engineer; called to the Irish bar in Michaelmas term, 1883; assistant surveyor and engineer, Selangor, Oct., 1876; first assistant commissioner of Perak armed police, Jan., 1877; collector and magistrate, Kinta, Perak, Jan., 1879; collector and magistrate, Krian, Perak, Aug., 1881; state treasurer, June, 1892; also ag. senr. mag., May, 1895.

LEES, SIR CHARLES, K.C.M.G. (1883), C.M.G. (1876).—Was private secretary and aide-de-camp to the governor-in-chief of the Leeward Islands, had local rank of lieut.-colonel, and brigade-major to the militia; entered the army in June, 1854; adjutant, 2nd batt. Royal Welsh Fusiliers, 1858; sold out in Jan., 1866; and was appointed to the adjutancy of the 3rd administrative battalion, Derbyshire rifle volunteers; resigned in May, 1867; appointed civil commandant at Accra, 1869; collector of customs, Lagos, Nov., 1872; sent to the Gold Coast to act as colonial Secretary under Sir Garnet Wolseley during the Ashanti War; administered the government of the Gold Coast Settlements for a short period after the war; administrator of the government of Lagos, July, 1874; and also lieutenant-governor of the Gold Coast Colony, Nov. 1874; despatched on a special mission to Coomassie in 1874, to preserve peace between the Jumbos and Ashantis; administered the general government on several occasions between 1876 and 1879, during the absence of governors-in-chief; governor of Labuan and British consul at Brunei, Sept., 1879; governor, Bahamas, 1881; governor, Leewards, 1883; governor, Barbados, 1885; governor, Mauritius, 1889; Br. Guiana, 1893-5; retired 1895.
LEFROY, A. O'GRADY, C.M.G. (1878).—Treasurer of Western Australia, 1856; was private secretary to Governor Fitzgerald, 1849 to 1855; acting colonial sec., 1875, to Aug., 1879; retired, 1890.

LEFROY, GEORGE ANTHONY, A.M.I.C.E.—Asst. surveyor, West Australia, 1879-80; prev. secy. to Sir H. J. Lefroy as admin. of Tasmania; joined public wks. depart., Perak, 1882; actg. superint. public works and res. eng., Larut, 1885-7; actg. res. eng., Perak State Railway, 1886-7; chief surveyor and in charge survey dept., 1887.

LEGGE, Chas. Henry Gladman.—Clerk to the attorney-general, Trinidad, Sept., 1867; 3rd clerk in the audit office, April, 1872; was clerk of the colonial hospital, port of Spain, Oct., 1873; 6th clerk, receiver-general's department, Nov., 1874; 2nd clerk, audit office, January, 1875; chief clerk pat. office, 1880; 2nd clerk, audit office, Demerara, May, 1880; (designation altered to "Inspector of Accounts," Jan., 1891) chief clerk, 16th Jan., 1882; acting auditor-general, 1883, 1886, 1888, and 1889.
LEGGE, Lieut.-Col. W. V.—Commandant and inspecting field officer, Tasmania, 1882.

LE HUNTE, GEORGE RUTVEN, Trim. Coll., Camb., 1873, M.A., 1880.—Called to the bar, Inner Temple, May, 1881; second private secretary to governor, Fiji, 1875; stipendiary magistrate, 1875; resident commissioner Colo., Viti Levu, 1877; acting commandant, A.N.C., 1878-9; acting colonial secretary, 1880; assistant colonial secretary, Jan., 1882; acting attorney-general, Nov., 1882; judicial commissioner for the high commission to Western Pacific Islands, May, 1883; acting attorney-general, May, 1884; acting receiver-general, Jan., 1886; president of Dominica, 1887; col. sec., Barbados, with dormant consuls as admnr., 1894.
LEICESTER, REGINALD BARNABY.—Assist. treas. and collr. of stamps, Penang, Feb., 1890.

LEIGH, J. T.—Clerical asst. treas., Lagos, 1876; clk. P.W. dept., 1877; 3rd clk. customs and treas., Jan., 1879; acted on various occasions as chief clk. and warehouse keeper; clk. and cashier savings bk., 1887; 1st clk. and statistician customs, Jan., 1891.
LEMIERE, H.—District magistrate, Mauritius, 1883

LEMON, ARTHUR HENRY.—Educated at Merchant Taylors School and Exeter Coll., Oxford (scholar); B.A., 1887; cadet, Straits, 1888; priv. sec. to governor, Sep., 1889 to Jan., 1890; attached to Chinese protectorate, Penang, Aug., 1891; sec. to currency conte., June, 1893; ag. dist. offr., South Malacca, Dec., 1893; passed in Malay 1894; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Apr., 1894; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., May, 1895; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of councils, June, ag. 2nd assist. col. sec., Aug., 1895; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Oct., 1895; dist. offr. Prov. Wellesley, June, 1896, but continued to act at Malacca.

LENOX, ARKULL NAPIER OLIVER.—Sixth clerk, immigration department, British Guiana, 1882; 5th clerk, 1884; 4th clerk, 1885; awardee second class certificate in Hindustan, 1885; acted as 1st clk., 1887, and July, 1889, to Jan., 1890; 3rd class clk., land dept., Jan., 1891; 2nd class clk., April, 1891.
LEONARD, THE HON. JAMES WESTON—Atty.-gen., Cape of Good Hope, 1 July, 1882; member of leg. assembly, and of exec. council, 1881; Q.C.; resigned, 1884.

LETHERIDGE, H. BRIDGMAN.—Served in 2nd Queen's Royal Regt. 1877-84; ass't inspr. G. Coast Constab., Oct., 1886; on special service in Eastern Akim, 1887; adjt. G.C.C., Oct., 1888; on special political mission to Ashanti, Gaman, and Schwi, Nov., 1888, to Feb., 1889; inspr. G.C.C., July, 1889; ag. inspr.-gen., Sept., 1889, to Sept., 1890; local commandt., Cyprus police, Aug., 1891; suptdt., Hong Kong Prison, 1892.

LEVEY, GEORGE COLLINS, C.M.G. (1878).—Member legislative council, Victoria, 1861-8; was secretary to the Victorian commission, and acting commissioner in charge of the Victorian exhibits at the Paris Exhibition, 1878; secretary, Melbourne Exhibition, 1880-1; executive commissioner, Victoria, Amsterdam Exhibition, 1883; secretary, Adelaide Jubilee Exhibition, 1887, and Tasmanian Exhibition, 1894.
LEWIS, ARTHUR KENNEDY.—Clerk to the consul, reg'r. of births and deaths, West Indies, S. Leone, Apl., 1893; transferred to reg'r. of births, Oct., 1893.

LEWIS, E. CORNEWALL.—Entered Government office, St. Vincent, as supernumerary, May, 1877; clerk to Kingstown police magistracy, 1880; acting colonial registrar, 1883; acted provost marshal, 1884; acting colonial postmaster, 1885; acting registrar, 1886; deputy commr., Kingstown, 1886; acct. of public wks., Grenada, 1887; col. postmtr., St. Vincent, 1887.

LEWIS, J. P.—Educated at Mill Hill School and Queen's Uni., B.A., 1876; M.A., 1882; joined Ceylon civil service, July, 1877; act. pol. agent Mataura, Jan., 1881; ditto, Pt. Pedro, 1881; office asst. to govt. agent, N.P. Jaffna, 1883; mag., Gampula, 1884; act. office asst. to govt. agent, Cent. Prov., Kandy, 1885; act. commr. of requests, Colombo, 1885-6; office asst. to govt. agent, C.P., 1887; act. dist. judge, Mataura; assistant agent, Negombo, 1891; ditto Vavu Vilai Kulam, Apl., 1896.

LEWIS, JACOB WILLIAM.—Educated at Uxbridge Grammar School, Sierra Leone; served in customs' department, Jan., 1868; assistant serving waiter after competitive examination, 1869; extra clerk, colonial office, Feb., 1869; assistant private secretary to governor, May, 1872; clerk of legislative council, Mar., 1872; confidential clerk and clerk of the council at Gold Coast, 1873; governor's clerk, Feb., 1874; served on the Mongray and Bargroo expeditions, Dec., 1875; on an expedition to the Scarcies, Apr., 1878; on the Bompeh and Ribbep expedition, 1880, and on the Scarcies, Port Loko, Sulymah expeditions, 1885; also clerk to education, May, 1882; West African medal clasps, Yomnie expdn., 1887-8 and Taini expedition, 1892; clerk of exec. council, June, 1892; J.P. for the colony, 1896.
LEWIS, JACOB WM. (Jrnr.).—Produce customs dept., S. Leone, June, 1892; 2nd officer, June, 1895.

LEWIS, JOHN CHRISTOPHER.—Entered Colonial service, 1861; postmaster, San Fernando, 1869; received the thanks of inhabitants for services in 1882; acting 2nd clerk, general office, 1868; acting postmaster-general, 1889-91; commissioner for affidavits and received the thanks of the inhabitants of San Fernando in 1882 for his zeal in discharging duties, and the legislative council in 1889 passed a unanimous resolution that his services were deserving of recognition.

LEWIS, WALTER LLWELLYN, M.A.—Medallist, Queen's University (Irel.); called to bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1876; joined 1st and afterwards 5th, Wales and Chester circuit; stipendiary justice, Trinidad, Dec., 1884; reg'r. general and registrar of the supreme court, stipendiary, Port of Spain and Western India, St. George, Sept., 1887; actg. puisne, 1888, and other occasions; chairman of common, 1886; of committee on agriculture, tracts, 1888; and prepared agric. contracts, 1889; puisne judge, 1893; ag. chief justice, 1894.
LEY, ANDREW K.—Priv. sec. to gov., 1882; actg. col. treas. and auditor, 1882 to judge general court, 1884-5; magistrate of requests, and postmaster-general, S.
LLOYD, Jas. S. — Educated at Codrington College, Barbados; appointed to 3rd clerkship, customs, Barbados Nov., 1876; 2nd indoor officer, customs, Dec., 1890.

LOCH, The Rt. Hon. Lord Loch of Drylaw, 1st Baron (creat. 1895). Sir Henry Brougham Loch, G.C.B. (1892), G.C.M.G. (1887), K.C.B. (1880), C.B. (1860).—Entd. 3rd Bengal Cavalry, 1844; served Sutlej campaign 1845-6; A.D.C. to the commander-in-chief, Lord Gough; adjutant and 2nd in command, Skinner's Horse, till 1853; sent on special military service to Turkey to assist in organizing Turkish troops, with local rank of major, 1854; crossed with army from Varna to Crimea, 1854; accompanied Earl of Elgin's special embassy to China, 1857-58; attached to head-quarters of commander-in-chief during operations in the field; bearer to England of Treaty of Yeddo, 1858; secretary to Earl of Elgin's second embassy to China, 1860; attached to head-quarters of the army during military operations; was treacherously made prisoner and cruelly treated by the Chinese, while engaged in negotiations under flag of truce; brought home ratified treaty of Tien-tsin, and Convention of Pekin; private secretary to Right Hon. Sir George Grey, secretary of state for home department, 1861; lieut.-governor, Isle of Man, 1863 to 1882; colonel commandant, 4th battalion, Cheshire Regiment; Her Majesty's commissioner of woods, forests, and land revenue from 1882 to 1884; govr. of Victoria, 1881; of the Cape, 1889-95.

LOCKHART, J. H. STEWART, F.R.G.S., M.R.A.S.—Edinburgh University; Greek medalist, &c.; appointed, after competitive examination, to a Hong Kong cadetship, 1878; attached to the Colonial Office for one year, 1879; passed cadet, 1882; superintendent, opium revenue, March, 1883; assistant colonial secretary and assistant auditor-general, Aug., 1883, acting registrar general, 1884 and 1885; reg-gen., 1887, and M.L.C., 1889; member of ex. council, 1891; ag. col. secy., April, 1894; col. secy. and regr.-gen., 24th Mar., 1895.
LOCKHART, NORMAN.—4th revenue officer, Dominica, Feb., 1880; acting postmaster, 1881; acting excise officer, May, 1882; 3rd revenue officer, 1883; quarantine officer, 1886.

LOFTIE, ROWLEY C.—Admitted to the bar of Western Australia, 1865; master and registrar of the supreme court and keeper of records, 1870; and subsequently master in lunacy and registrar of the vice-admiralty court of the colony; acting commissioner of titles, 1875; police magistrate, Perth, 1878; acting government resident at King George's Sound, 1879; govt resident, southern districts, 1881.

LONGLEY, J WILBERFORCE.—Born 1849, B.A. Acadia College, N. Scotia, 1871, M.A.1875; called to the bar, N.S., Sept. 1875; commissioner supreme court, 1876; elected to Provincial Parliament June, 1882; sworn in member of executive, July, 1884; attorney-general, May, 1886; commissioner for revising and consolidating the law and framing Judicature Act, Oct., 1882; editorial writer "Acadian Recorder" since 1873.
LORANGER, L. O., Q.C.—Attorney-general, province of Quebec, Canada; appointed a judge of superior court of Quebec, 1882.

LORD, EDWARD WILLIAM.—Entered police department, St. Lucia, 1872; acted keeper of the prison, Oct., 1876, to Mar., 1877; sub-inspector of revenue and police, Jan., 1880; chief revenue officer and landing waiter, Jan., 1882.

LORNE, MARQUIS OF, P.C., K.T., G.C.M.G., M.P., The Rt. Hon. JOHN DOUGLAS SUTHERLAND CAMPBELL.—Born 1845, married, 1871, H.R.H. Princess Louise Caroline Alberta, 4th daughter of Her Majesty the Queen; sat in the House of Commons for Argyllshire from Feb., 1868, to Sept., 1878; private secretary to his father, the Duke of Argyll, at the India Office, Dec., 1868; is author of poetical and other works, including "Trip to the Tropics;" governor-general of Canada, 1878 to 1883; M.P. for Manchester, 1895.

LOVELL, EDWARD A., M.A., Ph.D. — Asst. comptroller of customs, Gold Coast Colony, Feb., 1890; coll. of customs, Lagos, July, 1891; ag. treasurer and member ex. and legis. councils, 1893-4-5-6; ag. col. sec., 1894-5.

LOVELL, FRANCIS HENRY, C.M.G. (1893).—Colonial surgeon, Sierra Leone, Dec., 1873; medical officer, immigration department, Mauritius, 1878; chief medical officer and president general board of health, 1879; member council of government, 1885; surgeon general and medical officer of health, and member legislative council, Trinidad, Sept., 1893; also member exec. council, 1894.

LOVETT, W.—Junior clerk, P.O., Tasmania, Feb., 1841, served there till he reached the position of chief clerk; accountant to Treasury, Nov., 1860; ag. deputy auditor, April, 1865; assistant colonial treasurer, 1867; auditor-general, 1873.

LOW, SIR HUGH, G.C.M.G. (1889) K.C.M.G. (1883), C.M.G. (1879).—Secretary to government of Labuan, 1848; police magistrate, 1850; administered the government of Labuan five times between 1865 and 1876; British resident, Perak, 1877; negotiated treaty of Protection with Sultan of Brunei, 1888; retired 1889.

LOW, WILLIAM.—Acting private secretary to Lieut.-Gov. Longden, Dominica, June, 1866, to Mar., 1867; private secretary to Lieut.-Gov. Bulwer and clerk of the patents, April, 1867, to Jan., 1869; and to Lieut.-Gov. Freeling, Feb. to July, 1869, acted as colonial secretary of Dominica, 1868; police magistrate in St. Vincent, May, 1874; acting senior police magistrate, Bridgetown, Barbados, Oct., 1878; acting police magistrate, S. District, Grenada, May, 1883; ag. Treas., Ap. to Oct., 1884; stip. mag. 1st dist. St. Lucia, Ap. 1887; represented Windward Islands at Quarter-time, Confe., Oct., 1888; visiting justice of prisons, Grenada and St. Lucia, ag. treas., Grenada, Dec., 1890, to July, 1892; commr. of Tobacco, 1892.

LOWE, JOHN HENRY, M.I.C.E., entered service of Nelson provin. govt., N.Z., as dist. engr. and surveyor, for goldfields, 1866; appd. warden of Nelson goldfields; entered service of col. govt., N.Z., as asst. engr., 1872; result. engr.-in-charge of rys. under construction, Otago, 1873; engr.-in-charge of rys. open for traffic, Middle Island, 1877; chief eng., N.Z. rys. dept., 1887.
LOWRIE, W.—M.A., R.Sc.; professor of agriculture and principal agri. coll., S. Australia, 1887.

LOWTHER, KT. HON. JAMES.—Born 1840, Ed. Westminster School and Trim. Coll., Canb., B.A., 1862, and M.A., 1866; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, 1864; is a magistrate and deputy lieutenant for the North Riding of York; parliamentary secretary to the poor law board, Aug. to Dec., 1868; parliamentary under-secretary for the colonies, Feb., 1874, to Feb., 1878. First elected for the city of York, July, 1865; privy councillor and chief secretary for Ireland, 11th Feb., 1878; sat for the city of York to Mar., 1890; M.P., North Lincolnshire, Aug., 1881, to Dec., 1885; M.P., Thanet Divn. of Kent, 1888.

LUCAS, CHARLES PRESTWOOD, B.A.—Educated at Winchester College, and Balliol College, Oxford; open exhibitioner; first class in modern languages, 1873, and first class in final classical schools, June, 1876; Chancellor's prize for Latin essay, 1877; called to the bar by the Society of Lincoln's Inn, Easter, 1883; appointed, after competitive examination, to be a clerk in the secretariat of the state for the colonies, April, 1877; private secretary to Sir R. Herbert, 1st Dec., 1881; Earl Granville, Feb., 1882; again to Sir R. Herbert, Aug., 1886; also chairman of Committee of Emigrants Information, 1st till 31st Oct., 1896; first class clerk, 1st Sept., 1892; author of Historical Geography of the British Colonies; principal clerk, 1st Sept., 1896.

LUCIE-SMITH, A. VAN W.—Educated at Rugby; solicitor, British Guiana, 1877; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1881; acting solicitor, British Guiana, 1882, to 1887; president, court, Famagusta, Cyprus, 1887; Limassol, acting Queen's advocate, 1893; result. Kingston, Jamaica, Nov., 1896.

LUCKIE, D.M., F.S.S.—Trained in a lawyer's office in Montrose, Scotland; in 1862 emigrated to N. Zealand, as editor and part proprietor of "Nelson Colonist," which he largely popularized; in 1872 was elected member of parliament for Nelson City; in 1873 became editor of "Southern Cross," and when it was amalgamated with the "New Zealand Herald," the leading journal in the colony, became chief editor of combined papers; Govt. insurance commr., 1876.

LUDLOW, SIR HENRY, KT. (1890).—Ed. Christ's Hospital and St. John's Coll., Camb., 1857, subsequently M.A. and fellow of St. John's College; obtained the studentship given by the Inn of Court, 1861; called to the bar, Lin. Inn, Jan., 1862; attorney-general, Trinidad, 1863; chief justice, Leeward Islands, 1865; retired, 1870.
LUKIS, W. R. FELLOWES.—Dist. engr., Ceylon govt. rly., Jan., 1890; ag. ass't engr., May, 1893, to June, 1895; asst. loco. Feb., 1896.

LUMB, CHARLES FREDERICK.—Educated Liverpool Institute (Yates exhibitioner); Downing Coll., Canb. (scholar and prizes in mathematics, natural science, and law; math. tripos, 1870; M.A., and L.L.M., 1877; LL.B., 1892; barrister of Lincoln's Inn, 1874; practised on Northern circuit; vice-president Liverpool Institute, and member of Liverpool school board; prosecutor for the mint, 1886; second puisne judge, Trinidad, 1887; liber edun. commiss., 1887; of bd. of educ.; member royal college council, 1888; of library committee, 1889; puisne judge, Jamaica, 1892.

LUMGARH, GEORGE.—C.I.K., G.P.O., Mauritius, 1862; corrig. clk. col. sec. dept., 1875; reg. 1879; sec. Woods and Forests Bd., Sept., 1882; sec. to exec. and leg. councils, and chief clk. sec. dept., June, 1882; ag. regtr. gen., Apr., 1884; storekeeper-gen., May, 1890; confirmed, 1892; ag. collr. of customs, August, 1895.

LUNT, JOHN, M.I.C.E.—Inspector of Ballarat rly., 1858; ditto Sandhurst and E. rlys., 1863; on surveys, Melbourne to Pake, 1866; district engineer, N.E. rly., Jan., 1868; Gippsland rly., Feb., 1875; engineer for maintenance, April, 1878; engineer for existing lines.

LUSHINGTON, CHARLES MORANT.—Writer, 1876; magistrate, 1878; asst. to settlement agent N.W. Province, 1883; dist. Kegalka, Mar., 1889; ditto, Ratnapura, July, 1889; ag. asst. govt. agent, Negombo, Oct., 1889; N'Eliva, 1892; at Matada, 1894; at N'Eliva.

LYNCH, E. R.—Librarian, legislative c. Jamaica, Oct., 1855; island storekeeper, 1865. The special commission of over and terminer for trial of offences committed during the outbreak of 1865; clerk, Kingston district court, Oct., 1867; St. Ann's Bay district court, Mar., 1871; office abolished, 1878; island secretary, administrator-general, and comptroller in bankruptcy, April, 1879; deputy keeper of records, Feb., 1879.
YNTCH, G. W. A.—Ed. at Westminster and Cambridge, Camb., B.A., 1883; M.R.C.S.; L.R.C.P., Lond., 1885; M.R., B.C., Camb., 1885; govt. med. officer, Fiji, 1890.
YNTCH, H. E.—Stipendiary magistrate, third district, St. Lucia, 2 Jan., 1871; first district, Oct., 1874; magistrate, St. Vincent, 1884.
YNTCH, W. W. Q.C.—Commissioner of Crown lands, Quebec, 1879.
YNE, WILLIAM JOHN.—Elected to New South Wales parliament, 1880; sec. public works, 1885-86; again, 1891; member exec. coun.
AASDORP, A. F. S.—Solicitor-general, Cape Colony, Sept., 1878.
AASDORP, CHRISTIAN GEORGE.—Attorney-general, Transvaal, Sept., 1878; resigned, Jan., 1879; puisne judge, supreme court, Cape, Nov., 1879.
ACARLTHUR, MALCOLM STEWART.—Cadet, Nov., 1895; ag. dep. regt. of deeds, Penang, 1895.

ACALAY, HERRERT S. H., Assoc. M.I.C.E., G.S., &c.—Ed. C.M.S. gram. schl., Lagos, 1881; P.W. dept., Sept., 1881; clk. of grants and gentsman, Oct., 1884; served in borough and county engr. off., Plymouth, 1890-3; survy. of roads, Lagos, Sept., 1893.
ACALAY, JOHN DAVIDSON—Extra clerk, secretary's office, Sierra Leone, 1874; in ens., 1875; tide waiter, 1877; landing waiter, 1878; clerk of customs, 1883.

ACBRIDE, ROBERT KNOX, C.M.G. (1890).—Intending officer, public works department, 1866; irrigation assistant, 1872; provincial assistant, north central province, 1874; Uva, 1876; southern province, 1878; and central province, 1882; director of public works, 1885.

ACALLUM, MAJOR HENRY EDWARD, R.E., G. (1887)—Entered the Royal Military Academy, Woolwich (July, 1869), passed out first lieutenant (1871); after two years practical training at Chatham, proceeded to Portsmouth, and appointed superintendent of telegraphy, northern District. Transferred to the office of the inspector general of fortifications, Private secretary to Governor Sir William Jervois, S.S. and assisted him on various missions to the natives of the Malay Peninsula; mentioned in despatches for the assistance given in preparing a report for the defence of Singapore, and again in 1876, for services in connection with the commission inquiry; superintending engineer military works, Hong Kong, April, 1877; April (1878) selected to proceed to Singapore to take measures for the defence of that port. Appointed to the office of the inspector of works, royal naval yard, July, 1879, to July, 1880, when appointed deputy colonial engineer, Straits Settlements. Is a Rook medalist of 1871; the annual Fowke medalist for 1874; is an associate of institution of civil engineers, &c.; colonial engineer and surveyor-general, Straits Settlements, 1884; member of the executive and legislative councils. President, Singapore municipality, Feb., 1881 to July, 1886; appointed to construct new fortifications of Singapore, March, 1885; commissary Singapore vol. art., 1888; Special commr., in Pahang during disturbances, 1891; received thanks of S. of S.

McDONALD, Ernest E.—Ed. Sydenham coll., Kent; super. clk., col. sec.'s office, Brit. Honduras, 1887; clk. to atty.-gen., Jan., 1888; clk. col. engineer's dept., Sept., 1888; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, June, 1892.

MacDONALD, George.—1st class, Queen's scholar, 1876; senr. master, academy, Banbury, 1877; senr. mr., British schl., London, N.W., 1878; ditto, Eastbourne Schls., 1881; senr. asst. to H.M.S. inspr. of schls., Marylebone, 1883; director of edu., G. Coast, June, 1893. Is author of several schoolbooks and manuals.

McDONALD, Hon. James, Q.C.—Born 1828; called to the bar of Nova Scotia, 1851; created a Q.C. 1867; was chief railway commissioner of Nova Scotia from June, 1863, to Dec., 1864, when appointed financial secretary, which office he held until the union of 1867; one of the commissioners (representing Nova Scotia) to open trade relations between the West Indies, Mexico, and Brazil, and the B.N.A. provinces, 1865-66; sworn of the privy council of Canada, 17th Oct., 1878, and appointed minister of justice of Canada; sat in Nova Scotia assembly from 1859 until 1867, and from 1871 until July, 1872, when returned to the House of Commons; now chief justice of Nova Scotia; joint commissioner to inquire into charges agst. atty.-gen. of Jamaica, Dec., 1893.
MACDONALD-PATERSON, Thomas—Post-master-general, Queensland, 1885-7.
MACDOUGALL, John Lorne.—Auditor-general of Canada, 1st Aug., 1878.

MACDOUGALL, The Hon. William, C.B. (1867).—Born 1822, educated at Victoria University, Cobourg; admitted an attorney, Upper Canada, 1847; called to the bar, Hilary, 1862; founded (1848) the "Canada Farmer," subsequently merged in the "Canadian Agriculturist," which he published and edited until 1858; founded (1859) the "North American," a Reform newspaper of which he was chief editor until merged in the Toronto "Daily Globe," in 1857; was the leading political writer on the "Daily Globe," from 1857 to 1860; represented Canada at the New York Exhibition, 1858; was secretary of the Constitutional Reform Association of Upper Canada, 1859; a member of the executive council and commissioner of crown lands, Canada, May, 1862, to March, 1864, provincial secretary June, 1864, and acting minister of marine from July, 1866, until the union of the provinces, 1867; sworn of the queen's privy council in Canada, and minister of public works for the dominion, from 1st July, 1867, lieut.-governor of Rupert's Land and the north-west territories, Oct., 1869; chairman of the commission appointed to open trade relations with the West Indies, Mexico, and Brazil, 1865-6; delegate to the Charlottetown Union Conference, 1864; to that at Quebec, in the same year; and to the Colonial Conference in London to complete terms of Union of R.N.A. colonies, 1867; delegate to England, with late Sir G. E. Cartier, to confer with imperial authorities on the defence of the dominion, and for the acquisition of the north-west territory, 1868; commissioner for Canada for the settlement of the north-western border of the province, 1871; sent to England by dominion government, 1873, as special commissioner to confer with imperial government on subject of fisheries; returned to Canadian legislature in 1858, where he sat until the union of 1867, when returned to the house of commons; elected to Ontario assembly in May, 1875; resigned Sept., 1878, and again returned to house of commons; retired from public life, 1882, and resumed practice of his profession in Ottawa. Is a Q.C.
MACGREGOR, JAMES COMYN.—2nd lieut. 7th R.R., 1881; lieut., 1881; sub-inspector, New Providence since 1877.

MACGREGOR, MALCOLM.—Pioneer officer (visional), Ceylon, 14th April, 1870; superintending officer, 1st June, 1875; superintending officer (fixed), 1st Jan., 1878; acting provincial assistant, North-Central Province, 21st Sept., 1878; superintending officer, Dikoya, Feb., 1882; resumed duties as superintending officer, Aug., 1883; resumed duties as superintending officer, Dec., 1883; acting second financial and office assistant, 1st Feb., 1884; appointment confirmed, July, 1885; acting financial and office assistant, Sept., 1887; resumed duties as second financial and office assistant, 16th Oct., 1887; acting medical and office assistant, Feb., 1888; district medical officer, Gampola; passed examination, junior division, Oct., 1874; and senior division, Oct., 1877.

MACGREGOR, SIR WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1889).—Educated at Aberdeen, Glasgow, London, and Paris; M.B., Abdn., 1872; M.D., 1874; won gold medallist, 1872; formerly resident physician and resident physician, Glasgow Royal Infirmary; resident physician, Royal Lunatic Asylum, Aberdeen; assistant government medical officer, Seychelles, 1873; superintendent, lunatic asylum, Mauritius, 1874; chief medical officer, Fiji, 1875; also receiver-general, and member of executive and legislative councils, Jan., 1877; acted as registrar-general, agent-general of federation, and commissioner of lands; engaged in the suppression of the disturbances in the islands of Viti Levu, for which he was voted a salary of £100; joint commissioner, 1877, for settlement of debts due from natives and Europeans, and for the settlement of all pecuniary claims against the late government of Fiji; member of the Native Regulation Board, 1877; proceeded to Tonga, 1879, to report on the financial condition of that country; acting colonial secretary, Nov., 1883, to June, 1884, and Oct., 1874, to May, 1875; administrator of the government, acting high commissioner and consul-general for the Western Pacific, Jan. to Aug., 1884; representative of Fiji at the first session of Federal Council of Australasia, at Hobart, July, 1885; Albert medal of the 2nd class (1881), and the Clarke gold medal of the Royal Society of Australasia in 1885, for saving life at sea; dpty. admr. of Fiji, Sept., 1883, and July, 1886; admr., British New Guinea, 1888; Nov., 1895.

MACLURE, WM.—Appointed, after open competition, a clerk in the lower division of the civil service, and assigned to the audit office, 10th Nov., 1889; transferred to valuation office, Ireland, 1st Nov., 1890, and to colonial office, 1st June, 1891.

MACLWAITH, THE HON. SIR THOMAS, M.G. (1882), LL.D., M.L.A.—Born at Ayrshire, 1835, and educated there and at Glasgow University. He arrived in Melbourne in 1854, and was appointed engineer to the Government railways; in 1861 engaged in squattting pursuits in Queensland, and returned there in 1870; first entered Parliament in 1872, and represented the Warrego and Maranoa divisions many years; in 1873 he joined the Macalister ministry as minister for works, but resigned not long afterwards; in 1878 he was elected member for Mulgrave, and, on the defeat of the Douglas ministry in 1879, he formed a new Government, in which he took the position of premier; in consequence of two adverse votes, resigned, Nov., 1883. Among his most prominent acts was the annexation of New Guinea, a step that did not receive the sanction of the Imperial Government; in 1883 the freedom of his native place was conferred on him; he was made an Associate of the Institute of Civil Engineers in 1891, and had the dignity of L.L.D. conferred upon him by his Alma Mater. Sir Thomas retired from public life in June, 1886, but re-entered in 1888, when he was elected member for North Brisbane. The result of the general election in 1888 was the defeat of the Griffith ministry; Sir Thomas was then called on to form a government, which he succeeded in doing, taking the position of premier, treasurer, and chief secretary, which he resigned on account of ill health in 1889; col. treasurer in Griffith ministry, 1890; premier, chief sec. and minister of rly.s, 1892; resigned, 1893.

McINNIS, Lieut.-Col. EDWARD BOWATER, late 9th Lancers, C.M.G. (1890).—Entered the army, Mar., 1865; was adjt. 9th Lancers for several years, including campaigns in Afghanistan, 1878-79-80; present at the operations near Kabul, and on Sir Frederick Roberts' march from Kabul to Kandahar, and battle of Mazra (Kandahar), 1st Sept., 1880; bronze star for Kabul, Kandahar march; medal and clasps for Kabul and Kandahar; insp. gen., G. Coast constab., Mar., 1887, commandant of local forces, Trinidad, 1890; inspector-general, Br. Guiana, 1891.

McKEAN, Lieut.-Col. Alexander Chalmers, C.M.G. (1891).—Cornet, Iniskilling Dragoons, Oct., 1871; A.D.C., Bengal, 1880; served in Transvaal war, 1881; staff officer, Zululand war (Eshowe column), 1883; D.A.A. and Q.M.G., South Africa, 1884; actg. D.A.A.G., Natal, 1886; surveyor to Zululand and New Republic Boundary Com., 1886; assist. commr., Zululand, 1887; commr. of Basuto levies in Zulu disturbances, 1888; was honourably mentioned and made brevet lieut.-col. for services; asst. mil. sec. to gov., Malta, 1889.
MACKENZIE, DONALD.—Chief inspector of police, Penang, Dec., 1887; ag. supt. police, Sungai Ujong, Apr., 1890.

MACKENZIE RIVER, Bishop of, THE RIGHT REV. WM. CARPENTER BOMPAS, D.D. (consec. 1884).—Curate of Sutton-le-Marshe and Alford, Lincolnshire, and New Radford, Notts., 1859-65; missionary, Mackenzie River, 1865; bishop of Athabasca, 1874; of Mackenzie River, 1884.
McKERROW, J.—Formerly surveyor-general, and secretary for crown lands and mines, New Zealand; chief commissioner of rly.s, under Govt. Rlys. Act, 1887, Jan., 1889.

McKINNEY, JOHN A.—Ag. 3rd rev. offir., Br. Honduras, April, 1889; confirmed Aug., 1889; ag. 2nd aud. clerk, col. secy.'s office, Mar., 1890; 4th clerk, treas., customs, and P.O., Jan., 1891; 3rd clerk, Feb., 1891.

McKINNEY, W. J., C.M.G. (1890).—Postmaster, British Honduras, 1859; served as ensign, Belize volunteers, in 1866-67, against the hostile Indians of Yucatan; mentioned in despatches, and received the thanks of the legislative assembly for his services; acting police magistrate, 1875; treasurer, Sept., 1880.
MACKINNON, LOUIS FULLERTON.—Accountant, savings bank, Jamaica, 1870; manager of government railway 1879.
McLELLAN, David.—Member of House of Assembly and exec. coun., New Brunswick, provincial sec. and receiver gen.; president of exec. council, 1890.

McLEOD, Chas. Augustus.—Entered Cape civil service after examination, 1857; inaugurated a system of deeds registry for Griqualand East, 1872; chief clerk, office of regr. of deeds, 1879; assistant regr. of deeds, Nov., 1891; Lieut. and Capt. in D.E.O.V.R., 1882-5; is a J.P. for Cape Town and District.
MACLEOD, Lt.-Col. James Farquharson, C.M.G. (1870).—Served in Red River Expedition, 1870; judge of Albert District, N.W. territories, Canada.

MACLEOD, Murdoch.—Clerk in the government secretary's office, British Guiana, 1883; 2nd clerk, registrar-general's office, 1885; clk. of ct. of Essequibo River dist., 1892; agt. stip. mag. N.W. dist., June, 1893 to 15 Oct. 1894, and from June, 1895.
McLEOD, Neil.—Called to the bar, Prince Edward Island, 1872; member of house of assembly since 1879; provincial secretary and treasurer, 1879 to 1882; atty-gen. and premier, 1889.
MACLEOD, Norman Magnus, C.M.G. (1880).—Late captain 74th Highlanders. Was commandant of Swazi contingent against Sikukuni, 1879.
MACLEOD, P. F., M.D.—Medical officer, parish of St. George, Grenada, 21 July, 1881.

McLEOD, William.—Sub-inspector, Jamaica constabulary, Dec., 1866; 3rd class inspector, Jan., 1869; 2nd class, Oct., 1871; 1st class, Oct., 1873; acting inspector-general, Sept. to Dec., 1883, May to Oct., 1885, and April to Sept., 1886; June to Sept., 1888; May to Oct., 1890; June to Dec., 1891; and May to Nov., 1894.
MACLURE, John Stevenson Grant.—Ck. Leg. Coun., Bahamas, Mar., 1888; acted for a time as clk. in col. sec. off. and regr. of deeds off.; clk. police office, Aug., 1890.

MACLURE, William Malcom Grey, M.D., M.R.C.S., England.—Coroner, Nassau, N.P., Bahamas, 1865; physician to prison and police force, and health officer, 1873; medical inspector and chairman board of health, 1880; member of house of assembly from 1866 to 1879, when appointed to legislative council.

McNAIR, J. F. A., Major, Royal (late Madras) Artillery (retired), C.M.G. (1879).—Educated at King's College, London, and student at the School of Mines; entered the Madras artillery in 1845; served in India, the Straits Settlements, and Labuan; private secretary to the governor, Straits Settlements, 1857, and subsequently executive engineer and superintendent of convicts, Singapore; chief engineer, Straits Settlements, on their transfer to the crown in 1867; accompanied the governor to Siam, Aug., 1867; surveyor-general, Straits Settlements, 1872; chief commissioner of Perak, throughout the disturbances in that country in 1875-6; and engaged in the affair at Kotah Lamah, on the Perak river; special commissioner to Selangore, to inquire into piracy, and to Perak in connection with the Pangkor treaty, 1874; again to Siam, in Aug. of that year, on special mission; and again, early in 1875, with Sir A. Clarke, in connection with unsettled state of affairs there; special mission to the State of Sungai Ujong, 1878; secretary to Sir W. Robinson's mission to Siam in Nov., 1878, to present the G.C.M.G. to the king; author of "Perak and the Malays," &c.; act. lieut.-governor and resident councillor of Penang from Feb., 1881, to Aug., 1884, when he retired.

MCNEIL, Major-General Sir John Cartairs, K.C.M.G. (1880), (K.C.B., 1882), (C.M.G., 1870), V.C.—Served during the campaign of 1858 in India as A.D.C. to Sir F. Lugard during the siege and capture of Lucknow; in 1861-3 A.D.C. to Sir D. Cameron in New Zealand; commanded the Tipperary flying column during the Fenian disturbances, 1866-67; served on the staff of the Red River expedition under Sir G. Wolseley in 1870, and in the Ashanti war, 1873-74; A.D.C. to H.R.H. the Duke of Cambridge, Jan. 1874, and an equerry to Her Majesty in Aug. following; served in the Egyptian expedition, 1882, personally attached to the Duke of Connaught; served in the Soudan Expedition, 1885; commanded troops at the action of Tobrek, in which clasp was granted.
MACPHERSON, Lt.-Col. John.—Accountant and chief clerk store branch department of militia and defence, Canada, Sept., 1872; director of store and keeper of militia properties, Nov., 1880.

McTURK, Michael.—Revenue officer and assessor, Essequebo, 1872; commissn. of taxation, Essequebo district, 1873; special mag. of suptd. cr. lds. and forests, Essequebo, 1876; govt. gold mining officer, Br. Guiana, 1887.
MADDEN, Sir John, Q.C., Kt. Bach. (1893).—Chief justice of Victoria, 1893; administrator to govt., 1893 and 1895.
MADDEN, W.—President of the Board of Land and Works, and commissioner, crown lands and survey, Victoria; 19th Aug., 1881, to 8th Mar., 1888.
MAGRATH, Thos. Henry.—Junior clerk, P.O., Tasmania, 1853; chief clerk, 1869; comptroller of money orders, 1879 and of savings bank, 1882; sec. to P.O., 1888.
MAHONY, Lieut.-Col. John, C.M.G. (1879).—Paymaster, army pay department, in the war; retired with the honorary rank of Lieut.-Col. Mar., 1884.

MAINWARING, Captain K. H. A., R.N.—Entered royal navy Sept., 1850; lieut. May, 1855; served in Mediterranean as flag lieut. to Arthur Fanshawe and Sir Henry Codrington; served in China 1863 till 1868; commander, Oct., 1867, on board H.M.S. "Aboukir," guardship at Jamaica, Oct., 1868, till July, 1871, when he was appointed to the "Chameleon," in the Pacific; retired as captain in Oct., 1873; stipendiary magistrate, Jamaica, April, 1874; acting inspector-general of police, 1875 to 1876; harbour master, Kingston, 1876; and asst. pol. mag., 1876-8; pol. mag., 1888-91, when that office was abolished.

MALCOLM, Ormond Drimmie.—Called to the bar of the Bahamas, July, 1861; acting p.s. magistrate in 1864 and 1866; elected member of the house of assembly in 1865, speaker, 1868; ass. judge, court of common pleas, 1875; queen's counsel in 1876; acting attorney-general, 1876, and again in 1880; confirmed, Mar., 1882; agt. of justice, July, 1883, to Feb., 1884; agt. admin., June, 1895.

MALING, Captain Irwin Charles, C.M.G. (1892).—Educated at Wimbledon School and Royal Military Academy; late 23rd Loyal Welsh Fusiliers; deputy magistrate and collector, Nadia district, Bengal, 1857; served throughout the Indian mutiny, 1857-59; present many engagements (medal); ensign, 1861, 82. Princess Victoria's Regiment; private secretary and A.D.C. to the governor, West Coast of Africa, 1868; lieut. 35th Royal Sussex Regt., 1870; private secretary to the Marquis of Normanby, governor of Queensland, 1871, and New Zealand, 1872; colonial secretary and registrar-general of Canada, 1879; administered the government in 1880-82, 1884, 1885, and 1886-7; actg. col. sec., Leeward Islands, 1888-9; and representative at sanitary conference at Demerara, 1888; actg. min., 1889; admin. of St. Vincent, 1889; retired, 1893.

MALCHSCH, CHARLES CHRISTIAN, A.M.I.C.E.—Surveyor, works department, admiralty, 1866; chief engineer, German admiralty works, Kiel, 1873; surveyor state engrs. dept., California; surveying Sacramento and Fraser Rivers, &c., California and British Columbia, 1875; exploring for mosa for Chinese govt., 1881 to 1883; assist. engineer, P.W. Dept., Hong Kong, 1884; suptd. r. lds., 1892; ag. assessor of rates, 1894; J.P., 1894.

IAMO, LOUIS ARNETT.—Clerk in chief secretaries office, Cyprus, 1880; higher division clerk, 1881; senior clerk, receiver-general's department, 1882; acted as treasurer, 1888-89; clk. chief sec., 1894.

LAN, COLONEL ALEXANDER.—Served with field force under the late Brigadier Murray, 1862; employed on the 30-mile radius ringhai during the summer of 1863 (medal); with the Anglo-Chinese contingent in Colonel Den's Taiping campaigns of 1860-64, being present during the operations before Soochow, and siege and storming of Chang-chow (Chinese Imperial Star 2nd class); in 1868-69 stationed in Formosa, and assisted the U.S. consul Mr. W. A. Pickering, C.M.G., in concluding a friendly settlement with the savage tribes; part in the negotiations at Fort Zelandia and withdrawal of British force (thanked in despatches); selected in 1873 to raise a corps of militia police for the Treaty district of Newchwang, northern Manchuria, at that time infested by mounted banditti, and subsequently commanded force embodied (thanks of the British and Indian ministers, and of the Imperial High Commissioner, with the brevet of colonel in the Chinese army, and the cross of the Italian Crown, 4th class); served with the Nile expedition of 1884-45, as head officer and staff officer, and afterwards as lieutenant at Dal (medal with clasp and Khedive star); A.D.C. to General Valentine Baker, 1884, in 1885, and actg. dep. inspr.-general of garrison in 1886-7-8 (thanks of Egyptian Government, and Order of the Osmanli, 4th class); dt. of local forces, Trinidad and Tobago, 1887.
ANISTY, J. F.—Traffic superintendent, Natal government railways, 1878; dep. prot. of Indian grants, 1892.
ANN, JOHN.—Clerk in insolvency office, Nth Australia, 1862; clerk of local court, 1868; surveyor of land titles office, 1870; to commissioners of public works, 1875.

ANN, MAJOR-GENERAL J. R., R.E., C.M.G. (1).—Entered R.E., 1840; surveyor-general Mauritius, 1856 to 1861; director of roads, 1862; surveyor-general of Jamaica, 1867; resigned, 1868, and re-appointed in Feb., 1874; official C.C., 1874; retired, 1886.
ANNERS-SUTTON, F. H. A.—Supervisor customs, G. Coast Colony, Sept., 1891; comptor's clerk, Feb., 1892; priv. sec. to governor, Jan., 1892; clerk of exec. and leg. councils, April, 1892.
MANNING, F. N., M.D.—Med. suptdt hospital for insane, Gladesville, N.S. Wales, Nov., 1867; inspector-general of the insane, N.S. Wales, 1879.

MANSEL, GEORGE, C.M.G. (1891).—Served in the Weenen Yeomanry through the Langalibalele rebellion in 1873; served as sub-inspector and inspector in the Natal Mounted Police, through the Zulu war, 1879 (medal with clasp), and Boer war, 1880 and 1881; raised and commanded the Reserve Territory Carabiniers from 1883 to 1887, when the force was changed into the Zululand police; served in this force through all disturbances in Zululand from 1883 to 1888; twice mentioned in despatches as having shown conspicuous coolness and gallantry in action.

MANSERGH, CORNEWALL LEWIS WARWICKSHIRE.—Clerk, crown lands office, Cape Town, May, 1880; 2nd class clk. and private secretary to commissioner of crown lands and public works, May, 1884; first class clerk, April, 1892; chief clerk to the assistant commissioner of railways and public works, October, 1892.

MARCHAND, FELIX GABRIEL.—Member of the legislative assembly, Quebec, for the county of St. John's in 1867, re-elected in 1871, 1875, 1878, 1881, and in 1886; entered the cabinet as provincial secretary in 1878, and minister of crown lands in 1879; chosen speaker of the assembly in 1887; in Jan., 1862, organized the 21st bat. of infantry, and was apptd. lieut.-col., Canadian militia, 1866; during the Fenian invasions of 1866 and 1870, took active service with his batn., and commanded a brigade on the frontier; is an author of works in prose and verse; has received the decoration of officer of public instruction of France, and is F.R.S. of Canada, and of many other literary institutions.

MARSH, EDWARD HOWARD, B.A.—Ed. at Westminster and Cambridge; Scholar, Trin. Coll., 1891; 1st class classical tripus, 1893; senior chancellor's medal for classics, 1895; 1st class with distinction classical tripus, Part II, and B.A., 1895; apptd. after competitive examn. 2nd class clk., Colonial Office, Sept. 29, 1896.

MARSH, SIR WILLIAM HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1887), C.M.G. (1881).—Clerk to procurer-general, Mauritius, Feb., 1848; crown solicitor, April, 1854; chief clerk, colonial secretary's office, Oct., 1859; special immigration commissioner to India, May, 1865; junior assistant colonial secretary, April, 1866; senior assistant colonial secretary, 1867; sole assistant, 1870; acting colonial secretary, 1875-6; auditor-general, April, 1876; colonial secretary and auditor-general, Hong Kong, 1879; acting auditor and accountant-general in Cyprus, 1881; administered government Hong Kong on six occasions; retired 1887.
MARSHAL, FRED WM.—Supervisor of customs, G. Coast, May, 1892; principal clk. col. sec. office, Sept., 1892; priv. sec. to ag. gov., Dec., 1893, to March 1894; local auditor, Lagos, June, 1894.

MARSHALL, ARTHUR LACY.—Clerical assistant, immigration department, British Guiana, Dec., 1875; supernumerary clerk, 1876; clerical assistant to colonial secretary, Tobago, 1879; acting 2nd revenue officer, 1881; confirmed, May, 1882; provisional 1st revenue officer, Nov., 1884; con. April, 1885; chief clerk, treas., July, 1885.
MARSHALL, LIBUT.-GENERAL FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1879).—For services rendered in the African war, 1879.
MARTIN, JOSEPH.—Member of leg. assembly, and exec. council, Manitoba; attorney-general and commr. of railways and lands.

MARTIN, Louis.—Volunteer in surveyor-general's department at Mauritius, Dec., 1859; clerk in the audit office, Jan., 1860; junior examiner, Mar., 1862; senior examiner, Sept., 1869; chief clerk, immigration department, Mar., 1880; colonial postmaster and supt. of telegraphs, Sept., 1892.

MARTIN, COL. SIR RICHARD EDWARD ROWLEY, K.C.M.G. (1895), C.M.G. (1888).—Inskilling Dragoons.—Commanded mounted infantry at Cetywayo's installation, Zululand, 1883; commandant of general depot, Bechuanaland field force, 1884; major Bechuanaland border police, 1885 to 1887; was Br. commissur. presidt. Portuguese-Swazi Boundary Commission, and Br. commissur. on special mission to Tongoland, 1888; proceeded on visit of inquiry to Swaziland, 1889; secretary to British commission to Swaziland, 1889; British member of temporary joint commission of admin., 1889-95; commandant of police and dep. consm., Rhodesia, Mar., 1896.
MARTIN, S. B.—Ed. Wesleyan Mission Sch., Cape Coast; 6th class offr., customs, G. Coast, Sept., 1890; elk. to director of telegraphs and storekeeper, Aug., 1891; elk. and accontnt., July, 1893.
MARTINS, FRED. G.—Ed. C.M.S. coll. inst., Lagos; junior bailiff, supreme court, June, 1884; clerk to queen's advocate, Aug., 1886; storekeeper, P.W. dept., Jan., 1891; audit elk., Jan., 1896.
MARTINS, G. N.—Ed. C.M.S. Gram. Schl., Lagos; messenger, audit, and P.O., 1889; customs, outdoor officer, 1890; ag. elk., P.O., 1893; 4th elk. of customs, 1893; 3d elk., and statistician, Mar., 1894.

MARTINS, PEDRO JOSIAH.—Supernumerary clerk, treas., Accra, Oct., 1883; junior clerk, governor's office, 1884; storekeeper, P.W. dept., Lagos, 1885; audit clerk, Jan., 1889; in charge of audit office, Apr. to Nov., 1889, Dec., 1889 to Aug., 1890; Aug., 1891, to Mar., 1892, and again Aug., 1893.
MASKEW, W. W.—Resident magistrate, Middleburg, Cape Colony, 1875; civil commissioner, Clan William, Aug., 22, 1884.
MAISON, ARTHUR WIER, B.A.—Puisne judge sup. ct., Natal, 1896.

MAISON, CLAYTON T., M.I.C.E., J.P.—Educated King Edward's School, Birmingham; from 1874 to 1877 was engaged in the railway branch of the department of public works, New South Wales; resident engineer of the Geraldton and Northampton railway, Western Australia, 1877; superintended the construction of Point Moore Lighthouse and other public works in the Victoria district; afterwards resident engineer for four years of the Eastern Railway in the same colony; acting commissioner of railways on two occasions during 1882 and 1883; was provisionally appointed in 1884 director of public works and commissioner of railways; and member of the executive and legislative councils, general manager and maintenance engineer, June, 1885; commissioner of railways and member of exec. coun., Jan., 1890; after the introduction of responsible government, was appointed collector of customs, June, 1891.

MAISON, J. D.—Educated at "The College," Llandoverry, and the London University; a writer in the Ceylon civil service, 1872; acting police magistrate, Panadura, Sep., 1872; acting assistant government agent, Galle, Nov., 1872; police magistrate, Balapitymodara, June, 1873, to continue to act at Galle; acting police magistrate, Galle, 1876; landing surveyor, Galle, 1876; acting district judge, Matara, 1876; acting police magistrate, 1877; assistant collector of customs, Jaffna, 1877; police magistrate, Colombo, 1878; commissioner of requests and police magistrate, Galle, 1879; police magistrate, Colombo, 1888; dep. col. of customs, Colombo, Dec., 1891; pol. mag., Colombo, May, 1894.
MASON, L. H.—Protector of Indian immigrants, Natal, 1883.

MASSIAH, J. P.—Police magistrate of Bridgetown and St. Michael, Barbados, Jan., 1872; parochial treasurer of St. John's, 1872 to 1873; acting coroner of St. John's and St. Philip's several occasions; acting police magistrate of St. John's and St. Philip's, 1889, to Feb., 1891; senior police magistrate, Bridgetown, Jan., 1895.

MASSON, THE HON. LOUIS FRANCOIS ROQUE.—Born 1833; called to the bar of Lower Canada, 1859; was brigade-major, 8th district of Lower Canada, from 1863 to 1865; holds the rank of lieut.-colonel in the militia; entered Canadian parliament, 1867; sworn to privy council of Canada and appointed minister of militia and defence, 19th Oct., 1878; retired, called to the senate, 1882, and to the legislative council of the province of 1890. Quebec, lieutenant-governor of Quebec, 1884-7; senator, 1890.

MATHEWS, THOMAS VINCENT.—Chief officer, receiver-general and treasurer's department, Bahamas, 16th October, 1874; third elk., 1st March, 1875; acting private secretary and administrator, E. B. A. Taylor, from 22nd September to 16th December, 1879; acting 2d clerk and book-keeper, Mar., acting collector of customs, 1883; 2d clerk and bookkeeper, 1884.
MATHIEU, M.—Puisne judge, superior court, Quebec, 1881.

MATTHEWS, MAYNARD REGINALD NEWTON—Jun. elk. telegraph office, Durban, 1878; postmaster, Bothas Hill, Verulam, and Stanger, 1879; elk. R. M.'s off., Durban, 1880; field postmaster, Durban, and sec. wreck commn'st., etc., 1881; elk. elk., 1886; ag. elk. mag. ct. and reg. rec., elk. Durban and Umzini, 1886-7; elk. R. M.'s reg. circuit ct., subdistrict of stps., and dist. of peace, Klip River, Dec., 1887; J. P., Klip River, divn., 1894; has acted frequently as R. M.'s River; ag. R. M., Inanda divn., Feb., 1895.

MAUNSELL, HORATIO EDMUND.—B.A. M.A. and M.Ch., Trinity College, Dublin, 1867; medical officer, public hospital, Kingston, Jan., Nov., 1876; served on army medical staff, 1868 to Oct., 1872, during three years of period served in Jamaica; appointed to govern medical service, Jamaica, Sept., 1876; appointed St. Ann's Bay, Feb., 1881.

MAURICE, MORGAN SYDNEY.—Passed service examination, 2nd grade, in Mauritius, 1874; confidential clerk to the royal commission to inquire into the treatment of Indian immn. in Mauritius, in 1873; acted as senior ass. district clerk in the district and stipendiary surveyor of Black River in 1874; clerk and interpreter to the British consul, Island of Reunion, resigned; a sworn translator of Tamil supreme court, Mauritius, 1878; employed short time in the colonial engineer's dept., Natal, in 1879; and subsequently served as dental clerk on the staff of Major-General Hon. Sir H. H. Clifford; audit clerk in the general manager of telegraphs, Cape, Oct., and shortly thereafter transferred as conf. clerk to Sir George Strahan; 3rd class clerk.

MAURITIUS, 5th Bishop of, Wm. Walsh, L.A., D.D.—Of St. Alban's Hall and Merton Coll., Oxford; asst. secr., C.M.S., 1865-70; chaplain in Rome, 1878-9; vicar of St. Mary, Newington, 1879-86; mission chaplain to Bishop of London, 1886; prebendary of St. Paul's, 1889; Bishop of Mauritius, 1890.

MAVROGORDATO, Alexander Stephen—Dispenser Civil Hospital, Nicosia, Cyprus, June, 1879; clerk to principal forest officer; June, 1881; asst. as forest officer, May, 1883, to Dec., 1884; engaged on the locust destruction as paymaster, 1883 to 1886; and as asst. to commr., magistrata, 1889-92; examiner of accounts, audit pt., May, 1886; transferred to rec. gen.'s department, April, 1891; asst. in survey and forest limits, Dec., 1892; also president survey limitation commn.; superintendent locust destruction operations, 1893-6; asst. in forest and agricultural depts., June, 1895; financial asst., W. Dept., Oct., 1896.

MAVROGORDATO, Theodore E.—Born 1871; educated at Berlin; clerk to commissioner, magistrata, 1881; clerk and interpreter to Nicosia magistrata, police, 1882; asst. inspr., police, Limassol, 1883; inspector, police, Kyrenia, Sept., 1884; commt. of police, June, 1895.

MAXWELL, Francis R. O.—Cadet, Sarawak vice, June, 1872; asst. result, 1872; result, tong Lapai and Sarabas, Jan., 1876; result, class, 1st divn. (Sarawak proper), and member prime Council, Oct., 1880; retired June, 1895; w.a.g. consul for Sarawak, Brunei, and North Borneo, and ag. resident, Labuan.
MAXWELL, Joseph P., M. Inst. C.E.—Public works department, New Zealand, 1874; general manager, N. Z. railways, 1880; rly. commissioner for Rlys. Act, 1887, Jan., 1889.

MAXWELL, Joseph Renner, M.A., B.C.L.—Educated at Sierra Leone grammar school and Allerton Coll., Ox.; 2nd class honours, jurisprudence, A., 1879; M.A., 1880, B.C.L. (honours), 1884; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, in 1880; and fitted for three years at the bar of the Gold Coast Colony; Queen's advocate, Gambia, Mar., 1884; also registrar of deeds, and colonial registrar; acting superintendent, police, Aug., 1885; April, 1886; member of leg. coun., 1886; chief magistrate, 1887.

MAXWELL, William D.—Educated at Codrington College, Barbados; schoolmaster, St. Nicholas primary school, Barbados, Aug., 1855; of Anglican school, Dominica, April, 1863; clerk and warehouse keeper, Barbados, Oct., 1863.

MAXWELL, Sir William Edward, K.C.M.G. (C.M.G., 1885).—Educated at Repton; layed in supreme court, Penang and Singapore, 1865 to 1869; qualified as an advocate of the local bar in 1867; police magistrate and commissioner court of requests, Penang, 1869; ditto, Malacca, Feb., 1870; ditto,apore, Aug., 1871, ditto Prov.
